title: [integ] workflow launcher always resolves stdio, never attaches to a running HTTP fleet singleton by default -- 'apra-fleet workflow auto-sprint' cannot run
description:
Feature: N/A (dist/cli/workflow.js launcher, core auto-sprint entry point exercised by integ-test-playbook.md Test scenario step 4)
Expected: per docs/adr-workflow-server-resolution.md and the launcher's own --help text, resolution order with no override set is 'probe HTTP singleton first, self-spawn stdio only as fallback'. With a healthy 'apra-fleet start' HTTP singleton already running (verified via 'node dist/index.js status' -> State: running, Port: 18700), 'node dist/index.js workflow auto-sprint --issue <id> --members <m> --branch <b> --base <base> --max-cycles 1 --dispatch-timeout-s 900' should attach to that singleton over HTTP and run the sprint.
Actual: it fails immediately with 'Error: No reachable apra-fleet HTTP singleton was found. cli.mjs no longer self-spawns a per-invocation stdio MCP server (Plan Part 2.1) -- resolution said: "APRA_FLEET_SERVER_CMD/APRA_FLEET_SERVER_BIN set (explicit stdio request)"'. Root cause: dist/cli/workflow.js's runWorkflow() pre-populates a COPY of the env (resolutionEnv.APRA_FLEET_SERVER_BIN = deps.serverBin) before calling deps.resolveConnection(resolutionEnv), solely so its OWN best-effort connection-probe call has a fallback value. But packages/apra-fleet-client/src/client/server-resolution.mjs's resolveFleetServerConnection() treats ANY truthy APRA_FLEET_SERVER_BIN as an 'explicit stdio request' (step 1 of its resolution order) and skips the HTTP probe (step 2) entirely -- so mode is ALWAYS 'stdio' regardless of whether a healthy HTTP singleton exists. applyEnvDefaults() then also writes that same APRA_FLEET_SERVER_BIN into the real env passed to the workflow passthrough process, so packages/apra-fleet-se/bin/cli.mjs's own resolveFleetServerConnection() call inherits the same poisoned env and also resolves 'stdio' -- but cli.mjs requires mode==='http' outright (Plan Part 2.1: no per-sprint stdio self-spawn), so it errors and exits 1. Net effect: 'apra-fleet workflow auto-sprint' can NEVER attach to a running HTTP singleton by default in dev/npm-installed mode (non-SEA) -- the exact single-machine CI-runner topology this playbook's smoke test and docs/tools-infrastructure.md's model both assume. Workaround found: explicitly exporting APRA_FLEET_TRANSPORT=http before the workflow command forces resolveFleetServerConnection() past the poisoned step-1 check straight to the step-2 HTTP probe, which then succeeds -- confirming the HTTP singleton itself is healthy and this is purely a launcher env-defaulting bug, not a server problem.
Test: integ-test-runner smoke test (playbook part 2), Test scenario step 4, 2026-07-21/22 cycle. Reproduced twice (once with a bare positional issue-id form that is itself also stale against the current CLI's --issue/--members/--branch/--base flag contract, and again with the corrected flag form from docs/authoring-workflows.md).
Repro: from a fresh 'node dist/index.js install && node dist/index.js start' with the server healthy on its default/scratch port, run (no APRA_FLEET_TRANSPORT/APRA_FLEET_SERVER_BIN/APRA_FLEET_SERVER_CMD set): 'node dist/index.js workflow auto-sprint --issue <id> --members <m> --branch <b> --base <base> --max-cycles 1' -- observe the stdio-request/no-HTTP-singleton error even though 'node dist/index.js status' shows the server running.
Fix direction: dist/cli/workflow.js's launcher-only best-effort resolveConnection probe (used solely to decide whether to default APRA_FLEET_SERVER_BIN for the CHILD workflow process) must not use a value that itself changes the outcome of the very resolution it is probing -- e.g. probe with the caller's real env unmodified first, and only fall back to defaulting APRA_FLEET_SERVER_BIN for the child process if that unmodified probe actually resolves to non-http.

title: Re-verify undici dev-mode bundling fix (apra-fleet-eft.19) end-to-end
description: Fix commits 430a51f2 (bundle undici into dev-mode workflowRuntime manifest in src/cli/install.ts buildDevManifest) and regression test c4cbc788 (tests/install-workflows.test.ts) landed, but apra-fleet-eft.19 (P0) is still IN_PROGRESS and was never confirmed against a real dev-mode sandbox because the smoke test never got past install. Stand up a fresh dev-mode install and confirm workflow auto-sprint starts without ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND for 'undici', then close the bead.
acceptance_criteria: A fresh dev-mode install (node dist/index.js install) is stood up from current main in a clean sandbox HOME; workflow auto-sprint is launched and reaches at least the Plan phase WITHOUT throwing ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND for "undici" (or any transitive undici import). Evidence captured: the dev-mode workflowRuntime manifest lists undici + undici-types, and the run log shows no module-resolution failure. If the run is clean, apra-fleet-eft.19 is confirmed fixed (planner does not close beads; report the verified result so the orchestrator/exit-check can close eft.19 and eft.22). If it still fails, file the concrete resolution error observed rather than closing.
close_reason: Verified end-to-end in a fresh dev-mode sandbox (clean HOME, node dist/index.js install + start): .apra-fleet/node_modules/@apralabs/apra-fleet-client's workflowRuntime bundle includes undici (undici-types correctly excluded, types-only). Ran 'node dist/index.js workflow auto-sprint' against a toy epic/task in a scratch git+bd repo; the run progressed through transport init, MCP session creation, list_members, bd show precondition, git/dolt sync, and reached '=== Group: Sprint Cycle 1 === --- Phase: Plan C1 R1 ---' (Plan phase) with the planner agent actually dispatched -- at no point did any ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND for undici (or any transitive undici import) occur. Final failure at Plan dispatch was 'Authentication failed on toy-doer' (no real LLM login configured in the throwaway sandbox member) -- an expected sandbox limitation, unrelated to undici/module resolution. apra-fleet-eft.19 is confirmed fixed; recommend orchestrator/exit-check close it. Separately found and filed apra-fleet-eft.23 (BUG): first-run onboarding banner corrupts JSON tool responses on the literal first MCP call after a fresh install (unrelated to undici, discovered incidentally during this verification).

title: [test] dev-mode workflowRuntime manifest bundles undici so the client transport resolves
description:
Verify the apra-fleet-eft.19 fix end-to-end: the dev-mode install manifest ships undici so apra-fleet-client's transport.mjs can import it.

Test placement: extend tests/install-workflows.test.ts (the existing 'runInstall workflow-runtime extraction' suite already asserts on workflowRuntime manifest contents).

What to test / how to assert pass-fail (vitest, npm test):
- buildDevManifest()/runInstall workflowRuntime section includes an entry for the undici package tree (e.g. a key path under node_modules/undici, plus undici-types if applicable) -- fails before the fix, passes after.
- Assert the bundled apra-fleet-client transport dependency is satisfiable: after extraction, resolving 'undici' from the installed apra-fleet-client location succeeds (no ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND). A focused unit assertion on the manifest key set is acceptable if a full install+resolve harness is too heavy.

Acceptance criteria:
- New/updated assertion in tests/install-workflows.test.ts fails against the pre-fix buildDevManifest and passes against the fixed one.
- npm test passes with the new assertion; the assertion names undici explicitly so a future regression (drop of undici from the bundle) is caught.
close_reason: Added regression test in tests/install-workflows.test.ts verifying buildDevManifest() bundles undici in the workflowRuntime manifest; confirmed test fails pre-fix and passes post-fix; full test suite green (2335 passed).

title: [impl] Bundle undici (and undici-types) into the dev-mode workflowRuntime manifest
description:
Fix root cause of apra-fleet-eft.19: buildDevManifest() in src/cli/install.ts builds the workflowRuntime bundle (packages/apra-fleet-workflow, apra-fleet-client, ajv, fast-deep-equal, fast-uri, json-schema-traverse, require-from-string) but never collects the 'undici' package tree, even though packages/apra-fleet-client/src/client/transport.mjs imports 'undici' as a direct dependency (declared in apra-fleet-client/package.json). Every dev-mode 'node dist/index.js install' therefore ships apra-fleet-client with a missing dependency, and any workflow command touching the MCP client transport (including 'workflow auto-sprint') crashes with ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND: Cannot find package 'undici'.

Files to change: src/cli/install.ts (buildDevManifest workflowRuntime map, ~line 265-273).

Behaviour: add collectPackageTree() calls for undici (root node_modules/undici) and its own runtime dependency undici-types (node_modules/undici-types) to the workflowRuntime bundle, matching the existing pattern for fast-deep-equal etc. Verify no other transitive undici runtime deps are missing by inspecting node_modules/undici/package.json dependencies.

Acceptance criteria:
- src/cli/install.ts buildDevManifest() collects the undici package tree (and undici-types if undici declares it) into workflowRuntime.
- Repro from the bug is resolved: with HOME set to a fresh sandbox, 'node dist/index.js install && node dist/index.js start && node dist/index.js workflow auto-sprint --help' no longer throws ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND for 'undici' (transport module resolves undici from the installed tree).
- npm run build passes; no other manifest section changed.
close_reason: Fixed: bundled undici into dev-mode workflowRuntime manifest; verified repro resolves and negative-tested that removing the bundled package reproduces the original ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND crash.

title: [integ] dev-mode install omits undici dependency, crashing workflow auto-sprint
description:
Feature: smoke test (integ-test-playbook.md Test scenario step 3), blocks apra-fleet-eft.10/.11/.12 smoke verification
Expected: 'node dist/index.js workflow auto-sprint <goal> --max-cycles 1 --skip-dolt-push' runs the sprint workflow against the running fleet server.
Actual: the command attaches to the HTTP server singleton fine, then immediately throws:
  Error [ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND]: Cannot find package 'undici' imported from <HOME>/.apra-fleet/node_modules/@apralabs/apra-fleet-client/src/client/transport.mjs
  (repro'd against a fresh dev-mode sandbox install at ~/temp/.apra-fleet-tests)
Test: integ-test-playbook.md part 2 smoke test, Test scenario step 3 (workflow auto-sprint). node dist/index.js workflow auto-sprint --help reproduces the same crash, so no sprint arguments are even required.
Repro:
  1. export HOME=~/temp/.apra-fleet-tests; export APRA_FLEET_PORT=18700
  2. node dist/index.js install && node dist/index.js start
  3. node dist/index.js workflow auto-sprint --help
  4. Observe ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND for 'undici'
Root cause: packages/apra-fleet-client/package.json declares 'undici': '^8.7.0' as a direct dependency (used by src/client/transport.mjs, see commit 78664304 'fix(client): disable undici idle timeouts on all streamable-HTTP fetches'), but src/cli/install.ts buildDevManifest()'s workflowRuntime bundle only collects apra-fleet-workflow, apra-fleet-client, ajv, fast-deep-equal, fast-uri, json-schema-traverse, and require-from-string package trees -- it never bundles undici (or undici-types), even though undici is present and hoisted at the repo root's node_modules. Every dev-mode install (node dist/index.js install) therefore ships an apra-fleet-client with a missing dependency, and any workflow command that touches the MCP client transport (including 'workflow auto-sprint', the sprint entry point) crashes on first use.
Impact: blocks integ-test-playbook.md part 2 (smoke test) Test scenario steps 3-4 entirely -- auto-sprint cannot run, so the canary-close/branch-commit assertion can never be exercised. Also blocks apra-fleet-eft.10/.11/.12 to the extent their own tests depend on workflow auto-sprint running in a dev-mode sandbox.
notes: STATUS AUDIT (2026-07-21): fix 430a51f (bundle undici into dev-mode workflowRuntime manifest) + regression test c4cbc78 both on the sprint branch. No work remains; reset in_progress->open pending integ verification.
close_reason: integ-test-runner (2026-07-21): no longer reproduces. Fresh dev-mode sandbox install (~/temp/.apra-fleet-tests) + 'node dist/index.js workflow auto-sprint --issue gh-toy-txc ...' this cycle: log shows '[workflow] fleet server: attached to HTTP singleton at http://127.0.0.1:18700/mcp' with zero ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND for undici -- the client transport resolved cleanly. Sprint later failed for an unrelated reason (git fetch against neutralized origin, filed separately as apra-fleet-eft.47).

title: auto-sprint: ROOT CAUSE for auto-sprint-5/-8 -- Ensure Sprint Branch always force-resets to origin/base, never fetches or adopts origin/<branch>
description:
CONFIRMED ROOT CAUSE, via direct code read (packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/runner.js:1483-1519, phase 'Ensure Sprint Branch', runs once per launch in the 'Sprint Setup' group, on every member in branchEnsureMembers = union of orchestrator/doer/reviewer pools):

  await command(`git fetch origin ${validated.baseBranch} --quiet`, ...);
  await command(`git checkout -B ${validated.branch} origin/${validated.baseBranch}`, ...);

Two consequences, both already independently observed and filed as separate issues before this code was read:

1. It NEVER fetches `origin/<branch>` (the actual sprint branch) at all -- only `origin/<baseBranch>`. So even if the sprint branch has real, pushed commits on origin (e.g. bf203c3, pushed to origin/auto-sprint/eft-service this session), a member's local git has literally no way to learn about them through this phase.
2. `git checkout -B <branch> <start-point>` unconditionally force-recreates the local branch AT <start-point>, discarding whatever commits were previously on that branch name (old tip commits survive via reflog/dangling objects -- not deleted, just orphaned from the ref -- matching auto-sprint-5's finding exactly). Because the start-point (`origin/<baseBranch>`) is a remote-tracking ref, git's default checkout behavior auto-sets the new branch's upstream to it -- exactly reproducing auto-sprint-8's finding that fleet-rev's local 'auto-sprint/eft-service' branch tracks origin/feat/fleet-reorg instead of origin/auto-sprint/eft-service.

This DIRECTLY CONTRADICTS docs/README.md's documented claim: "The sprint branch (--branch) is created from --base if it does not already exist; if it already exists (e.g. resuming on the current working branch), it is reused as-is." The actual code has NO existence check of any kind -- it force-resets to base's tip on every single launch, unconditionally, on every member. "Reused as-is" is not what happens.

CRITICAL IMPLICATION FOR RECOVERY: simply fixing a member's branch by hand (fetch + reset to origin/<branch>) and relaunching will NOT hold -- the very next `node bin/cli.mjs` invocation will immediately re-run this exact phase and force-reset the branch back to origin/<baseBranch> again, discarding the fix (and any of this session's recovered/pushed work: bf203c3 and everything after it) a second time. A durable fix needs one of:
  (a) Patch this phase to check whether origin/<branch> exists and, if so, fetch+adopt IT (not baseBranch) as the checkout start-point, only falling back to baseBranch when the branch is genuinely new -- this is what the README already claims happens and should actually be implemented.
  (b) In the meantime, merge the sprint branch's recovered work into the actual base branch (feat/fleet-reorg) so future 'Ensure Sprint Branch' resets don't lose it (since they'd then start from a base that already contains it).
  (c) Or, as a manual per-launch workaround only, re-push/fast-forward origin/<baseBranch> itself to include the sprint work before each relaunch -- fragile, not recommended as a standing practice.

STATUS: not yet fixed. auto-sprint-5 and auto-sprint-8 should both be treated as INSTANCES of this one root cause, not independent bugs -- linking here rather than duplicating the fix work on each.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 7c451f3 (pushed to origin/auto-sprint/eft-service): Ensure Sprint Branch now fetches and adopts origin/<branch> when it exists, falling back to origin/<baseBranch> only for a genuinely new branch. All 343 apra-fleet-se tests pass; golden transcripts regenerated and reviewed. This also self-heals auto-sprint-8's wrong-upstream-tracking symptom on the very next launch, since checkout -B from origin/<branch> naturally sets correct tracking.

comments.author: Bot
comments.text: Follow-up (found via independent fable-model review of this fix, then verified against the code before acting): the failSoft fetch of origin/<branch> collapsed 'branch doesn't exist yet' and 'fetch failed for any other reason' into the same destructive reset-to-base fallback. Fixed in apra-fleet-reorg commit 4887b29 (auto-sprint/eft-service) -- now only falls back when git's error text confirms 'fatal: couldn't find remote ref'; any other failure throws loudly. Added test coverage for both paths (previously untested). Full suite 350/350 passing.

title: auto-sprint: TWO consecutive sprint runs died silently (~25-47 min in) while a doer sub-session was still actively working -- points to external kill of orchestrator process, not an app bug
description:
OBSERVED: Both auto-sprint runs for apra-fleet-eft died with the identical signature:
  - run1 (task b0wm0tejb): launched 2026-07-17 23:26:53, orchestrator log's last write 23:40:08 (~13 min). BUT the actively-dispatched doer sub-session (c61ec80b, streak [eytc7]) kept working and hit max_turns_reached (51 turns) at 23:44:01 -- 4 min AFTER the orchestrator log went silent. A retry sub-session (31e46e4f) continued working until at least 23:50:24. Real death (dashboard down, no node.exe alive) confirmed by ~00:02.
  - run2 (task baspge4p1): launched 2026-07-18 02:19:50, orchestrator log's last write 03:03:08 (~43 min), right after dispatching Streak [apra-fleet-eft.5, apra-fleet-eft.5.1]. BUT the actively-dispatched doer sub-session (d05aa1aa, streak [ks34t], doing real eft.5 ledger work -- git status showing src/supervisor/ledger.mjs, test/supervisor-ledger.test.mjs as legitimate in-progress output) kept working until at least 07:07:18 UTC (~03:07:18 local) -- 4 min after ITS orchestrator log also went silent. This task-notification arrived with status "killed".

PATTERN: in both runs, the same ~4-minute gap between (a) the orchestrator's own log going silent and (b) the actively-dispatched CHILD doer session's last recorded activity. Neither run shows a crash signature (no stack trace, no uncaught exception, no "Error:" line, no Windows APPCRASH event for node.exe in either window -- checked via Get-WinEvent). The doer sessions were not stuck/looping either: run1's hit a legitimate max_turns cap doing real multi-file refactor work; run2's was mid-`git status` on real, coherent feature work.

ANALYSIS:
1. This is NOT the same root cause as apra-fleet-eft.13 (EADDRINUSE/orphaned-port cascade) -- neither log shows a port conflict or Transport-closed cascade as the terminal event. run2 DID show 3x "Authentication failed on fleet-reorg" + one 930s dispatch timeout mid-run (matches this operator's known local-member-OAuth-expiry failure mode), but the sprint visibly recovered from that and successfully completed 3 more streaks (eft.3, eft.4) afterward -- so auth expiry is a real, separate, already-known issue but not what killed either run.
2. Both orchestrator processes were launched via Bash `run_in_background` (harness-tracked backgrounding, chosen specifically as the non-evasive alternative to the README's raw `&`+`disown` recipe -- see auto-sprint-1). The consistent "child briefly outlives orchestrator by ~4 min, both eventually killed with no crash signature" pattern across two independent runs, at two very different elapsed-time points (13 min vs 43 min -- ruling out a fixed short timeout), is more consistent with an EXTERNAL termination of the orchestrator's process (and, shortly after, its live children) than with any bug inside runner.js or cli.mjs.
3. HYPOTHESIS (not yet confirmed): the harness's `run_in_background` may scope the spawned node.exe process to a Windows Job Object tied to the underlying shell/pty session, and something in that shell's lifecycle (recycling, an idle/keepalive boundary, or cleanup tied to this orchestrating conversation's own tool-call cadence) may be tearing down that Job Object periodically -- which would kill the orchestrator immediately but leave its already-spawned grandchild CLI doer sessions (spawned as more independent processes, possibly outside the same Job Object) to finish their current turn before also disappearing. This would mean harness-tracked backgrounding does NOT reliably protect a 30-60+ minute sprint from the exact "tool-call process group killed" failure mode the suspect README warned about (auto-sprint-1) -- ironically for a different reason than the README's own (false) justification.
4. NOT YET RULED OUT: OS-level resource pressure (memory climbed from ~11.2GB/16GB at run1 to ~11.9GB/16GB at run2 check, both around 70% system memory) silently killing the process without a WER crash report is possible but less likely to produce the exact same ~4-minute child-outlives-parent gap twice.

RECOMMENDATION: if a third run is attempted, capture `wmic process where name='node.exe' get ProcessId,CreationDate,ParentProcessId` (or Get-CimInstance Win32_Process) polled every few minutes throughout the run, so a future death can be correlated against exact PID/parent-PID lifecycle instead of reconstructed after the fact from session-transcript timestamps. Also worth asking the harness/environment owner directly whether background Bash tasks in this session type have an enforced maximum lifetime.

title: [bug][important] Parallel doer streak dispatch to a single member is unsafe -- causes hours-long hangs + response corruption; needs worktree-per-streak redesign
description:
Auto-sprint's Develop phase can assign multiple doer streaks to run "in
parallel" within one round (e.g. `Develop C1 R1` on 2026-07-15 assigned
streaks `[["apra-fleet-fih.2"],["apra-fleet-fih.3"]]`). Today this caused a
live sprint (`apra-fleet-fih`) to stall for over an hour with zero forward
progress: both streaks were dispatched concurrently to the SAME fleet
member (`fleet-reorg`), which is a single working-directory/checkout, not
an isolated session per dispatch.

## Constraint (confirmed by user, authoritative)

A member = one folder/checkout. It is NOT possible to safely run two
concurrent `execute_prompt` dispatches against the same member -- there is
no isolation between them (shared git checkout, shared session/process).

The only correct way to achieve genuine parallel doer work is: register
multiple members, each pointed at its own git worktree, dispatch one
streak per member/worktree, and after all streaks complete, merge the
worktree branches back with proper branch hygiene (clean up worktrees,
resolve/avoid conflicts, delete merged branches). This is real
infrastructure work (worktree lifecycle + member registration automation +
post-merge cleanup), not a one-line fix.

## What happened without this (observed failure mode)

Two concurrent dispatches against one shared-folder member produced:
- Repeated 900s inactivity timeouts on both streaks (contention for the
  same folder/session -- likely git index lock or transport-level
  single-invocation assumption).
- One dispatch's response came back with a corrupted multi-byte UTF-8
  sequence (`📋` emoji prefix decoded as U+FFFD replacement character),
  breaking JSON schema-repair parsing. Plausibly caused by output
  interleaving between the two concurrent invocations against a channel
  the transport assumes is single-occupancy per member.
- One streak (`fih.2`) left a real, syntactically-valid but unverified
  partial edit in the working tree when its dispatch died mid-response --
  recovered and committed as a WIP checkpoint
  (`34f2752` on `test/apra-fleet-se-speedup`) rather than lost.

## Immediate stopgap (do before the real fix, cheap and safe)

Until worktree-per-streak isolation exists, `runner.js`'s Develop-phase
streak dispatch must not fan out more than one streak concurrently against
a single member. Either serialize streak dispatch (one at a time, same
member) or hard-fail pre-sprint validation if the streak-assignment output
would produce >1 streak with only one member available/registered for the
scope. Silent concurrent dispatch against one folder must not be possible
-- it should fail loud, not hang for an hour.

## Real fix (needs its own planning pass, do not attempt inline)

1. Member-per-worktree provisioning: given a scope with N ready parallel
   streaks, auto-create N git worktrees (or reuse pre-registered
   worktree-backed members) before Develop phase dispatch.
2. Route each streak's doer dispatch to its own worktree-backed member.
3. Post-round merge/branch-hygiene step: after all streaks in a round
   complete (success or failure), merge each worktree's branch back into
   the sprint branch, handle conflicts (fail loud + surface to reviewer,
   do not auto-resolve), then remove the now-merged worktrees so they don't
   accumulate across rounds/sprints.
4. Decide member registration lifecycle: are worktree-backed members
   ephemeral (registered/deregistered per sprint) or a fixed pool reused
   across sprints? Affects `~/.apra-fleet/data/registry.json` growth and
   whether stale worktree directories need periodic GC.
5. Update `doer.md`/`planner.md` prose contracts if the scope/member
   assignment model changes as part of this (may intersect with the
   graph-consistency remediation plan's Phase B/C work already tracked
   separately).

Acceptance criteria for this backlog item once planned: a sprint that
assigns 2+ parallel doer streaks completes them in genuinely isolated
worktrees, merges cleanly with no manual intervention required in the
common case, and any merge conflict is surfaced as a loud, actionable
failure rather than silent data loss or an hour-long hang.

comments.author: Azure Pipeline
comments.text:
CORRECTION after root-cause investigation (2026-07-15, two independent read-only
agents + manual verification):

The originally-suspected mechanism ("both streaks dispatched concurrently to
the same member with zero serialization, causing a fast-fail collision") is
only half right and needs a precise correction:

1. `packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/runner.js` (the code path actually
   executed by `node packages/apra-fleet-se/bin/cli.mjs`, confirmed via
   `bin/cli.mjs:18`'s direct import from `../auto-sprint/runner.js`) ALREADY
   has same-member serialization: a `memberLocks` Map added in commit
   `51df121` (2026-07-13), verified present and structurally sound at
   `runner.js:1931-2038` (acquire/release wrapped in try/finally, covers the
   throw path). So concurrent streaks to one member queue rather than
   collide-and-fast-fail in this code path.
2. A SEPARATE, real gap exists in `vendor/apra-pm/.claude/workflows/auto-sprint.js`
   (the file installed/used by the `auto-sprint` Claude Code SKILL, distinct
   from the dev-CLI path above) -- confirmed zero matches for `memberLocks`,
   `member_name`, or `doerMember` in that file's parallel-doer block
   (~lines 2050-2166). If that installed-skill path is ever used for a
   single-member sprint, it WILL hit the original fast-fail collision bug
   that `51df121` already fixed in `runner.js` but never backported here.
   Tracked as follow-up: port `runner.js`'s `memberLocks` pattern into
   `vendor/apra-pm/.claude/workflows/auto-sprint.js`'s parallel-doer
   dispatch, or ensure the two implementations converge into one.
3. Given (1), the actual apra-fleet-fih hang's repeated 900s timeouts are
   NOT explained by a fast-fail collision (memberLocks would have queued
   them, and a collision fails near-instantly, not after 15 minutes each).
   The recurring timeouts remain a separate, not-fully-explained reliability
   issue -- possibly a genuine CLI/dispatch hang, possibly related to
   apra-fleet-grq's stdout decode bug (a hang while reading/processing a
   response corrupted mid-stream is plausible but unconfirmed).

This bead's core architectural point stands regardless: even with
memberLocks preventing collisions, "parallel" streaks against one member are
serialized in practice (defeats the purpose of parallel dispatch) -- the
real fix is still worktree-per-streak + member-per-worktree, as described
above. Filed apra-fleet-grq separately for the confirmed stdout/stderr
UTF-8 chunk-decode corruption bug (independent of this bead).

title: [bug] FleetWorkflow.command() does not strip execute_command's 'Exit code: N\n' prefix, breaking every bd --json call dispatched to a real fleet member
description:
Discovered live: the very first real (non-mocked) auto-sprint run against apra-fleet-7pm crashed immediately in the "Ensure Sprint Branch" phase.

Root cause: src/tools/execute-command.ts:275-276 ALWAYS formats the execute_command MCP tool's response as `Exit code: ${code}\n${output}` -- this is by design, confirmed independently (we saw this exact format testing execute_command against fleet-rev in this same session). But packages/apra-fleet-workflow/src/workflow/index.mjs's command() method (around line 1049/1069) reads `result.content[0].text` and returns it verbatim as `outText`, with no stripping of the "Exit code: N\n" prefix. Every downstream caller in packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/runner.js that expects raw command stdout (especially the ~15 `bd list/show ... --json` call sites feeding parseBdJson()) receives this prefix and fails to JSON.parse.

Actual crash observed:
```
[WorkflowEngine] Execution Failed: Error: [bd JSON Parse Error] Failed to parse JSON output from 'bd list --parent apra-fleet-7pm --ready --json': Unexpected token 'E', "Exit code:"... is not valid JSON. Raw output snippet: "Exit code: 0\n[\n  {\n    \"id\": \"apra-fleet-7pm.1\", ...
```

This means the ENTIRE auto-sprint engine has apparently never been exercised against the real apra-fleet execute_command tool for any JSON-parsing bd call -- only against a mock fleet API (buildMockFleetApi(), used throughout the dv5 test suite and others) that returns raw stdout without this prefix, i.e. does not match production execute_command behavior. Any sprint dispatched at a real fleet member is broken today, not just this one.

Fix location: command() in packages/apra-fleet-workflow/src/workflow/index.mjs should strip a leading `Exit code: N\n` (or more robustly, parse out the code and pass through everything after the first newline) before returning outText -- OR runner.js's parseBdJson()/every raw-output consumer should do so. The former is more correct: it's a general command()-contract mismatch with execute_command's real response shape, not bd-specific, so any other command() caller expecting raw stdout (not just bd --json calls) is equally affected.

Also: buildMockFleetApi() (test/*.mjs across apra-fleet-se and apra-fleet-workflow test suites) should be updated to replicate the real 'Exit code: N\n' prefix, so this class of bug is caught by the existing test suite instead of only surfacing on a real fleet dispatch.

Discovered during: first real single-member auto-sprint run against apra-fleet-7pm (SEA-binary workflow subsystem epic), 2026-07-13.
acceptance_criteria: command() in packages/apra-fleet-workflow/src/workflow/index.mjs strips the 'Exit code: N\n' prefix from execute_command's real response format before returning outText (or equivalently extracts the exit code and passes through only the text after it); a real (non-mocked) 'bd list ... --json' dispatched via command() to an actual fleet member parses successfully; buildMockFleetApi() test helpers are updated to replicate the real prefix so this regresses loudly in CI instead of only on a live run; re-running auto-sprint against apra-fleet-7pm gets past the Ensure Sprint Branch phase.
close_reason: Fixed in commit 0b878fc: execute_command now returns structuredContent{exitCode,stdout,stderr} alongside the display text; FleetWorkflow.command() prefers it. Verified live end-to-end against the freshly rebuilt/restarted server (v0.3.5_0b878f) -- bd list --json now returns clean parseable stdout via structuredContent, no Exit-code-prefix contamination. Full test suites green (root 2215/2215, apra-fleet-se 222/222, apra-fleet-workflow 112/112). CI pending on push.

title: [impl] Provision toy-doer CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN via member encryptedEnvVars as the primary smoke-test path
description:

Direction (ORCHESTRATOR STEER, post-Integ-C4): STOP iterating on the credential-FILE shape. On macOS runners the real credential lives in the Keychain, so REAL_HOME/.claude/.credentials.json never exists and the bare-token synthesized file is rejected by the real CLI; the SAME token exported into the dispatch ENVIRONMENT authenticates fine. Remove the need for auth --oauth in the smoke test. Ensure the env var reaches getCleanEnv()'s child so LocalStrategy's clean-env dispatch sees it.

Done / acceptance criteria:
- Playbook step 3 provisions toy-doer's CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN via encryptedEnvVars (secret-store reference, no plaintext) as the primary, cross-platform path; file path retained only as an optional bonus.

title: [impl] Short-circuit real-bd post-terminal-error teardown in the shared mock-sprint scenario harness
description:
Files: the shared mock-sprint scenario harness in packages/apra-fleet-se (runDevelopLoopScenario / withScenarioMarkers and the scenario teardown it invokes), plus the dolt/git-sync bracket code it calls on teardown.

Problem: under APRA_FLEET_BD_MOCK=off the engine throws the terminal AGENT_DISPATCH_FAILED error promptly and correctly, but the harness's post-error scenario teardown (real dolt/git sync brackets, same family as eft.17's real-dolt-latency findings) runs unconditionally and hangs far past the test's own documented fast-abort contract, so the node:test harness kills the file at its 120s/180s hard limit before assertions run. This is the shared root cause behind both eft.54 (auth-failure-no-retry) and eft.50 (attempt1-clean-fail/attempt2-dead-session).

Behaviour: first instrument/trace what the scenario teardown does after a thrown terminal dispatch error under real bd, then make the terminal-error path skip the unnecessary real-bd sync teardown work (or apply the eft.17.1-style short-circuit/caching) so teardown completes quickly on a terminal error. The happy-path teardown (non-terminal) must be unchanged.

Done / acceptance criteria:
- Under APRA_FLEET_BD_MOCK=off, after a terminal dispatch error the scenario teardown returns within the test's documented fast-abort bound (no multi-minute hang), verified by the two regression tests (eft.54 test child and eft.50 test child) completing well under their node:test file timeouts.
- Non-terminal (happy-path) scenario teardown behaviour is unchanged; existing mock-sprint suites stay green.
- ASCII only.
acceptance_criteria: Terminal-error scenario teardown under real bd completes within the test fast-abort bound; happy-path teardown unchanged; existing mock-sprint suites stay green.
close_reason: Closed

title: [impl] Make the bare-token OAuth credential file CLI-acceptable (or retire the bare-token fallback for a CLI-honored path)
description:
Regression follow-up on bug eft.48 (all prior children eft.48.1-48.5 closed; bug STILL REPRODUCES per verification-closure pass #2, 2026-07-21). Fresh evidence (eft.48 notes): with ONLY $HOME/.claude/.credentials.json populated as accessToken + synthetic expiresAt -- the exact shape eft.48.3's bare-token branch writes and the shape a CI runner without a real credentials file falls back to -- the installed claude CLI (2.1.212) still says 'Not logged in - Please run /login', even though check-toy-doer-credentials.mjs (hardened by eft.48.4) reports OK. Control: exporting the SAME token as an ambient CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN env var into the identical clean-env probe authenticates fine -- so the token is valid; the credentials-FILE bare-token branch specifically is what the CLI rejects. This is the residual mechanism the closed eft.48.3 'full object passthrough' branch did NOT cover.

Scope: this is NOT a duplicate of eft.48.3 (that wrote the full-object branch when a real credentials object is available). This task targets the bare-token synthetic-session branch that the playbook still endorses for credential-less CI runners.

Fix direction (pick the one the real CLI actually honors; determine empirically via clean-env repro): EITHER (a) write the additional credential field(s) the CLI requires (e.g. refreshToken/scopes/subscriptionType) so a bare-token-derived .credentials.json is accepted by the clean-env dispatch path, OR (b) retire the bare-token file fallback in 'apra-fleet auth --oauth' and have LocalStrategy clean-env dispatch export the token via CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN into the child env (the path proven to authenticate) instead of relying on the file, updating integ-test-playbook.md ## Setup accordingly.

Files to change (<=3): the 'auth --oauth' credential-file writer and/or LocalStrategy clean-env env construction; integ-test-playbook.md ## Setup if the endorsed fallback path changes. ASCII only.

Done / acceptance criteria:
- A toy-doer member provisioned WITHOUT a real full credentials object (bare-token-only, the credential-less CI-runner path) authenticates through LocalStrategy's clean-env dispatch against the real installed claude CLI -- no 'Not logged in'/'Authentication failed'.
- The chosen mechanism is proven by a direct clean-env repro (env -i HOME=$SANDBOX ... claude -p ...) reaching past auth (e.g. to a model-not-found error), matching the passing control case in eft.48's notes.
- If path (b) is taken, integ-test-playbook.md ## Setup documents the CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN env-var provisioning as the endorsed credential-less path and no longer relies on the rejected bare-token file.
- No regression to eft.48.3's full-object branch when a real credentials object IS available.
- ASCII only.

title: [test] Planner retry that fails attempt 1 cleanly then targets a dead session on attempt 2 fails fast, never hangs
description:
Regression verification for bug eft.50, pinning the fix in eft.50.1. The existing dead-pid/stalled-session regression tests (per eft.28/eft.46 closure) do NOT cover the ordering where retry attempt 1 fails cleanly BEFORE attempt 2 hangs on a dead session; this test adds exactly that ordering.

What to test (framework: node --test/vitest as used by the existing mock-sprint harnesses; reuse packages/apra-fleet-se/test/mock-sprint-planner-dispatch-dead-pid.test.mjs and/or mock-sprint-planner-dispatch-stalled-session.test.mjs patterns):
- Construct a Planner dispatch scenario where attempt 1/5 fails fast with a clean typed error (e.g. an auth/AGENT_DISPATCH_FAILED-class failure, matching eft.48's precondition), THEN attempt 2/5 would reuse a persistent interactive session whose launch-time pid is dead.
- Assert attempt 2 does NOT hang: within a bounded (few-seconds) window it either re-dispatches fresh (stale session evicted) or surfaces a typed AgentDispatchError -- assert on the emitted log line / state update / typed error, and that state.updatedAt keeps advancing (no frozen-state silent gap).
- Assert the watchdog/timeout is not the mechanism that eventually rescues it (i.e. it fails fast well before dispatch_timeout_s), distinguishing this from the pre-fix 6m+ silent hang.

How to assert pass/fail: the test must FAIL against the pre-eft.50.1 code (reproducing the silent hang / no fast fail) and PASS after the fix. Assert on structured signals (typed error reason, log presence, state progress), not wall-clock timing alone.

Done / acceptance criteria:
- New test reproduces the attempt-1-clean-fail-then-attempt-2-dead-session ordering and fails on current main, passes after eft.50.1.
- Asserts fast typed failure or fresh re-dispatch (no indefinite silent hang, no frozen state).
- Runs in the existing apra-fleet-se test suite.
- ASCII only.
close_reason: Added packages/apra-fleet-se/test/mock-sprint-planner-dispatch-attempt1-clean-fail-attempt2-dead-session.test.mjs (commit 8603c4c4), covering the attempt-1-clean-fail-then-attempt-2-dead-session ordering; verified via record+replay bd-mock runs (PASS at ~110-142s, well under the 150s ceiling; asserts fast typed dispatch_failed errors, no [dispatch-watchdog] log line, continuous log advancement, and terminal ABORTED state).

title: [impl] Re-arm dead/stale interactive-session detection on every Planner dispatch retry, not just the first attempt
description:
Decomposition of open bug eft.50 (had no task children). eft.28's dead-session fix (src/tools/execute-prompt.ts pre-dispatch liveness check ~line 426 + in-flight PID poll ~line 252) does NOT cover the specific ordering this bug reproduced live: Planner retry attempt 1/5 fails fast+clean (eft.48 auth error), then retry attempt 2/5 logs '[Dispatch] phase: Plan C1 R1' and hangs silently 6m+ with zero further output, zero state updates, zero child process -- the member's launch-time claude pid (16031) was already dead, and the retry reused/awaited that dead session's broken channel instead of detecting death and failing fast or re-dispatching fresh.

Root cause to fix: the dead-session liveness/eviction guard is effectively armed for the first dispatch's session but not re-evaluated for a subsequent retry's fresh attempt that reuses the same persistent interactive session record. The runner's watchdog (packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/runner.js withDispatchWatchdog, ~line 2832) IS re-created per retry, but at --dispatch-timeout-s 900 it only bounds the hang at ~15.5min -- it does not make attempt 2 fail FAST on an already-dead session the way eft.28 intended.

Files to change (<=3): primarily src/tools/execute-prompt.ts (ensure the pre-dispatch dead-PID liveness check + stale-session eviction fires on EVERY dispatch/retry that would reuse an existing interactive session, including one entered after a prior attempt in the same round already failed); touch src/utils/pid-helpers.ts / http-transport session bookkeeping only if the launch-time pid is not being carried/re-checked across retries. ASCII only.

Done / acceptance criteria:
- When a Planner dispatch retry attempt would reuse a persistent interactive session whose launch-time claude pid is dead, execute_prompt EVICTS the stale session and re-dispatches fresh (or fails with a typed AgentDispatchError) BEFORE awaiting the dead channel -- on retry attempt 2+ exactly as on attempt 1.
- The liveness/eviction check is re-evaluated at the start of each dispatch attempt, not cached/short-circuited from a prior attempt in the same round.
- The specific ordering (attempt 1 fails clean, attempt 2 targets a now-dead session) no longer produces a silent hang: it produces either a fresh re-dispatch or a bounded, logged, typed terminal failure -- never zero-output silence past a few seconds.
- No regression to eft.28.1/28.5 behavior for the first-attempt dead-PID and connect-back-session cases.
- ASCII only.
notes: ORCHESTRATOR CI EVIDENCE (2026-07-21, branch CI runs 29850654314 + 29851340563 both red): commit 41cc588 (this bead's re-arm fix) breaks the eft.28.3 regression guard packages/apra-fleet-se/test/mock-sprint-planner-dispatch-stalled-session.test.mjs -- 'Promise resolution is still pending but the event loop has already resolved', i.e. the interactive Planner dispatch against a stalled/dead session no longer aborts within dispatch_timeout_s in the mock scenario. Verified NOT flaky and NOT caused by the orchestrator's 5e5ab49/d9b2c71 commits: fails deterministically at tip d9b2c71 on local Windows after a full rebuild, AND still fails with runner.js/errors.mjs reverted to pre-5e5ab49 state (bisect), AND failed on two different CI OSes (macos then ubuntu). The re-arm change appears to swallow or outlive the stall-abort path that test pins down -- the exact eft.28/eft.50 hang this bead exists to prevent. Must be fixed before this bead closes; the stalled-session test passing is part of its acceptance.
close_reason: Fix (41cc5880) + CI-regression follow-up fix (d87a5ec5, this session, keeps withDispatchWatchdog's timer ref'd) committed and pushed. Verified: mock-sprint-planner-dispatch-stalled-session.test.mjs (the eft.28.3/28.4 test CI reported broken) now PASSES (560.1s, watchdog fires on all 5 retry attempts); mock-sprint-planner-dispatch-dead-pid.test.mjs (eft.28.2) and mock-sprint-planner-dispatch-attempt1-clean-fail-attempt2-dead-session.test.mjs (eft.50.2) still pass -- no regression; dispatch-watchdog.test.mjs (3/3) and session-registry/execute-prompt-interactive vitest units for eft.50.1 (34/34) pass. npm run build is green.

title: [test] toy-doer clean-env dispatch authenticates against the real claude CLI with the full-shape credential file
description:
REGRESSION verification for parent eft.48. Pins the residual mechanism fixed by eft.48.3 (full OAuth credential-object write) and eft.48.4 (guard that proves CLI-acceptance) so 'Authentication failed on toy-doer' cannot recur silently.

What to test:
- getOAuthCredentialPatch / handleOAuth (src/cli/auth.ts): given a full-credential source, the written ~/.claude/.credentials.json claudeAiOauth object contains accessToken AND the additional session fields (expiresAt/refreshToken/scopes/subscriptionType) the installed claude CLI requires; given a pre-existing file, unrelated keys are preserved (deep-merge).
- check-toy-doer-credentials.mjs guard: PASSES on the full-shape file, FAILS on the old accessToken-only shape.
- Clean-env acceptance: assert that a file written by the new path is accepted through the same 'env -i HOME=<sandbox> ... bash -l -c' clean-env path LocalStrategy uses -- either via a real claude-CLI auth probe when available, or a documented harness stub that models the CLI's 'valid session requires additional fields' acceptance rule when the real CLI is not present in CI.

How to assert pass/fail: vitest unit test(s) under the auth.ts test suite (framework: vitest, per repo 'npm test'); assert on the parsed credentials-file JSON shape and on the guard's OK/NOT-PROVISIONED result code. No token/credential value may appear in assertions, fixtures committed to the repo, or test output.

Done / acceptance criteria:
- Test fails against the pre-eft.48.3 accessToken-only write shape and passes against the new full-shape write.
- Test covers the guard (eft.48.4) returning FAIL on accessToken-only and PASS on full-shape.
- Runs under 'npm test' (vitest); no real secret is embedded or printed.
- ASCII only.
close_reason: Closed

title: [impl] Harden check-toy-doer-credentials.mjs to prove the written file is CLI-acceptable, not just non-empty accessToken
description:
REGRESSION follow-up to parent eft.48. The eft.48.2 guard scripts/check-toy-doer-credentials.mjs reported 'OK' while the real Planner dispatch still failed with 'Authentication failed' -- because its read-only probe (lines ~146-214) only asserts a non-empty claudeAiOauth.accessToken string. That false-OK is what let this regression slip past 48.2's verification, so the guard must actually validate the credentials file is sufficient for the installed claude CLI.

Files to change (<=3): scripts/check-toy-doer-credentials.mjs (and src/utils/credential-validation.ts only if a shared shape-check belongs there). Do NOT print token/credential material. ASCII only.

Approach: strengthen the probe so 'OK' means the file is genuinely CLI-acceptable, not merely present. Either (a) assert the credential object carries the full session shape written by eft.48.3 (accessToken + the required additional fields expiresAt/refreshToken/scopes/subscriptionType), or (b) run a real clean-env ('env -i HOME=<fleetHome> ... bash -l -c') lightweight claude auth probe and require it to authenticate. Keep the existing clean-env HOME-resolution semantics that match LocalStrategy's exec path. Reason for change must stay a shape/behavior check, never echoing the secret.

Done / acceptance criteria:
- The probe reports OK only when the credentials file would be accepted by the installed claude CLI (full session shape present, or a real clean-env auth probe passes), and reports NOT-PROVISIONED for a file that has only a bare accessToken.
- Against the OLD accessToken-only file shape (pre-eft.48.3), the guard now FAILS (proving it would have caught this regression).
- Against the full-shape file written by eft.48.3, the guard PASSES.
- No credential/token value is emitted to any transcript or log line.
- ASCII only.
close_reason: Closed

title: [impl] Write the full OAuth credential object in 'apra-fleet auth --oauth' so the installed claude CLI accepts the file
description:
REGRESSION follow-up to parent eft.48 (children 48.1/48.2 closed, but the integ-test-runner 2026-07-21 verification-closure pass proved the defect STILL REPRODUCES against the residual mechanism below -- this is NOT a duplicate of the closed 48.1 playbook-doc fix).

Residual mechanism (from eft.48 notes, direct clean-env repro): src/cli/auth.ts getOAuthCredentialPatch('claude', token) (lines ~113-123) writes ONLY {claudeAiOauth:{accessToken: token}}. The installed real claude CLI (2.1.212) rejects that file with 'Not logged in - Please run /login' because it requires the fuller logged-in-session shape (at least one of expiresAt / refreshToken / scopes / subscriptionType, matching a genuine 'claude /login' credentials.json). So even after a correct provisioning step (48.1), the clean-env ('env -i ... bash -l -c') LocalStrategy dispatch fails 'Authentication failed on toy-doer'.

Files to change (<=3): src/cli/auth.ts (getOAuthCredentialPatch and, if needed, parseTokenArgs/handleOAuth to accept a full-credential source). Do NOT print the token or any credential material to stdout/stderr/logs. ASCII only.

Approach: allow 'apra-fleet auth --oauth' to capture/persist the full OAuth credential object rather than just accessToken. Accept either (a) a JSON credential blob (the full claudeAiOauth object incl. accessToken + expiresAt + refreshToken + scopes + subscriptionType) resolved from the secure store / positional, deep-merged into ~/.claude/.credentials.json, or (b) if only a bare access token is supplied, write the additional fields required for the installed CLI to treat the file as a valid session (documented, minimally-sufficient set). Preserve existing deepMerge-into-existing-file behavior and the secure.<name> resolution path.

Done / acceptance criteria:
- After 'apra-fleet auth --oauth' runs against a full-credential source, ~/.claude/.credentials.json contains claudeAiOauth with accessToken AND the additional session fields (expiresAt/refreshToken/scopes/subscriptionType) the installed claude CLI requires.
- A clean-env ('env -i HOME=<sandbox> ... bash -l -c "claude -p ..."') invocation using the written file authenticates (no 'Not logged in - Please run /login', no 'Authentication failed').
- No credential/token value is ever emitted to any transcript, log line, or error message.
- Existing --api-key path and secure.<name> resolution remain unchanged; deep-merge into a pre-existing credentials file still preserves unrelated keys.
- ASCII only.
close_reason: Closed

title: [integ] Smoke test: auto-sprint Planner dispatch hangs silently again on retry 2/5 after eft.48's auth failure -- eft.28 fix does not hold
description:
Feature: N/A (playbook part 2 -- smoke test, integ-test-playbook.md ## Test scenario steps 3-4)
Expected: per apra-fleet-eft.28's closure (2026-07-21), every Planner dispatch retry attempt either succeeds or fails with a clean, typed, logged error (AgentDispatchError/AGENT_DISPATCH_FAILED) within a bounded time -- no indefinite silent hang, 'the dispatch/retry loop logged continuously ... with zero silent gaps'.
Actual: this cycle's smoke test run ('node dist/index.js workflow auto-sprint --issue gh-toy-4ef --members toy-doer --branch smoke-test-canary --base main --max-cycles 1 --dispatch-timeout-s 900') reproduced eft.48's Authentication-failed error cleanly on retry attempt 1/5 (fast, ~1-2s, fully logged), then printed 'Planner dispatch: waiting 5s before retry attempt 2/5...', ran the pre-dispatch git/dolt sync brackets, logged '[Dispatch] phase: Plan C1 R1 | member: toy-doer | label: none' -- and then went completely silent. GET /state's updatedAt froze at 2026-07-21T16:12:02.443Z while wall clock advanced past 2026-07-21T16:17:58Z (5m56s with zero state updates, zero new log lines, zero child processes under either the workflow CLI process or the fleet server process -- confirmed via ps/pgrep, nothing spawned). Killed manually (kill -9) after ~6m08s total elapsed on retry attempt 2 alone with no error, no timeout firing, no progress. This is the EXACT symptom eft.28 was filed and closed for (member's launch-time claude process -- pid 16031, confirmed dead by the time of the hang -- appears to die, and a later dispatch attempt reuses/waits on that dead session's already-broken channel instead of detecting death and failing fast), recurring here on retry attempt 2 after retry attempt 1's fast, clean auth failure.
Test: integ-test-playbook.md Test scenario steps 3-4, this cycle's real (unmocked) smoke-test run against a freshly-Setup sandbox (all eft.47/eft.18/eft.30 fixes applied, sandbox-sync-remote isolation verified via check-sandbox-sync-remote.mjs and check-toy-doer-credentials.mjs, both passing, before this dispatch).
Repro:
1. Fresh Setup per integ-test-playbook.md (through the credential-provisioning step, apra-fleet-eft.48.1) in a sandbox where the toy-doer credential is provisioned via 'apra-fleet auth --oauth' (still insufficient per apra-fleet-eft.48 -- see that bug).
2. node dist/index.js workflow auto-sprint --issue gh-toy-4ef --members toy-doer --branch smoke-test-canary --base main --max-cycles 1 --dispatch-timeout-s 900
3. Observe retry attempt 1/5 fail fast and cleanly with 'Authentication failed on toy-doer' (matches eft.48).
4. Observe retry attempt 2/5 print '[Dispatch] phase: Plan C1 R1 | member: toy-doer | label: none' and then never produce another log line or state update -- confirm via GET :8080/state (updatedAt frozen) and ps/pgrep (no live child process anywhere) that the workflow process itself is not doing any visible work.
Fix direction: this looks like the same class eft.28.1/28.3/28.5-28.7 targeted (dead-session detection/eviction + liveness timeout for the Planner dispatch channel) but the fix does not cover the specific path where retry attempt 1 already failed cleanly (non-hung) and retry attempt 2 is the one that hangs -- i.e. the liveness/eviction logic may only be wired to the very first dispatch attempt's session, not re-armed for each subsequent retry's fresh attempt. Needs a repro under the mock-sprint-planner-dispatch-dead-pid/stalled-session harnesses that specifically fails attempt 1 first (as a distinct precondition) before attempting to hang attempt 2, since the existing regression tests (per eft.46/eft.28 closure) evidently do not cover that ordering.
notes: integ-test-runner (2026-07-21, this cycle, real-bd pass): recurs and appears WORSE than the previously-recorded 155286ms/~110s-ceiling overshoot -- this cycle's full real-bd pass shows mock-sprint-planner-dispatch-attempt1-clean-fail-attempt2-dead-session.test.mjs (eft.50.2's own regression test) hitting the node:test harness's hard 180000ms timeout itself (Error [ERR_TEST_FAILURE]: test timed out after 180000ms), not just exceeding its internal assertion bound. Log shows the typed AGENT_DISPATCH_FAILED error (dispatch_failed, 'reused interactive session now targets a dead launch-time process -- fast eviction + typed dispatch failure, not a hang') IS thrown correctly and promptly, but the test never reaches its post-error assertions before the hard timeout fires (integ-suite-run.log lines ~811-830, elapsed=249s). Same family/likely same root cause as the new apra-fleet-eft.54 filed this cycle for mock-sprint-planner-auth-failure-no-retry.test.mjs (same shape: correct fast typed error, then silent hang past the test's own timing contract) -- both point at real-bd (APRA_FLEET_BD_MOCK=off) post-error cleanup/teardown in the shared mock-sprint harness, not the dispatch-error logic itself. Left open; see apra-fleet-eft.54 for the sibling failure and shared-root-cause hypothesis.

title: [test] toy-doer smoke-test Planner dispatch authenticates after the documented provisioning step (no AGENT_DISPATCH_FAILED)
description:
Verifies the fix from eft.48.1: with the new credential-provisioning step in integ-test-playbook.md's Setup, the smoke test's toy-doer member authenticates through LocalStrategy's clean-env dispatch instead of failing 'Authentication failed on toy-doer ... run provision_llm_auth' (typed AgentDispatchError, code AGENT_DISPATCH_FAILED) that eft.48 reported.

What to test / how to assert:
- Add an automated guard script in scripts/ (model it on scripts/check-sandbox-sync-remote.mjs) that, after the playbook's ## Setup + provisioning step runs against a fresh sandbox toy-doer, asserts the member's dispatch env carries the provisioned credential -- i.e. the member record has encryptedEnvVars.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN populated, OR a clean-env probe ('env -i HOME=<sandbox> ... bash -l -c' matching LocalStrategy's exec path) resolves the credential. The check exits 0 when provisioned, non-zero (with an actionable message) when not.
- Reference the guard from the playbook's Test scenario so the smoke-test flow fails loudly at Setup if provisioning was skipped, rather than only surfacing after 5 wasted Planner dispatch retries.
- End-to-end acceptance (may require real runner credentials, consistent with this being an [integ] bug): a fresh smoke-test auto-sprint run's first Planner dispatch authenticates and the canary reaches Planning -- the run no longer terminates 'Sprint FAILED' with AGENT_DISPATCH_FAILED / 'Authentication failed'. Where real credentials are unavailable in the unit context, assert the provisioning precondition via the guard above and document the end-to-end expectation.

Done / acceptance criteria:
- A committed guard/check asserts toy-doer's credentials are provisioned after Setup; it exits non-zero on the pre-fix (unprovisioned) state and 0 after eft.48.1's step.
- The guard is wired into the smoke-test flow (playbook Test scenario references it).
- ASCII only.
close_reason: Closed

title: [impl] Document a credential-provisioning step for toy-doer in integ-test-playbook.md so LocalStrategy clean-env dispatch authenticates
description:
Root cause (from parent eft.48): LocalStrategy dispatches for local members run through a clean-env exec path ('env -i ... bash -l -c') that strips the operator's ambient CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN / macOS Keychain session. A local member only authenticates through that path if its credentials were provisioned (agent.encryptedEnvVars.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN populated via provision_llm_auth, OR machine-level creds written by the 'apra-fleet auth' CLI which getCleanEnv() picks up because it sources login profiles via 'bash -l' -- see docs/tools-infrastructure.md 'apra-fleet auth (CLI)'). integ-test-playbook.md registers toy-doer (## Test scenario step 1 / ## Setup) but never provisions credentials, so every real Planner dispatch fails 'Authentication failed on toy-doer ... run provision_llm_auth'.

Files to change: integ-test-playbook.md (## Setup and/or ## Test scenario step 1). Keep it shell-drivable per the playbook's own rule (## Setup/Reset/Teardown are copy-paste Bash; member registration uses the register-member CLI, not the MCP tool). Prefer the 'apra-fleet auth --oauth' CLI path (single-machine CI-runner model, docs/tools-infrastructure.md lines 96-161) sourcing a credential from the persistent secret store (secure.<name>) rather than the SSH-based provision_llm_auth MCP flow; if the MCP provision_llm_auth path is documented instead, note the member_id resolution. Add the provisioning command block immediately after toy-doer is registered, before the auto-sprint run.

Done / acceptance criteria:
- integ-test-playbook.md contains a numbered, copy-paste Bash step that provisions LLM credentials for the freshly-registered toy-doer member, placed after register-member and before the auto-sprint run.
- The step documents which credential source the runner uses (e.g. secure.<name> from the persistent store seeded via 'apra-fleet secret --set ... --persist') and asserts it never writes to the real fleet-e2e-toy git/Dolt remote.
- The step's mechanism matches LocalStrategy's real clean-env exec path so the credential is visible to 'env -i ... bash -l -c' dispatch (either encryptedEnvVars populated, or creds written where getCleanEnv()'s 'bash -l' profile-sourcing finds them).
- ASCII only; no non-ASCII characters introduced.
close_reason: Closed

title: [integ] Smoke test's toy-doer member has no LLM credentials provisioned -- every real Planner dispatch fails 'Authentication failed'
description:
Feature: N/A (playbook part 2 -- smoke test, integ-test-playbook.md ## Setup + ## Test scenario step 3)
Expected: after ## Setup registers toy-doer (register-member --type local --name toy-doer --path $HOME/toy-repo --llm claude) and ## Test scenario step 3 runs 'apra-fleet workflow auto-sprint --issue <canary> --members toy-doer ...', the real Planner dispatch authenticates and produces a plan.
Actual: every real dispatch attempt (10 total across 2 separate fresh auto-sprint runs this cycle, 5 retries each) fails identically: '[Agent API Error] Authentication failed on "toy-doer". Run /login to refresh your credentials, then run provision_llm_auth to deploy them to this agent.' The sprint correctly gives up after 5 retries and reports 'Sprint FAILED' with a typed AgentDispatchError (code AGENT_DISPATCH_FAILED) -- this is NOT a hang (eft.28's fix holds), but the canary never gets planned/closed, so Test scenario steps 3-5 can never pass.
Root cause (confirmed via direct repro + docs/learnings.md's eft.40.4 evidence): LocalStrategy dispatches for local members run through a clean-env ('env -i ... bash -l -c') exec path by design, which strips the operator's own ambient CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN/macOS Keychain session. A member only authenticates through this clean-env path if 'agent.encryptedEnvVars.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN' has been populated via the real 'provision_llm_auth' tool. integ-test-playbook.md's ## Setup registers toy-doer but never calls provision_llm_auth (or any credential-provisioning step) for it, so the member has zero stored credentials. Manually confirmed: 'claude --agent planner -p ... --model opus' run directly (inheriting my ambient CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN) succeeds instantly; the SAME command run via 'env -i HOME=<sandbox> ... claude ...' (clean env, matching LocalStrategy's real exec path) fails with the CLI's own '{"result":"Not logged in - Please run /login"}', matching the fleet's exact failure signature (exit=1, in=0, out=0, ~1-2s).
Test: 2 separate 'node dist/index.js workflow auto-sprint --issue gh-toy-4ef --members toy-doer --branch smoke-test-canary --base main --max-cycles 1 --dispatch-timeout-s 900' runs, both against a freshly-Reset sandbox with all eft.47/eft.18 neutralize/bootstrap fixes applied and verified working (git fetch origin succeeds, integ-canary tag resolves) -- both still fail identically on every one of 5 Planner dispatch retries with the same Authentication-failed error, then terminate cleanly (Sprint FAILED).
Repro:
1. Fresh/Reset sandbox per integ-test-playbook.md ## Setup / ## Reset (through the full Neutralize sequence).
2. register-member --type local --name toy-doer --path $HOME/toy-repo --llm claude (verbatim playbook command -- no provision_llm_auth call).
3. Resolve a canary (integ-canary tag lookup or self-provision).
4. node dist/index.js workflow auto-sprint --issue <canary> --members toy-doer --branch smoke-test-canary --base main --max-cycles 1 --dispatch-timeout-s 900
5. Observe: every Planner dispatch attempt (up to 5 retries) fails with 'Authentication failed on toy-doer ... run provision_llm_auth'; sprint terminates as FAILED; canary never closes.
Fix direction: integ-test-playbook.md's ## Setup needs a documented provision_llm_auth (or equivalent CLI) step for the toy-doer member using real credentials the runner has access to, OR the Test scenario needs to explicitly seed agent.encryptedEnvVars.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN the same way the eft.40.4 verification harness did. This is a playbook/test-environment gap, not an application-code defect -- LocalStrategy's clean-env isolation and the provision_llm_auth requirement are working exactly as designed; the smoke test simply never exercises that provisioning step.
notes:
integ-test-runner (2026-07-21, verification-closure pass #2): STILL REPRODUCES -- left open, not closed. Ran a fresh Setup (this machine has no $REAL_HOME/.claude/.credentials.json, so step 3 correctly fell back to the documented bare CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN env-var path per the playbook's own fallback rule), provisioned via 'secret --set INTEG-TOY-DOER-TOKEN --persist -y | auth --oauth --llm claude secure.INTEG-TOY-DOER-TOKEN' exactly as documented, and confirmed check-toy-doer-credentials.mjs (hardened by eft.48.4) reports OK (synthetic expiresAt present, sufficient shape). Despite that OK, the real auto-sprint run failed identically to this bug's original report: 'Authentication failed on toy-doer ... run provision_llm_auth', classified from the real CLI's own 'Not logged in' text. Root-caused further via direct clean-env repro (env -i HOME=$SANDBOX ... bash -l -c claude -p ...): with ONLY $HOME/.claude/.credentials.json populated (accessToken + synthetic expiresAt, the exact shape eft.48.3's bare-token branch writes), the real installed claude CLI (2.1.212) still says 'Not logged in - Please run /login' for both a valid and an invalid model id, ruling out a model-selection red herring. Control test: exporting the SAME token as an ambient CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN env var (not via the credentials file) into the identical clean-env probe authenticates fine (gets past auth straight to a model-not-found error) -- so the token itself is valid; the credentials-FILE path specifically (the one LocalStrategy's clean-env dispatch and this playbook's step 3 both rely on for local members) is what the real CLI rejects when the file only carries accessToken+synthetic-expiresAt (no refreshToken/scopes/subscriptionType). eft.48.3's 'full object passthrough when available' branch may well work (per the ORCHESTRATOR's Windows rehearsal note, which sourced a genuine full claudeAiOauth object from a real credentials file) but the 'synthesize expiresAt for a bare token' branch -- the one this environment's fallback actually exercises, and which the playbook explicitly endorses as a valid path for CI runners without a real credentials file -- does not make the file CLI-acceptable in practice. Fix direction: either the bare-token synthetic-session-shape trick needs a different/additional field the real CLI actually honors, or the playbook's bare-token fallback needs to be retired in favor of requiring a real full credentials object (or the env-var/provision_llm_auth path) on runners like this one. Leaving eft.48 open.
ORCHESTRATOR STEER (2026-07-21, post-Integ-C4): the C4 evidence pins the remaining defect precisely. On macOS runners the real Claude credential lives in the Keychain -- REAL_HOME/.claude/.credentials.json does NOT exist -- so the playbook's full-claudeAiOauth-JSON path can never engage there and the flow degrades to the bare CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN env fallback, whose synthesized file (accessToken + synthetic expiresAt, eft.48.3) the real CLI still rejects; C4's runner proved the SAME token exported as an env var authenticates fine. STOP iterating on credential-FILE shape: provision the token into the member's dispatch ENVIRONMENT instead, using the mechanism that already exists -- register_member/update_member encryptedEnvVars.CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN, which LocalStrategy's clean-env dispatch injects and which check-toy-doer-credentials.mjs already probes as its registry.json path. Concrete shape: playbook step 3 sets the member's CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN env var via the CLI (secret store reference, never plaintext in chat/git) as the PRIMARY path on all platforms; keep the file path only as a bonus when a real credentials file exists (Windows/Linux runners). This also removes the need for auth --oauth entirely in the smoke test. Note for the doer: the env-var route must reach getCleanEnv()'s child env for LOCAL members -- verify with the checker's env-var probe, then the real dispatch.
ORCHESTRATOR CI EVIDENCE (branch CI run 29860404996, windows-latest): tests/check-toy-doer-credentials.test.ts (clean-env probe tests), plus auth-oauth-secret.test.ts and check-integ-suite-budget.test.ts on the same runner, fail on Windows because the checker's probe runs "env -i ... bash -l -c ..." via execSync with no shell override -- cmd.exe cannot parse it ('true' is not recognized...). Latent, not new: Windows CI never reached these tests before (earlier runs failed first on other OSes). Fix shape, precedent 04c5e12 (integ-test-playbook-teardown.test.ts): use bash.exe explicitly on win32 (BASH_SHELL pattern) where Git Bash suffices; where the probe is POSIX-only BY DESIGN (LocalStrategy's Windows clean-env is PowerShell registry-derived, the env-i-bash path never runs there), gate with an explicit platform skip instead. Files likely touched by eft.48.6/48.7 -- fold into their scope if still open, else new child.
ORCHESTRATOR EVIDENCE AUDIT (2026-07-21, post-Integ-C5): the C5 'STILL REPRODUCES' verdict above is STALE EVIDENCE, not a post-fix result. Its repro describes the PRE-eft.48.6 file shape (accessToken + synthetic expiresAt, no scopes) and the C5 integ runner explicitly reused the part-2 smoke run from earlier in its resumed session ('was already run and torn down earlier in this same session'), i.e. from BEFORE eft.48.6 (bare-token write now adds scopes:[user:inference]) was merged and deployed. The C5 R2 reviewer's real-CLI check on fleet-rev showed the post-48.6 bare-token write authenticating (past auth to the bogus-probe-model error). CONCLUSION: no smoke run has ever exercised the 48.6 fix; this bug may already be resolved. Next run's integ MUST execute a FRESH part 2 (fresh sandbox, fresh provisioning) at the current tip before treating this as reproducing; do not inherit this cycle's verdict.

title: [test] Stale bootstrap Dolt DB but JSONL-tagged canary: smoke test resolves the git-tracked integ-canary without writing the real remote
description:
Feature: N/A (regression test under bug apra-fleet-eft.18, pins the eft.18.3 fix).

What to test: reproduce the JSONL-vs-Dolt-DB divergence and assert the canary resolver recovers from the authoritative git-tracked JSONL. Fixture: a toy-repo clone whose git-tracked .beads/issues.jsonl has gh-toy-4ef labeled 'integ-canary' (as PR #96 merged) while the bootstrapped local Dolt DB is stale (gh-toy-4ef labels=['e2e-testing'] only, no integ-canary), simulating a fresh 'bd bootstrap --yes'.

How to assert pass/fail (vitest, same harness/mocks as eft.18.2): (1) with the Dolt-DB lookup returning [] for label=integ-canary, the resolver reads the JSONL and returns gh-toy-4ef as the canary id; (2) when neither the Dolt DB nor the JSONL carries the tag, the resolver falls back to a local-only self-provisioned canary (eft.18.1 behaviour preserved); (3) in every path assert NO write or push targets the real fleet-e2e-toy Dolt remote (spy on the push/remote calls and assert not-called / inert remote).

Done criteria: test fails against pre-eft.18.3 code (canary unresolved when Dolt DB stale) and passes after the fix; runs green in the normal vitest suite with no network/real-remote access.
close_reason: Added tests/integ-canary-jsonl-reconcile.test.ts (vitest, real bd binary against disposable local sandboxes, no upstream remote ever configured): (1) stale-Dolt-DB label lookup returns [] even on retry without reconciling; a local-only 'bd import' from the git-tracked JSONL followed by one retry resolves the canary, pinning the eft.18.3 fix; (2) when neither the DB nor the JSONL carries the tag, self-provisioning (eft.18.1) still yields exactly one usable local canary; (3) a call-spy plus 'bd dolt remote list'/'git remote' checks assert no invocation ever pushes or references the real fleet-e2e-toy remote. Commit 04b0b460, pushed to auto-sprint/eft-service. Verified: npx vitest run on the new file (2/2 pass), plus co-located suites (29/29 pass), and 'npm run build' (tsc) clean.

title: [impl] Resolve integ-canary from the git-tracked JSONL, not the stale bootstrap Dolt-DB sync path
description:
Feature: N/A (regression fix under bug apra-fleet-eft.18). Residual mechanism (NEW -- do NOT duplicate the closed self-provision fallback in eft.18.1): 'bd bootstrap --yes' hydrates the toy-repo canary lookup from a SEPARATE Dolt-DB sync path (Database gh_toy, git+https://github.com/Apra-Labs/fleet-e2e-toy) rather than from the git-tracked .beads/issues.jsonl. PR #96 labeled gh-toy-4ef 'integ-canary' in the git-tracked JSONL only, so a fresh bootstrap's Dolt DB still shows labels=['e2e-testing'] and the smoke test's 'bd list --label=integ-canary' returns [] even though the merged JSONL is correct.

Files to change: the smoke-test canary-resolution helper in the integ-test playbook harness (same module touched by eft.18.1's self-provision fallback -- extend, do not replace it) plus any bootstrap/canary-lookup wiring it calls.

Expected behaviour: when the label lookup against the bootstrapped Dolt DB is empty, the resolver first reconciles/reads the git-tracked .beads/issues.jsonl (the authoritative merged source) to find any issue tagged integ-canary before falling back to self-provisioning a local-only canary. The lookup must never depend solely on the stale Dolt-DB sync path, and must still never write or push to the real fleet-e2e-toy Dolt remote.

Done criteria: after a fresh 'bd bootstrap --yes' against a clone whose git-tracked JSONL has gh-toy-4ef labeled integ-canary (as PR #96 merged), the smoke test resolves gh-toy-4ef as the canary from the JSONL even though the synced Dolt DB is stale; when the JSONL also lacks the tag the existing self-provision fallback still triggers; no write/push to the real Dolt remote occurs in either path.
close_reason: Revised integ-test-playbook.md (## Reset + ## Test scenario step 2) so the canary tag lookup reconciles the local Dolt DB from the git-tracked .beads/issues.jsonl via a local-only 'bd import' and retries once before falling back to the eft.18.1 self-provision path. bd import never pushes, preserving isolation. Commit 2e566f53, pushed to auto-sprint/eft-service.

title: [test] Neutralized sandbox origin is fetchable-yet-isolated: git fetch origin main succeeds and all 4 sync-remote checks pass
description: Verify the eft.47 fix end to end at the isolation boundary. Test: from a fresh sandbox toy clone, run the documented ## Setup + Neutralize sequence (now pointing origin at the local bare clone), then ASSERT: (1) node scripts/check-sandbox-sync-remote.mjs $HOME/toy-repo exits 0 with all 4 checks OK; (2) 'git fetch origin main' against the neutralized origin exits 0 (the sprint engine's Ensure-Sprint-Branch fetch can run); (3) the neutralized origin URL does NOT resolve to git+https://github.com/Apra-Labs/fleet-e2e-toy (still isolated from the real remote -- no push/fetch can reach real GitHub). title: [impl] Neutralize sandbox git origin to a fetchable local bare clone (not /dev/null) and accept it in check-sandbox-sync-remote.mjs
description: Root cause: eft.31's neutralize step rewrites the sandbox clone's git origin to file:///dev/null/neutralized-sandbox-origin, which is unfetchable, so the sprint engine's legitimate 'git fetch origin main' in the 'Ensure Sprint Branch' phase fails with exit 128 and the sprint aborts before any Plan dispatch. Files (<=3): integ-test-playbook.md (## Setup Neutralize subsection, git remote set-url step) and scripts/check-sandbox-sync-remote.mjs (checkGitOriginNotHazard). Change: (1) In the Neutralize sequence, instead of pointing origin at /dev/null, create a second BARE local clone of the toy-repo content (e.g. git clone --bare into a sandbox path) and set origin to that file:// path -- a real, empty-writable, fetchable git repo fully isolated from git+https://github.com/Apra-Labs/fleet-e2e-toy. (2) Update checkGitOriginNotHazard so a local file:// bare clone passes as non-hazard while the real GitHub fleet-e2e-toy remote is still rejected. DONE when: after the documented Neutralize sequence, 'git fetch origin main' succeeds against the neutralized origin AND check-sandbox-sync-remote.mjs still reports all 4 checks OK, with no reachability to the real remote. ACCEPTANCE: from a fresh sandbox following ## Setup + Neutralize verbatim, git fetch origin main exits 0 and the auto-sprint run proceeds past 'Ensure Sprint Branch' into Plan.
close_reason: Neutralize step now points sandbox git origin at a fetchable local bare clone instead of /dev/null; verified git fetch origin main exits 0 and check-sandbox-sync-remote.mjs still reports all 4 checks OK.

title: [test] DOM-level scroll regression: #stream-list scrollbar engages and last activity reachable via scrollTop
description: Fills the gap the bug notes name: no automated DOM-level test exists for this acceptance yet (grep of packages/apra-fleet-se/test and tests/ found no scrollHeight/streamList/clientHeight coverage). Use the existing viewer test harness (jsdom/DOM-level, same framework as other viewer tests). Test: load the dashboard populated with enough activity rows to exceed the viewport, then ASSERT streamList.scrollHeight > streamList.clientHeight (scrollbar engaged, PASS) and that setting streamList.scrollTop reaches/exposes the last activity row. Fail the test if scrollHeight == clientHeight (container grew instead of capping). Framework: viewer's existing DOM test harness (vitest + jsdom). title: [impl] Bound #stream-list via min-height:0 down the flex-chain so its overflow-y:auto scrollbar engages
description: Residual after eft.43.1 (which only bounded html/body + tree rows). Files: the viewer dashboard HTML/CSS in packages/apra-fleet-se viewer (the page that renders #stream-list). Change: apply min-height:0 (and flex-basis where needed) to every flex ancestor between the viewport-height flex column and #stream-list, so #stream-list's own overflow-y:auto actually caps to the viewport instead of the container growing with content. Keep body overflow:hidden's snappiness constraint from eft.27 (no scroll-jank regression). DONE when, with more activities than fit the viewport, #stream-list.scrollHeight > #stream-list.clientHeight (its scrollbar is live) and the container height stays viewport-bounded rather than growing with content. ACCEPTANCE: loading the dashboard with enough activities to exceed the viewport yields streamList.scrollHeight > streamList.clientHeight, and the last activity row is reachable via streamList.scrollTop.
