Separately (not fatal, but noted): during this same run, 'bd dolt push' was invoked unconditionally during the pre-dispatch sync bracket and failed with 'could not read Username for https://github.com: Device not configured' -- it retried once then continued: this did NOT block progress (sandbox isolation was preserved because the push failed rather than succeeded) but conflicts with the playbook's stated 'skip_dolt_push semantics via the disabled sync.remote' framing (Setup section) -- the sync.remote-disabled config does not actually prevent 'bd dolt push' from being attempted, it is only saved from reaching the real remote by the sandbox having no GitHub credentials configured. Worth a closer look but not the primary defect here.
Test: integ-test-playbook.md Test scenario steps 1-4 (register-member, self-provision canary since no upstream integ-canary tag found, run auto-sprint max_cycles:1, assert canary closes). Steps 1-3 ran; step 3's own dispatch never completes; step 4's assertions (canary closed, branch has a doer/harvest commit) were never reachable.
Repro:
1. Fresh Setup per integ-test-playbook.md (install, start server on 18700, clone fleet-e2e-toy).
2. register-member --type local --name toy-doer --path $HOME/toy-repo --llm claude (verbatim playbook command, no --unattended flag).
3. bd bootstrap --yes (needed on a truly fresh clone); self-provision an integ-canary task (no upstream tag found this run).
4. Set projects[$HOME/toy-repo].hasTrustDialogAccepted=true in $HOME/.claude.json (otherwise the FIRST symptom you hit is 'this workspace has not been trusted', masking this bug).
5. node dist/index.js workflow auto-sprint --issue <canary-id> --members toy-doer --branch <branch> --base main --max-cycles 1
6. Observe: 'Dispatch phase: Plan C1 R1' logs once, then total silence in both the workflow CLI output and the fleet server log for many minutes with no watchdog/timeout ever firing (despite apra-fleet-eft.20.3 having supposedly added a watchdog to surface silent doer/orchestrator death -- that fix does not appear to cover this earlier failure point, where the member's OWN claude process died before ever producing a plan).
Related: apra-fleet-eft.20 (a different, later-phase silent-stall symptom -- state.json corruption after a plan WAS approved; that bug's fixes (eft.20.1-3) are all closed but evidently did not cover this earlier dispatch-hang case). apra-fleet-eft.24 (pre-sprint validation hard-fail on a childless target) did NOT reproduce this run -- validation passed fine and 'Plan C1 R1' was dispatched.
notes: integ-test-runner (2026-07-21): left OPEN, inconclusive -- could not independently verify this cycle. The smoke test's auto-sprint run aborted in the 'Ensure Sprint Branch' phase (git fetch against the neutralized sandbox origin, exit 128) BEFORE any Planner dispatch was attempted -- newly filed as apra-fleet-eft.47 -- so this cycle never reached the dispatch path eft.28's fix covers. Additionally, this cycle's real-bd suite run (part 1) showed the two direct regression tests for this fix -- mock-sprint-planner-dispatch-dead-pid.test.mjs (eft.28.2) and mock-sprint-planner-dispatch-stalled-session.test.mjs (eft.28.4) -- both FAILED via node:test timeout under concurrency=8 (dead-pid: the correct AGENT_DISPATCH_FAILED error was produced, just ~46ms after the test's own 180s kill; stalled-session: no log captured before its 650s kill). Filed as apra-fleet-eft.46 (likely real-dolt-latency/contention under concurrency, not a proven correctness regression, but not proof of correctness either). Net: no positive re-confirmation this cycle that the dispatch-hang fix still holds end-to-end under real conditions. Re-check once eft.47 is fixed (so the smoke test can reach Plan dispatch again) and/or eft.46 is resolved.
close_reason: integ-test-runner (2026-07-21): CONFIRMED FIXED, both re-check conditions this bug's own last note named are now met (eft.47 fixed so the smoke test reaches Plan dispatch again; eft.46 resolved). This cycle's smoke test ran 2 separate real auto-sprint attempts against gh-toy-4ef, each hitting 5 Planner dispatch retries (blocked by a NEW, distinct credential-provisioning gap, filed separately as apra-fleet-eft.48) -- in all 10 attempts across both runs, the dispatch/retry loop logged continuously (dispatch attempt, wait Ns, retry N/5) with zero silent gaps, and each run terminated with a clean, typed, logged error (AgentDispatchError / code AGENT_DISPATCH_FAILED, 'Sprint FAILED') rather than an indefinite silent hang -- the exact symptom this bug reports never reproduced. This live observation plus the real-bd suite's direct regression coverage (mock-sprint-planner-dispatch-dead-pid.test.mjs and mock-sprint-planner-dispatch-stalled-session.test.mjs, both PASS this cycle within their timeouts -- see eft.46's closure) together confirm the eft.28.1/28.3/28.5/28.6/28.7 dead-session-eviction and liveness-timeout fixes hold end to end.

title: [test] after bd bootstrap + neutralize step, sandbox sync.remote is inert (no active fleet-e2e-toy remote before any auto-sprint run)
description: Add a shell-drivable assertion (in the integ-test-playbook.md sandbox flow and/or a small test/check script alongside the existing integ tooling) that verifies the eft.25 remedy: in a fresh toy-repo sandbox clone under HOME=~/temp/.apra-fleet-tests, run 'bd bootstrap --yes' then the neutralize step from eft.25.1, then grep/parse .beads/config.yaml and assert there is NO active (uncommented) 'sync.remote' line pointing at git+https://github.com/Apra-Labs/fleet-e2e-toy. Also assert the safety outcome that eft.25's description checks: 'git rev-list --left-right --count HEAD...origin/main' shows 0 outbound commits pushed to the real remote. Keep the check idempotent and sandbox-only (never touches the real remote).
acceptance_criteria: Assertion FAILS if run only after 'bd bootstrap --yes' (active sync.remote present -> hazard) and PASSES after the eft.25.1 neutralize step (sync.remote inert). Check is shell-drivable, ASCII-only, and never writes to or pushes the real Dolt remote.
close_reason: Added scripts/check-sandbox-sync-remote.mjs, wired into integ-test-playbook.md's Setup verify step: asserts no active sync.remote line references fleet-e2e-toy and 0 outbound commits ahead of origin/main. Verified it FAILs on the eft.25 hazard shape and PASSes after the eft.25.1 neutralize step, both via unit tests (tests/check-sandbox-sync-remote.test.ts, 12 passing, local-only git fixtures, no real remote touched) and a manual CLI smoke run.

title: [impl] Neutralize sandbox sync.remote after bd bootstrap so toy-repo runs never push to the real fleet-e2e-toy Dolt remote
description: File: integ-test-playbook.md (## Setup / ## Reset sections, around lines 97-146 where the sandbox toy-repo clone and the 'bd bootstrap --yes' recovery step live). Root cause (eft.25): 'bd bootstrap --yes' rewrites .beads/config.yaml to add an ACTIVE 'sync:\n  remote: git+https://github.com/Apra-Labs/fleet-e2e-toy' block (leaving the shipped commented-out 'sync.remote disabled' line stale), so a later 'bd dolt push' from a real sprint run would push sandbox mutations to the shared external remote, defeating the playbook's isolation guarantee. Implement fix direction (b) (in-repo, does not depend on a bd behaviour change): add an explicit, shell-drivable sandbox step -- run immediately after any 'bd bootstrap --yes' and before any auto-sprint run -- that re-disables/neutralizes sync.remote in the sandbox clone's .beads/config.yaml (comment it out again or otherwise render it inert) so skip_dolt_push semantics hold even once real doer/harvester commits start landing. Document why (latent hazard: fires the next time a sandbox run completes a real commit, e.g. once eft.24 is fixed).
acceptance_criteria: After running 'bd bootstrap --yes' followed by the new neutralize step in a fresh toy-repo sandbox clone, .beads/config.yaml has NO active sync.remote pointing at git+https://github.com/Apra-Labs/fleet-e2e-toy (it is commented out / inert). The step is shell-drivable (copy-pasteable) and lives in the ## Setup/## Reset flow before the ## Test scenario sprint run. Existing playbook steps remain ASCII-only and internally consistent.
close_reason: Added shell-drivable neutralize step to ## Setup (and cross-ref in ## Reset) of integ-test-playbook.md that comments out any active sync.remote pointing at fleet-e2e-toy after bd bootstrap --yes, tested idempotent against both flat and nested config.yaml forms on macOS/BSD sed.

title: [test] childless single-issue sprint target passes pre-sprint validation and reaches Planning
description: Add an automated test (co-located with the existing auto-sprint runner tests, e.g. packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/*.test.mjs; use the real-bd/mock-sprint harness pattern already used for bdListScoped/pre-sprint-validation coverage). Assert both sides of eft.24: (1) bdListScoped with targetIssues = a single leaf bead that has zero children returns a non-empty set that INCLUDES the target itself (for the plain '' query and for a --status query); (2) pre-sprint validation does NOT hard-fail for that childless target and the cycle advances past the 'Sprint Setup' group into Planning. Include a regression guard for the decomposed-target case: a target that already has task children must not be returned as a directly-dispatchable ready bead alongside its own children.
acceptance_criteria: Test fails against the pre-fix runner (childless target -> empty scope -> validation abort) and passes after eft.24.1: childless leaf target yields non-empty scope including itself and reaches Planning; decomposed-target guard still holds. Test runs green under the existing suite runner.
close_reason: Closed

title: [impl] Include target issue(s) in bdListScoped scope so a childless leaf sprint target passes pre-sprint validation and reaches Planning
description: File: packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/runner.js, bdListScoped() (~lines 2698-2745; scope BFS at 2711-2723). Root cause (eft.24): scopeIds is built from strict DESCENDANTS of targetIssues only; when the -i target is a leaf bead with zero children, scopeIds.size===0, bdListScoped short-circuits to [] for both the --ready and notDoneBeads queries, and the pre-sprint validation hard-fail at ~runner.js:3255 fires before the Planner ever runs. Change: seed scopeIds with the targetIssues themselves (add each target id to scopeIds, and to the frontier so its future descendants are still discovered) so a childless target is in-scope and validation proceeds, letting the Planner decompose the leaf into task-type children. Preserve existing behaviour for targets that already have descendants. Be mindful of the xbu.C5 / GRAPH-SEMANTICS guard: a target that HAS been decomposed into task children must still not be dispatched directly to a doer alongside its own subtasks -- verify the existing decomposed-node dispatch guard still holds after the target is added to scope (i.e. including the target in scope must not cause a grouping node with children to be dispatched).
acceptance_criteria: Running auto-sprint with -i pointed at a freshly-created childless leaf issue (issue_type=task, no children) no longer aborts with 'Pre-sprint validation failed: No open/in-progress/blocked/deferred beads found for scope'; the target itself appears in bdListScoped results (both '' and --status queries) and the sprint reaches the Planning phase. A target that already has task-type children still behaves as before and is NOT dispatched directly to a doer (decomposed-node guard intact).
close_reason: Closed

title: [integ] bd bootstrap in the toy-repo sandbox re-activates sync.remote pointing at the real fleet-e2e-toy Dolt remote
description:
Feature: N/A (playbook part 2 -- smoke test sandbox safety, integ-test-playbook.md ## Reset / ## Test scenario)
Expected: the smoke test's toy-repo sandbox clone must never be wired to push to the real Dolt remote (git+https://github.com/Apra-Labs/fleet-e2e-toy) -- integ-test-playbook.md Test scenario step 3 explicitly requires 'never write to the real Dolt remote from a sandbox run', and the fresh clone's .beads/config.yaml ships with sync.remote commented out for exactly this reason ('# sync.remote disabled -- no Dolt push for this toy project').
Actual: running 'bd bootstrap --yes' (the documented recovery step for the 'no beads database found' error hit on a fresh clone -- see apra-fleet-eft.18's repro, which also used it) clones/hydrates from the remote successfully, but as a side effect REWRITES .beads/config.yaml to add an ACTIVE, uncommented block:
  sync:
      remote: "git+https://github.com/Apra-Labs/fleet-e2e-toy"
while leaving the old commented-out 'sync.remote disabled' line stale below it. From this point on, any 'bd dolt push' invoked by a real auto-sprint run against this sandbox (the orchestrator's per-commit D-push discipline, e.g. doltPushAfter() in packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/runner.js) would push sandbox test mutations to the SAME shared external Dolt remote real users/CI depend on -- defeating the playbook's explicit isolation guarantee. This run's own sprint attempt aborted before any commit/push occurred (see apra-fleet-eft.24), so no push actually reached the remote this cycle (confirmed: git rev-list --left-right --count HEAD...origin/main == '0 0' after the run) -- but the hazard is latent and will fire the next time a sandbox run gets far enough to complete a real doer/harvester commit, e.g. once eft.24 is fixed.
Test: manual inspection -- diff .beads/config.yaml before/after 'bd bootstrap --yes' in a fresh toy-repo clone; before has sync.remote commented out, after has it live.
Repro: 1) HOME=~/temp/.apra-fleet-tests APRA_FLEET_PORT=18700, fresh git clone of https://github.com/Apra-Labs/fleet-e2e-toy per playbook Setup. 2) cat .beads/config.yaml -- note sync.remote is commented out. 3) bd bootstrap --yes. 4) cat .beads/config.yaml again -- note the new active 'sync: remote: git+https://github.com/Apra-Labs/fleet-e2e-toy' block.
Suggested fix direction: either (a) have bd bootstrap not persist an active sync.remote when the pre-existing config explicitly had it disabled/commented, or (b) have the playbook/runner explicitly re-disable/neutralize sync.remote (or set an override) in the sandbox immediately after bootstrap, before any auto-sprint run, so skip_dolt_push semantics hold even once real work starts landing.
close_reason: Verified 2026-07-20/21: bd bootstrap --yes still reactivates sync.remote (expected/unchanged bd behavior), but the documented eft.25.1 neutralize step immediately fixes it -- check-sandbox-sync-remote.mjs's checkSyncRemoteInert reports OK right after. Dolt-level remote hazard covered separately by eft.30 (also closed).

title: [integ] auto-sprint pre-sprint validation rejects a childless single-issue sprint target, blocking the smoke test's core path
description:
Feature: N/A (playbook part 2 -- smoke test, integ-test-playbook.md ## Test scenario step 3)
Expected: per integ-test-playbook.md Test scenario step 3-4, running 'apra-fleet workflow auto-sprint -i <canary-id> -m <member> -b <branch> -B main -c 1' against a freshly self-provisioned, childless integ-canary task bead should let the Planner decompose/plan and dispatch work so the canary eventually closes.
Actual: the sprint aborts immediately in the 'Sprint Setup' group ('Ensure Sprint Branch' phase), before the Planner ever runs, with 'Pre-sprint validation failed: No open/in-progress/blocked/deferred beads found for scope --parent <canary-id>. Nothing to do.' Root cause: packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/runner.js's bdListScoped() (used both for the --ready check and the notDoneBeads fallback at ~line 3222-3255) computes scope as strict DESCENDANTS of targetIssues only (BFS over the parent-child childrenOf map) and never includes the target issue(s) themselves in scopeIds. When the target passed via -i is a leaf bead with zero children (exactly what a fresh self-provisioned canary is, and what any brand-new single-task sprint target would be), scopeIds.size===0 so bdListScoped short-circuits to [] for both the --ready and notDoneBeads queries, and the hard fail at runner.js:3255 fires unconditionally -- before Planning, so the Planner never gets a chance to decompose the leaf into task-type children. This makes it structurally impossible to auto-sprint a single freshly-created leaf issue; only a target that ALREADY has at least one not-done descendant can proceed.
Test: integ-test-playbook.md Test scenario steps 1-5, run against sandbox HOME=~/temp/.apra-fleet-tests, APRA_FLEET_PORT=18700, self-provisioned canary gh-toy-9rt (issue_type=task, no children): 'node dist/index.js workflow auto-sprint -i gh-toy-9rt -m toy-doer -b integ-smoke-branch -B main -c 1' exits 1 with the error above; full stack in the run log points to runner.js:3255 inside runSprintCycle, called from workflows/auto-sprint/bin/cli.mjs main.
Repro: 1) Setup per playbook (install/start/clone). 2) register-member. 3) bd bootstrap --yes in $HOME/toy-repo; confirm no integ-canary tag upstream. 4) bd create --title 'canary' --label integ-canary (a plain task bead, no children). 5) node dist/index.js workflow auto-sprint -i <new-canary-id> -m <member> -b <any-new-branch> -B main -c 1. Observe the immediate Pre-sprint validation hard-fail.
Note: this differs from the previously-filed apra-fleet-eft.20 (which reached Plan-approved/Develop against a canary that already had a decomposed child gh-toy-53y.1) -- that run's target evidently already had a child by the time it ran; a genuinely fresh, childless target hits this earlier, harder wall and never reaches Planning at all.
notes: integ-test-runner: reproduced again on 2026-07-20T13:47:43Z against current build (v0.3.5_3c525b). Canary gh-toy-xtr (self-provisioned, no children) -> auto-sprint terminalReason: "Pre-sprint validation failed: No open/in-progress/blocked/deferred beads found for scope '--parent gh-toy-xtr'. Nothing to do." Canary remained open, 0 commits landed on integ-smoke/gh-toy-xtr vs origin/main. Still blocking Test scenario step 3/4. No fix yet.
close_reason: Verified 2026-07-20/21: live auto-sprint run against a freshly self-provisioned childless canary (gh-toy-se5) proceeded past Sprint Setup into Plan C1 R1 and attempted Planner dispatch -- the old 'No open/in-progress/blocked/deferred beads found for scope' hard-fail did not occur. bdListScoped fix confirmed working.

title: [impl] Surface silent doer/orchestrator death after Plan phase: watchdog must log+persist a terminal error instead of exiting quietly
description: The smoke-test run went completely silent: after the doer member session was created (fleet log line at session-registration), the fleet server log showed NO further activity for the rest of the ~10-min run -- no error, no exception, no exit line -- and both the doer member pid and the orchestrator pid were dead afterward with only the plan-approval commits landed. The observable defect to fix here is that a doer sub-session / orchestrator dying mid-Develop produces ZERO diagnostic signal. Add liveness/exit instrumentation so this failure mode is never silent again: (1) the PID-liveness watchdog (from supervisor feature apra-fleet-eft.4) must detect a doer member or orchestrator child that stops making progress / dies and write an explicit terminal error line to the fleet log AND to the sprint state file (e.g. phase-level lastError / a failed status), rather than the run just ending; (2) wrap the doer-dispatch boundary so an unhandled rejection or abrupt process exit during Develop is logged with cause before teardown. This does NOT require fully root-causing the external kill (tracked generically in auto-sprint-2 / auto-sprint-6); it requires making the death observable and recorded. Files: supervisor watchdog + doer-dispatch path under packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint. Cross-reference auto-sprint-2, auto-sprint-6.
acceptance_criteria: When a doer sub-session or orchestrator child dies or stalls mid-Develop, an explicit terminal error is (a) written to the fleet server log and (b) recorded in the sprint state file (failed/lastError), so no run can end after Plan with zero diagnostic signal. A test simulates a doer child that exits/stops responding after the Develop checkpoint and asserts both the log line and the persisted error state are produced.
close_reason: Wired defaultRecordTerminalError into watchdog classifySprint() (dedup per sprintId, error-swallowing), added supervisor-watchdog.test.mjs coverage incl. a simulated doer-death-after-Develop test asserting the TERMINAL ERROR log line + persisted failed/lastError state, and added dedicated unit tests for runner.js's installFatalDiagnosticsGuard. Commit d82b91af. All 724 apra-fleet-se + 159 apra-fleet-workflow tests pass.

title: [test] sprint state.json round-trips through JSON.parse after every per-activity checkpoint write
description: Add a regression test around the per-activity sprint-state writer (feature apra-fleet-eft.2). Drive the writer through a full sequence of phase-transition checkpoint writes (Plan -> approve DAG -> Develop -> Review -> Harvest, incl. cycle/planApproved field updates) and after each write assert the on-disk state file parses cleanly with JSON.parse and reproduces the expected object. Include a direct assertion targeting the observed regression: a phase-transition write must never emit a doubled quote/delimiter (e.g. "phase"":"Develop"). Use the repo test framework (vitest). Test lives alongside the auto-sprint state-persistence tests.
acceptance_criteria: A vitest test drives the state writer through multiple sequential phase/field checkpoint writes and asserts every resulting on-disk state.json JSON.parses successfully and equals the expected object; the test fails if reintroduced with the doubled-quote serialization bug and passes against the fixed writer.
close_reason: Added regression test driving DebouncedStateWriter through full phase-transition checkpoint sequence with doubled-quote assertion; commit e249dc9d

title: [impl] Fix corrupted sprint state.json write: doubled-quote serialization producing "phase"":"Develop"
description: Locate the per-activity sprint-state writer implemented by feature apra-fleet-eft.2 (continuous running/ -> old_sprints/ state persistence) that produces sprint-logs/.state/<sprint>.state.json. A run observed in the smoke-test sandbox wrote MALFORMED JSON with a stray doubled quote after the phase key: ...,"cycle":1,"phase"":"Develop","planApproved":true} (note "phase"": instead of "phase":). This is almost certainly a hand-rolled string concatenation / partial in-place field patch rather than a single JSON.stringify of the whole object. Files: the state-persistence module under packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint (the writer that checkpoints phase transitions). Fix: serialize the entire state object via JSON.stringify (or otherwise guarantee the emitted bytes always round-trip), so no partial string splicing can ever inject a duplicate quote/delimiter. Ensure atomic write (write temp + rename) so a partial/interleaved write cannot leave malformed JSON on disk.
acceptance_criteria: The state writer emits state.json via a single serialization pass that always round-trips through JSON.parse; no code path performs partial in-place quote/field string splicing that could double a quote or delimiter; writes are atomic (temp file + rename). Manually reproducing rapid phase-transition checkpoint writes never yields a file that fails JSON.parse.
close_reason: Routed all sprint-state JSON writes (debounced per-activity writer + terminal persistState snapshot) through a single shared writeJsonFileAtomic() helper: one JSON.stringify() pass + temp-file-then-rename. Eliminates any possibility of partial field-splicing corruption and guarantees atomic writes. Regression tests added; full apra-fleet-workflow suite (158 tests) passes.

title: [integ] Smoke test: sandbox auto-sprint stalls silently after Plan phase, writes corrupted state.json, canary never closes
description:
Feature: smoke test (integ-test-playbook.md Test scenario, part 2 of the integration pass)
Expected: apra-fleet workflow auto-sprint against canary gh-toy-53y (max_cycles:1, skip_dolt_push:true) completes Plan -> Develop -> Review -> Harvest and closes the canary issue, per playbook Test scenario steps 3-4.
Actual: The auto-sprint claude process (branch integ-smoke/gh-toy-53y-1784530718) ran ~10 minutes then exited on its own. Only 2 commits landed (chore: sprint-meta, plan: approve task DAG) -- Plan phase completed and was approved (state file shows phase=Develop, planApproved=true, cycle=1) but no Develop/doer commits, no Review, no Harvest ever happened. The canary issue gh-toy-53y (and its decomposed child gh-toy-53y.1 with its [impl]/[test] subtasks) remained OPEN the entire time. Fleet server log (data/logs/fleet-65435.log) shows the doer member 'smoke-doer' (pid 65544) registered and a session was created at 02:59:00, then NO further log activity at all for the rest of the ~10-minute run -- no error, no exception, no exit line. The doer member process (pid 65544) and the auto-sprint orchestrator process (pid 66152) were both confirmed dead afterward with nothing further committed or logged. This matches the silent-death-mid-Develop-phase symptom already tracked generically in auto-sprint-2, but this is a fresh, independently reproduced instance in the isolated smoke-test sandbox with a concrete corruption artifact attached (see below), so filing separately rather than folding into auto-sprint-2 (different branch/run, 2 days apart, has its own new evidence).

Additionally found in the same sandbox run: the sprint state file sprint-logs/.state/integ-smoke-gh-toy-53y-1784530718.state.json contains MALFORMED JSON -- a stray duplicated quote after the phase key:
  ...,"cycle":1,"phase"":"Develop","planApproved":true}
(note the doubled quote: "phase"":"Develop" instead of "phase":"Develop"). Confirmed invalid with json.loads / JSON.parse ("Expecting ':' delimiter"). This is the durable per-activity state-persistence mechanism that feature apra-fleet-eft.2 ('[Phase 0] Continuous per-activity sprint-state persistence (running/ -> old_sprints/...)') implemented and which was closed as done. A corrupted state file is consistent with (though not proven to directly cause) the run going silent right after the phase=Develop checkpoint write: if anything downstream (crash-resume detection, watchdog, or the run's own next state read) tries to JSON.parse this file, it will throw.

Test: integ-test-playbook.md Test scenario steps 1-4 (register_member, find/self-provision canary, run apra-fleet workflow auto-sprint max_cycles:1 skip_dolt_push:true, assert canary closed + branch has a commit). Steps 1-3 ran; step 4's assertion FAILS -- canary gh-toy-53y is still open, and while the branch does have 2 commits, neither is a doer/harvest commit (no actual work on the canary's task landed).

Repro:
1. export HOME=~/temp/.apra-fleet-tests; export USERPROFILE="$HOME"; export APRA_FLEET_PORT=18700
2. Fresh Setup per integ-test-playbook.md (install, start server on 18700, clone https://github.com/Apra-Labs/fleet-e2e-toy)
3. register_member against $HOME/toy-repo; find/self-provision the integ-canary-tagged issue
4. Run apra-fleet workflow auto-sprint against that issue with max_cycles:1, skip_dolt_push:true
5. Observe: process runs for several minutes, only a plan-approval commit lands, canary stays open, fleet-<pid>.log in $HOME/.apra-fleet/data/logs/ goes silent after the doer member's initial session-registration line
6. Inspect sprint-logs/.state/<branch-safe-name>.state.json in the toy repo -- check whether it round-trips through JSON.parse cleanly
notes: integ-test-runner (2026-07-20, cycle 2): could not re-attempt this scenario this cycle -- a fresh self-provisioned canary now hits an EARLIER, harder blocker (apra-fleet-eft.24: pre-sprint validation rejects any childless single-issue sprint target before Planning ever runs), so the run never reached Plan/Develop far enough to re-exercise this bug's specific silent-death/corrupted-state.json scenario. Leaving open/unverified pending eft.24.
close_reason: Verified 2026-07-20/21: could not reach live Develop phase this cycle (blocked earlier by separate already-open eft.28, updated with fresh repro). Both discrete defects independently verified via direct fresh test runs: apra-fleet-workflow-sprint-state.test.mjs's eft.20.2 checkpoint round-trip test passes (drives real DebouncedStateWriter through full phase sequence, doubled-quote regression assertion included); supervisor-watchdog.test.mjs (eft.20.3) passed clean in playbook part 1's 85/85 real-bd suite.

title: [test] fresh toy clone with no upstream integ-canary yields a usable local canary and never writes the real Dolt remote
description:
Verifies apra-fleet-eft.18.1.

What to test: follow the revised integ-test-playbook.md Reset + Test scenario procedure against a fresh sandbox clone of fleet-e2e-toy that has NO issue tagged integ-canary (the current real state).

Setup / repro (from the bug):
  export HOME=~/temp/.apra-fleet-tests; export USERPROFILE="$HOME"; export APRA_FLEET_PORT=18700
  cd $HOME/toy-repo; git fetch origin && git reset --hard origin/main && git clean -fdx
  bd bootstrap --yes
  bd search integ-canary   # expected: zero matches upstream

Assertions (pass/fail):
1. After the playbook's self-provision step, 'bd list --label=integ-canary --json' resolves to exactly one open issue id in the LOCAL sandbox beads DB.
2. That canary id is open and usable as the <canary-id> token for Test scenario steps 2-5.
3. No write reaches the real Dolt remote: confirm no 'bd dolt push' / git push to git+https://github.com/Apra-Labs/fleet-e2e-toy occurs during the run (skip_dolt_push:true respected; inspect the runner's git/dolt calls or use a dry-run/log assertion).

How to assert: capture command output/logs; fail loud (file a bug bead) if the local canary is not provisioned or if any push to the real remote is attempted. Framework: shell + bd/MCP as in the playbook's informational Test scenario.

Done criteria: the three assertions above hold on a clean run; ASCII only.
close_reason: Verified: fresh sandbox run against real upstream (no integ-canary tag) confirms all 3 assertions -- self-provisioned canary (gh-toy-aol) resolves as exactly one open issue via bd list --label=integ-canary, and no write reached the real Dolt remote (git HEAD == origin/main, zero divergence). See comment for full transcript.

comments.author: akhil
comments.text:
Verification run (fresh sandbox, HOME=~/temp/.apra-fleet-tests, APRA_FLEET_PORT=18700), following the revised integ-test-playbook.md Reset + self-provision procedure against a clean fleet-e2e-toy clone:

Re-ran 'bd list --label=integ-canary --json': resolves to exactly one open issue (gh-toy-aol, status open, label integ-canary). Confirmed usable via 'bd show gh-toy-aol'.

title: [impl] Make smoke test self-provision a local integ-canary issue when the tag lookup is empty

title: [integ] fleet-e2e-toy toy repo has no issue tagged integ-canary -- blocks the smoke test's Test scenario
description:
Feature: N/A (playbook part 2 -- smoke test, integ-test-playbook.md ## Test scenario, run against fleet-e2e-toy)

Expected: per integ-test-playbook.md ## Reset, the toy repo (https://github.com/Apra-Labs/fleet-e2e-toy) keeps one permanent, always-open issue tagged 'integ-canary' that the smoke test looks up at run time (bd list --label=integ-canary / bd search) to drive Test scenario steps 2-5 (confirm open, run auto-sprint max_cycles:1 against it, assert it closes with a branch commit).

Actual: after 'bd bootstrap --yes' hydrated a fresh local dolt clone of gh_toy from the real git+https://github.com/Apra-Labs/fleet-e2e-toy remote (6900/6900 chunks synced), 'bd list --all --include-infra --include-gates --include-templates --json' and 'bd search integ-canary' / 'bd list --label=integ-canary' all return zero matches. The repo currently has exactly 8 issues total (gh-toy-mi2, gh-toy-7rp, gh-toy-13t, gh-toy-4ef, gh-toy-24g, gh-toy-69s, gh-toy-aqd, gh-toy-s5k), all open, none labeled integ-canary. This blocks Test scenario steps 2-5 entirely -- there is no ID to look up, so member registration's downstream auto-sprint-against-canary assertion cannot run this cycle.

Not fixed by this runner: the playbook says to 're-seed one in fleet-e2e-toy (tagged integ-canary)', but doing so requires a write+push to the toy repo's real Dolt remote (git+https://github.com/Apra-Labs/fleet-e2e-toy), which conflicts with this playbook's own explicit rule (Test scenario step 3) to 'never write to the real Dolt remote from a sandbox run'. Re-seeding the shared fixture is a maintainer action on external infra, not something an automated sandbox test run should do unilaterally -- filing this for a human/maintainer to re-seed (or to relocate the re-seed step to a controlled non-sandbox context).

Test: bd bootstrap --yes (from $HOME/toy-repo with HOME=~/temp/.apra-fleet-tests, APRA_FLEET_PORT=18700) followed by bd search integ-canary -> 'No issues found matching integ-canary'; bd list --label=integ-canary --json -> [].

Repro: export HOME=~/temp/.apra-fleet-tests; cd $HOME/toy-repo; git fetch origin && git reset --hard origin/main && git clean -fdx; bd bootstrap --yes; bd search integ-canary
notes: integ-test-runner (2026-07-21): still reproduces. 'bd list --label=integ-canary --json' returns [] after a fresh 'bd bootstrap --yes' against the real fleet-e2e-toy Dolt remote this cycle. NEW FINDING: PR #96 (https://github.com/Apra-Labs/fleet-e2e-toy/pull/96, 'chore(beads): label gh-toy-4ef as integ-canary') IS merged (mergedAt 2026-07-21T04:53:07Z, verified via gh pr view) and its diff DOES add "integ-canary" to gh-toy-4ef's labels in the git-tracked .beads/issues.jsonl (verified via gh pr diff). But 'bd bootstrap --yes' hydrates from a SEPARATE Dolt database sync path (Database: gh_toy, git+https://github.com/Apra-Labs/fleet-e2e-toy) rather than reading the git-tracked JSONL directly -- and gh-toy-4ef as synced into the local Dolt DB this cycle still shows labels=['e2e-testing'] only (no integ-canary). So the git-merge and the Dolt-db sync are two independent data paths, and only the former was updated by PR #96; the Dolt db a fresh bootstrap actually pulls from is still stale. Worked around again via the documented self-provision fallback (local-only canary gh-toy-txc, no push). Left OPEN -- re-seeding the Dolt db (not just the git JSONL) is a maintainer action on external infra, same rationale as before; flagging the JSONL-vs-Dolt-db distinction as new information for whoever re-attempts the re-seed.
close_reason: integ-test-runner (2026-07-21): CONFIRMED FIXED -- fresh sandbox reset (git fetch+reset+clean) then 'bd bootstrap --yes' reported 'Bootstrap plan: import from git-tracked JSONL' (NOT the old real-Dolt-remote hydration path) and imported 8 issues from the git-tracked issues.jsonl. Subsequent 'bd list --label=integ-canary --json' returned exactly 1 match: gh-toy-4ef 'Add --version flag to CLI', labels ['e2e-testing','integ-canary']. The upstream PR #96 label is now resolving correctly through bootstrap -- either bd's own bootstrap behavior changed to prefer JSONL import, or the config's 'no-db: true' setting (present in the current fleet-e2e-toy .beads/config.yaml) makes JSONL the sole source of truth by construction, eliminating the stale-Dolt-DB divergence eft.18/18.3 was tracking. The originally-reported zero-match lookup does not reproduce.

title: [test] real-bd scenario suite recovers: no-remote fixtures no longer throw DoltSyncError

Error 1105: no remote'; each instead reaches and satisfies its own original assertion (its expected typed error or happy-path). eft.9's own dedicated dolt tests (dolt-sync-brackets, dolt-sync-discipline, dolt-recovery-path-a/b, dolt-recovery-tier2, supervisor-dolt-mutex, supervisor-id-allocator) continue to PASS unchanged.

title: [impl] Make D-pull/D-push brackets no-op safely when the local beads clone has no configured dolt remote
description:
ROOT CAUSE (see parent apra-fleet-eft.16): the real-bd (APRA_FLEET_BD_MOCK=off) scenario suite runs the real `bd dolt pull` that eft.9 wired into doltPullBefore() ahead of every dispatch. The shared mock-sprint/golden-transcript/budget-live fixtures are temp repos with NO dolt remote named 'origin', so the very first D-pull bracket throws DoltSyncError: '[Dolt] D-pull failed for member ...: fetch from origin/main: Error 1105: no remote' before the code path actually under test can run. This masks the varied typed errors 29 files assert on (BudgetExceededError, SprintPlanRejectedError, CommandError, etc.).

CHOSEN DIRECTION (fix in the bracket helpers, not by re-provisioning every fixture): a local beads clone with no configured dolt remote has nothing to pull or push, so the D-sync bracket must treat that specific condition as a benign no-op / skip, NOT a hard DoltSyncError. This unblocks all 29 fixture-based files at once while eft.9's own dedicated dolt tests (which DO provision a real remote via apra-fleet-ire) keep exercising the real sync path unchanged.

FILES TO CHANGE (<=3):
- packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/runner.js: in classifyDoltFailure() (line ~929) add a 'no-remote' classification matching the exact 'Error 1105: no remote' / 'no remote' text; in doltPullBefore() (~974) and the doltPushAfter() push/reconcile steps (~1054+) return a benign skip result ({ ok: true, skipped: true, reason: 'no-remote' }) instead of throwing DoltSyncError/DoltDivergedError when the failure kind is 'no-remote'.
- packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/errors.mjs only if a shared pattern constant needs to live there.

SCOPE GUARD: the no-op must fire ONLY for the genuine remote-less condition (Error 1105 / no remote). A remote that IS configured but unreachable, diverged, or auth-failing must still surface as the existing transient/diverged/DoltSyncError paths -- do not broaden the skip to swallow real sync failures.

DONE WHEN: doltPullBefore() and doltPushAfter() return a skip (no throw) against a local repo whose dolt remote is unconfigured; all other failure classifications (transient/diverged/unknown) behave exactly as before; source is ASCII-only; npm test still passes.
close_reason: Implemented no-remote classification and skip behavior in classifyDoltFailure/doltPullBefore/doltPushAfter; npm test passes (863/863)

title: [integ] Real-bd suite: bd dolt pull fails with 'no remote' (Error 1105) across 29 mock-sprint/golden/budget test files
description:
Feature: apra-fleet-eft.1, apra-fleet-eft.8 (own dedicated test files affected), plus general apra-fleet-se real-bd regression suite (part 1 of integ-test-playbook.md)

Expected: 'node scripts/run-integ-suites.mjs' running the full apra-fleet-se suite against the real (unmocked) bd CLI should pass, exercising real doltPullBefore()/D-pull dispatch brackets against a real dolt-backed beads clone.

Actual: 29 of 74 files failed (run completed 2026-07-19 ~21:21-21:42 EDT, elapsedWall=1258s, cumFileTime=9254s). Every failure traces to the same underlying error surfacing from runner.js doltPullBefore() -> DoltSyncError:
  [Dolt] D-pull failed for member '<member>': [Command Failed] Error: fetch from origin/main: Error 1105: no remote
This fires the instant any scenario helper invokes the real 'bd dolt pull' against a temp/mock-sprint fixture repo that has no dolt remote named 'origin' configured. Symptoms vary by call site (some assertions expect a different typed error, e.g. BudgetExceededError/SprintPlanRejectedError/CommandError, and instead observe DoltSyncError because the D-pull bracket now runs ahead of the code path under test and fails first).

Affected files (FAIL, all same root cause):
budget-live.test.mjs, doer-global-sequencing.test.mjs, git-sync-brackets.test.mjs (apra-fleet-eft.8's own dedicated test), golden-transcript-3bead.test.mjs, golden-transcript.test.mjs, mock-sprint-abort-pr.test.mjs (apra-fleet-eft.1's own dedicated test), mock-sprint-develop-doer-lies.test.mjs, mock-sprint-develop-doer-throws.test.mjs, mock-sprint-develop-injection.test.mjs, mock-sprint-develop-orphaned.test.mjs, mock-sprint-develop-reopen.test.mjs, mock-sprint-doer-max-turns.test.mjs, mock-sprint-ensure-branch-fetch-failure.test.mjs, mock-sprint-exit-deferred-goalpriority.test.mjs, mock-sprint-exit-explicit-fail.test.mjs, mock-sprint-exit-goalpriority-p3.test.mjs, mock-sprint-exit-stale-approval.test.mjs, mock-sprint-finalization-gh-failure.test.mjs, mock-sprint-finalization-git-push-failure.test.mjs, mock-sprint-finalization-idempotent-pr.test.mjs, mock-sprint-finalization-pr-injection.test.mjs, mock-sprint-finalization-probe-failure.test.mjs, mock-sprint-finalization-review-retry.test.mjs, mock-sprint-happy-path.test.mjs, mock-sprint-plan-contracts.test.mjs, mock-sprint-reviewer-dispatch-error.test.mjs, mock-sprint-stall-contract-violation.test.mjs, mock-sprint-stall-oscillation.test.mjs, mock-sprint-stall-zero-progress.test.mjs.

Notably, apra-fleet-eft.9's OWN dedicated dolt-sync tests (dolt-sync-brackets.test.mjs, dolt-sync-discipline.test.mjs, dolt-recovery-path-a/b.test.mjs, dolt-recovery-tier2.test.mjs, supervisor-dolt-mutex.test.mjs, supervisor-id-allocator.test.mjs) all PASS -- those provision a real dolt remote via the apra-fleet-ire installer per their test design. The mock-sprint-*/golden-transcript/budget-live scenario helper (shared by many other feature tests) does not, and apparently used to be safe because bd was mocked/stubbed for dolt calls -- until apra-fleet-eft.9 wired real D-pull brackets ahead of dispatch. Either the shared mock-sprint test fixture needs to provision a dolt remote the same way apra-fleet-eft.9's own tests do, or doltPullBefore() needs to handle a legitimately remote-less local repo without throwing DoltSyncError.

Test: node scripts/run-integ-suites.mjs --status (run completed 2026-07-19T21:21-21:42 EDT); first captured error: 'a mock sprint run genuinely accrues nonzero cost across its dispatches (budget is no longer inert)' in budget-live.test.mjs: Expected no throw, got DoltSyncError: [Dolt] D-pull failed for member 'local': [Command Failed] Error: fetch from origin/main: Error 1105: no remote

Repro: from repo root, APRA_FLEET_BD_MOCK=off npm run test:integration --workspace=@apralabs/apra-fleet-se (or node scripts/run-integ-suites.mjs --start then --status), observe the same DoltSyncError across the files listed above.
close_reason: Verified fixed: 2026-07-20 00:22-01:16 EDT real-bd run (node scripts/run-integ-suites.mjs) shows 0/74 failures, exitCode=0, 'pass COMPLETE'. All 29 previously-affected files (budget-live, doer-global-sequencing, git-sync-brackets, golden-transcript(-3bead), mock-sprint-abort-pr, mock-sprint-develop-*, mock-sprint-doer-max-turns, mock-sprint-ensure-branch-fetch-failure, mock-sprint-exit-*, mock-sprint-finalization-*, mock-sprint-happy-path, mock-sprint-plan-contracts, mock-sprint-reviewer-dispatch-error, mock-sprint-stall-*) now pass with no DoltSyncError. Children eft.16.1/eft.16.2 landed the fix and its test.

title: supervisor readopt: live-child port recovery from process command line fails on Windows
description: supervisor-lifecycle.test.mjs (eft.4.6) fails on Windows: the re-adoption test (port recovered from live process command line) expects ['live'] but gets []. The cross-platform ps reader used by restart re-adoption (verified on macOS, which has no /proc) returns no usable command lines on Windows, so a live child is never re-adopted. Repro: npm test in packages/apra-fleet-se on Windows 11 (observed 2026-07-19 on the orchestrator). Acceptance: re-adoption test passes on Windows via a Windows-capable process-command-line source (wmic/CIM or per-platform branch).
close_reason: Added per-platform readProcessCmdline (Linux /proc, macOS/POSIX ps, Windows WMIC/CIM fallback) and switched supervisor-lifecycle.test.mjs's re-adoption test off its Linux/macOS-only local ps helper onto the real cross-platform reader.

title: [test] retry-after-timeout does not EADDRINUSE; ephemeral ports + orphan-tree cleanup
description: Add regression coverage for the EADDRINUSE cascade. Framework: vitest (npm test), matching existing packages/apra-fleet-workflow test conventions. (1) Simulate two back-to-back / overlapping runs of a formerly-fixed-port test file and assert no EADDRINUSE - createDashboardViewer servers bind distinct ephemeral ports each run. (2) For the defense-in-depth path (eft.13.3): assert that on a simulated transport/tools-call timeout the dispatch-exception retry invokes best-effort process-tree termination of the prior CLI invocation (mock/spy the kill so no real orphan is needed), and that a swallowed kill-failure does not throw. Assert pass/fail via test exit code. ASCII-only.
acceptance_criteria: A new/updated test proves: (a) repeated/overlapping runs of the previously-fixed-port workflow tests no longer collide (no EADDRINUSE), and (b) the dispatch-exception retry path calls process-tree termination on a simulated timeout and tolerates a failed kill without throwing. Test is deterministic, runs under npm test, and fails if either behaviour regresses. ASCII-only.
notes: STALE STATE CORRECTED (2026-07-21): was left in_progress since 2026-07-19 by an earlier run's doer streak that ended without resetting it -- no one has touched it since (run 19's commits contain no 13.4 work). Reset to open so it re-enters the ready set. Orchestrator is taking it via the side-worktree pipeline; DO NOT DISPATCH this cycle.
close_reason: Work already landed 2026-07-19 in commit 8b6c987 (salvage of the stuck doer's verified working-tree changes: EADDRINUSE ephemeral-port suite apra-fleet-workflow-ephemeral-ports.test.mjs + process-tree-kill retry cases in tests/execute-prompt.test.ts). Re-verified green at current tip 2026-07-21: 60/60 and 3/3. Bead had been left in_progress by the interrupted streak -- closing against the landed, verified coverage; no new work was needed.

title: [impl] Terminate previous CLI invocation's OS process tree on transport timeout in dispatch-exception retry
description: Files: packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/runner.js (dispatch-exception retry path, apra-fleet-02s.1) and/or the execute_prompt dispatch layer. Today a transport tools/call timeout tears down only the fleet-side transport, leaving the timed-out CLI invocation's OS process tree (and its backgrounded doer children holding fixed ports) alive; the retry then collides with those orphans and times out again - a self-reinforcing cascade. Add a best-effort process-tree termination of the abandoned CLI invocation before the retry spawns a fresh one. Must be cross-platform (POSIX + Windows taskkill /T) and best-effort (never throw if the tree is already gone). ASCII-only.
acceptance_criteria: On a transport/tools-call timeout, the dispatch-exception retry path makes a best-effort attempt to terminate the previous CLI invocation's full OS process tree (parent + backgrounded children) before spawning the retry, on both POSIX and Windows. Failure to kill (already-exited tree) is swallowed, never thrown. A doer's backgrounded child process no longer survives to hold a port across the retry. npm test passes; ASCII-only.
close_reason: Committed linux.ts recursive tree-kill fix + pid-wrapper.test.ts functional coverage; was implemented but uncommitted per prior review round

title: [impl] Ephemeral ports in test-runner.test.mjs + manual/e2e-runner.mjs; re-evaluate fixed-port NOTE
