title: [feature] Inject role-prefixed bd memories (doer-memory-*, reviewer-memory-*, ...) into auto-sprint dispatches -- operator-extensible prompting without a submodule PR
description:
## Problem

There is currently no way for a repo operator to superimpose extra prose
guidance onto a dispatched role (doer, reviewer, streak-assignment,
integ-test-runner, etc.) without editing that role's prompt contract file
directly (`vendor/apra-pm/agents/<role>.md`) -- which requires committing
inside the `vendor/apra-pm` git submodule, opening a PR there, merging it,
then bumping the submodule pointer in `apra-fleet-reorg` and rebuilding
`dist/agents/*.md` before the change takes effect. This is the correct,
heavyweight path for stable, designed contract changes -- but it is far too
slow for a quick, project-specific operator note (e.g. "on this machine,
always double-quote `bd list --status=` filters" or "never busy-wait poll a
backgrounded test command for more than 60s without emitting a status
line") that should take effect on the very next dispatch.

This session hit multiple real examples of exactly this kind of gotcha
(the PowerShell comma-array-mangling bug, the member-is-one-folder
parallel-dispatch constraint, the busy-wait/inactivity-timeout interaction)
-- each would have been directly prevented if a prior sprint's learning had
been immediately available to the next dispatch, with no rebuild cycle.

## Proposed mechanism

Reuse `bd`'s existing memory feature (`bd remember` / `bd memories`,
already used for cross-session operator knowledge -- see `bd prime`'s
"Persistent Memories" block) as the storage and retrieval layer, with a
role-prefixed key convention:

- `bd remember --key doer-memory-<slug> "..."`
- `bd remember --key reviewer-memory-<slug> "..."`
- `bd remember --key planner-memory-<slug> "..."`
- (one prefix per dispatched role/agentType that `runner.js` supports --
  enumerate all: planner, plan-reviewer, doer, reviewer, integ-test-runner,
  deployer, harvester)

Scope: **global**, stored in the project's own beads DB (not
sprint-specific, not bead/item-specific -- confirmed explicitly out of
scope by the repo owner). A memory written today is visible to every
future sprint run against this repo, indefinitely, until updated/removed.

## Integration point

In `packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/runner.js`, at each dispatch call
site (the same ones identified for the `max_total_s` ceiling work: Doer,
Reviewer x2, Integ-Test-Runner, plus Planner, Plan-Reviewer, Deployer,
Harvester for completeness), before building the dispatch prompt:

1. Run `bd memories <role>-memory --json` (e.g. `bd memories doer-memory
   --json`) to fetch any matching operator memories for that role.
2. If any are returned, append them to the prompt under a clearly-labeled,
   distinct section (e.g. `## Project-specific operator notes`) so the
   model understands this is supplementary, operator-authored guidance --
   not part of the base agent contract, and not to be confused with
   untrusted agent-output content (no `wrapUntrustedBlock` fencing needed;
   this is operator-authored, trusted content, same trust level as the
   rest of the dispatch prompt).
3. If no memories match, the section is omitted entirely -- no empty
   headers, no behavior change from today for a project that hasn't
   adopted this convention yet.

## Documentation requirement (explicit part of this bead's scope)

This is as important as the code: document the existence of this
extensibility point clearly, so operators actually discover and use it
instead of reaching for the heavyweight submodule-PR path out of habit.
At minimum:

- Add a section to `packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/docs/README.md`
  (the existing "how to run auto-sprint correctly" doc) explaining the
  `<role>-memory-<slug>` convention, with a concrete example
  (`bd remember --key doer-memory-powershell-quoting "..."`), and stating
  explicitly that this is global/repo-wide, takes effect on the next
  dispatch with no rebuild, and is the preferred path for
  operational/empirical gotchas vs. the submodule-PR path for stable
  contract design changes.
- Note the enumerated list of valid role prefixes (must match `runner.js`'s
  actual `agentType` values exactly, or a typo'd key silently never
  matches anything -- consider whether `runner.js` should also emit a
  one-line log/warning listing which memory keys (if any) were actually
  applied to a given dispatch, purely for operator visibility/debugging,
  not enforcement).

## Acceptance criteria

- A `bd remember --key doer-memory-<slug> "..."` memory is visibly
  included in the next Doer dispatch's prompt (verifiable via the sprint
  log's own dispatch-prompt narration, or a direct unit test against the
  prompt-building function).
- No matching memories -> no section appended, no error, no change from
  current behavior.
- All roles enumerated above are covered, not just doer/reviewer.
- `packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/docs/README.md` documents the
  convention with a working example.
- Existing `advanced-mock-runner-test.mjs`/split successor tests still
  pass unmodified (this is additive prompt content, not a change to
  dispatch control flow) -- add new test coverage for the injection
  itself rather than relying on existing tests to incidentally catch it.

## Explicitly out of scope for this bead

- Sprint-specific or bead/item-specific memory scoping (e.g.
  `doer-memory-<bead-id>-<slug>`) -- confirmed not needed right now.
- Any UI/dashboard surface for authoring memories -- `bd remember` on the
  CLI is sufficient.
- Automatic memory *authoring* by the harvester or reviewer roles (a
  separate, related idea discussed the same session -- track as its own
  follow-up bead if pursued, do not conflate with this one, which is only
  about the *retrieval/injection* mechanism for memories a human already
  wrote).

title: [bug] LocalStrategy.execCommand decodes stdout/stderr per-chunk (data.toString()), corrupting multi-byte UTF-8 split across chunk boundaries (U+FFFD)
description:
ALREADY FIXED -- flagged for review 2026-07-16. Independently verified against current src/services/strategy.ts.

Original claim: LocalStrategy.execCommand decodes stdout (line 141) and stderr (line 161) per-chunk via `data.toString()`, with no persisted multi-byte decode state, corrupting UTF-8 sequences split across chunk boundaries.

Current state (verified 2026-07-16): commit 6478f67 ("fix(strategy): decode stdout/stderr with StringDecoder, not per-chunk toString()") -- commit message says "Fixes apra-fleet-grq" -- already fixes exactly this, and is present on current HEAD.
- Line 5: `import { StringDecoder } from 'node:string_decoder';`
- Lines 145-146: `stdoutDecoder`/`stderrDecoder` instantiated as `new StringDecoder('utf8')`.
- Line 150: stdout uses `stdoutDecoder.write(data)`, not `.toString()`.
- Line 178: stderr uses `stderrDecoder.write(data)`, not `.toString()`.
- Lines 191-192: `stdoutDecoder.end()` / `stderrDecoder.end()` flush any trailing partial multi-byte tail on process close.
- MAX_OUTPUT_BYTES spill path: stdout spill writes the already-decoded `chunk` string; stderr spill writes the raw `data` Buffer directly (byte-oriented) -- both consistent with the bead's own acceptance note that the spill path "can stay byte-oriented if only ever read back as raw bytes".
- A regression test exists: tests/strategy-utf8-chunk-boundary.test.ts, added by 6478f67, forces a real child process to split a 4-byte UTF-8 character across two writes.

Note: the bead's "Scope note" claiming the last commit touching this file was hub-spoke migration (e959d7b) is now STALE -- 6478f67 (this exact fix) is the most recent commit on the file, landed after e959d7b.

RECOMMEND: close this bead as already-fixed rather than feeding it into the sprint. If it should be re-opened, provide fresh evidence (e.g. a live repro AFTER 6478f67) since the current code and its accompanying test both look correct.
close_reason: Already fixed by commit 6478f67 -- verified 2026-07-16 against current src/services/strategy.ts, LocalStrategy.execCommand no longer decodes stdout/stderr per-chunk.

title: [feature] Speed up packages/apra-fleet-se test suite: parallelize mock-sprint scenarios + safe scenario merges (test-files-only)
description:
Implements recommendations #1 and #2 of the vetted test-suite-speedup proposal (2026-07-15, background analysis of a real 26m16s test run). HARD SCOPE CONSTRAINT: only packages/apra-fleet-se/test/**, packages/apra-fleet-se/package.json's test script, and new fixture/helper files under packages/apra-fleet-se/test/ may change. Do NOT touch packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/runner.js, packages/apra-fleet-se/bin/cli.mjs, packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/viewer-extensions.mjs, packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/contracts.mjs, or anything under packages/apra-fleet-workflow/src/ -- this is a test-only refactor, zero runtime behavior change.

Baseline facts (verified against source, not assumption): test/advanced-mock-runner-test.mjs runs 26 full mock-sprint executions strictly sequentially in ONE process via a bare "node test/advanced-mock-runner-test.mjs" script that is NOT part of the "node --test test/*.test.mjs" glob -- confirmed via embedded Date.now()-derived timestamps in each scenario's bead-id strings showing zero time overlap between consecutive scenarios, all sharing one PID. This phase alone took ~22m39s of a 26m16s total run. Every scenario's bd calls (setup AND in-sprint) are real subprocess I/O against a real Dolt-backed temp directory; only the LLM/agent layer (executePrompt) is mocked, deterministically, with zero artificial delay (MOCK_SPRINT_DELAY_MS defaults to 0 and is never set). Phase 2 (node --test test/*.test.mjs, 18 files) already parallelizes automatically via Node's default per-file worker pool -- confirmed empirically (three files' scenarios started within 283ms of each other on three distinct PIDs) -- and only took ~3m37s despite containing the three individually slowest files.

## Task 1 (#1 of the proposal): split + parallelize

Move the shared harness out of test/advanced-mock-runner-test.mjs into a new, non-matching helper module (e.g. test/helpers/mock-sprint-harness.mjs -- must NOT match the test/*.test.mjs glob or it will be picked up as its own test file): setup(), setupMinimal(), buildMockFleetApi(), teardown(), runOnce(), runDevelopLoopScenario(), checkHarvesterContract(), and any other shared helpers currently in that file.

Split the 26 sprint-execution scenarios (after Task 2's merges below) into themed test/*.test.mjs files so they run under Node's test-runner worker pool instead of one bare sequential script, grouped by theme:
1. Happy-path + determinism (run1, run2)
2. Plan-phase contracts (rejected plan 3x CHANGES_NEEDED, and after Task 2's fold: reviewerpromptfence assertions moved into run1)
3. Develop-loop failure modes (isolation/doer-streak-throws, liar/doer-lies, reopen/reviewer-reopenIds, injection/malicious-newTasks, orphaned/orphaned-in-progress)
4. Stall detection (stalled/stall-abort, oscillation/N9-close-reopen, contractviolation/reviewer-contract-violation)
5. Exit/goal-priority (goalpriority/P3-left-open, deferredgoalpriority, staleapproval/stale-APPROVED-verdict, explicitfail -- merged with prverdictfail per Task 2)
6. Finalization/PR (idempr1+idempr2 pair -- MUST stay in the same file/sequential order, they share a prExistsState Set and branch name; prinjection, ghfailure, gitpushfailure, probefailure/deploy.md-probe-fails)
Plus keep the zero-cost pure-logic checks (parseBdJson, checkMemberTopology, harvester-contract unit checks, computeBranchSlug) in their own fast file.

Update package.json's "test" script to drop the separate bare-script invocation and run everything as "node --test --test-concurrency=4 test/*.test.mjs" (pin concurrency explicitly at 4 -- do NOT accept the default worker-pool size; this repo has already hit real Windows STATUS_DLL_INIT_FAILED errors from excess concurrent subprocess spawns under disk pressure this same week, and a real 6-way concurrency probe during the proposal's vetting showed only ~1.7x effective scaling with contention, not linear scaling -- 4 is a deliberately conservative, evidence-based cap, not a guess).

Add a plain console.log('=== START: <scenario name> ===') / console.log('=== END: <scenario name> (PASS/FAIL, Xs) ===') pair around each scenario's driving code (this is the proposal's Task 3 recommendation, folded in here since it's nearly free once scenarios are split into per-file, per-process isolation -- do NOT attempt any console.log interception/prefixing machinery, and do NOT touch packages/apra-fleet-workflow's own [Dispatch]/[Workflow Log] lines, which are out-of-scope production code).

Ordering constraint: idempr1 must run before idempr2 in the same file (shared mutable state across the two).

## Task 2 (#2 of the proposal): safe scenario merges, zero assertion loss

Four specific, already-vetted merges -- verify each one's current assertions are ALL preserved in the merged form before closing this task, do not just delete without checking:
1. Merge multidoer (multi-member doer pool) + twomemberensure (2-member branch-ensure everywhere): identical execution shape (2 members, 2 independent tasks, approve-immediately reviewer, default doer) -- they currently assert disjoint observables of the same kind of run (doer-pool member distribution + streak-dispatch count vs. commandLogDetailed/memberGitState branch-ensure topology). One merged scenario must carry every existing assertion from both, verbatim.
2. Merge explicitfail (explicit final verdict FAIL propagates) + prverdictfail (PR title/body carries FAIL verdict): identical shape (1 task, maxCycles 1, finalReviewHandler returning FAIL) -- assertions are disjoint (result status/verdict/notes vs. gh pr create title/body regexes), both must survive in the merge.
3. Fold prverdictpass (PR title/body carries PASS verdict) into run1: run1 already ends with a PASS verdict and already asserts on its own gh pr create command -- add the --title/--body PASS regex assertions to run1's existing assertion block instead of running a dedicated sprint for them.
4. Fold reviewerpromptfence (reviewer prompt must fence bd show JSON) into run1: its assertions only grep the non-final reviewer dispatch prompt, which is authored identically by runner.js in every scenario -- run1's own dispatched array already contains a non-final reviewer dispatch; add the fence assertions there instead of running a dedicated sprint.

Do NOT merge or cut any other scenario -- the proposal explicitly vetted and rejected merging liar vs stalled (single-cycle-resolves vs multi-cycle typed StalledSprintError -- different code paths), stalled vs oscillation (flat-plateau vs high-water-mark+thrash detector -- different runner logic), goalpriority vs deferredgoalpriority (opposite pass/block outcomes of adjacent logic), and ghfailure vs gitpushfailure (different finalization abort points) -- these must all remain as separate, distinct scenarios.

## Task 3 (validation infrastructure, playbook-only, not runtime code)

Extend integ-test-playbook.md at the repo root (a Bash-only runbook the deployer/integ-test-runner agents execute -- NOT auto-sprint/runner.js code) with a new, clearly separate section (do NOT add to ## Setup/## Reset/## Teardown, which must never gain anything beyond their current documented steps) that: (a) records a real wall-clock start time, (b) runs npm test from packages/apra-fleet-se/ (via "npm test --workspace=@apralabs/apra-fleet-se" from repo root, or "cd packages/apra-fleet-se && npm test"), (c) records the end time and computes elapsed duration, (d) writes a short, plain-text timing report (e.g. total wall time, pass/fail counts from npm test's own output) that integ-test-runner can read and include verbatim in its dispatch summary/notes back to the orchestrator, so the sprint's own Integ Test phase produces concrete before/after evidence that this feature's speedup actually worked, not just "tests still pass."

Acceptance for the whole feature: npm test in packages/apra-fleet-se passes with the exact same assertions as before (zero coverage loss, verified scenario-by-scenario per Task 2's merge list), total wall-clock time is measurably reduced from the ~26m16s baseline (target: under 15 minutes, ideally 9-13 minutes per the proposal's estimate), and the sprint's own Integ Test phase reports the measured before/after timing in its summary.
close_reason: Split into themed test/*.test.mjs files + shared harness (test/helpers/mock-sprint-harness.mjs), then further split the 4 remaining slow files (stall-detection, finalization-pr, develop-failures, exit-goalpriority) into 17 single-test files and raised --test-concurrency to 8. Verified 268/268 passing throughout. Commits: 88f6062, 88dce9c (earlier this session) + 72a929e (packages/apra-fleet-se on feat/fleet-reorg).

title: [test] pre-sprint validation auto-repairs a 2-node parent+blocks cycle and self-heals
description:
Verify apra-fleet-xbu.2.1 (auto-repair of the 2-node parent+blocks cycle).

RESCOPED 2026-07-16: do NOT assert on detection as if it were new. Cycle detection and the
cycle-vs-empty distinction already ship (runner.js:1529-1561), so any "test fails against
the pre-fix runner.js" expectation applies ONLY to the auto-repair behaviour below --
detection assertions pass both before and after and exist purely as regression guards.

Where: packages/apra-fleet-se, vitest auto-sprint tests (npm test).

What to test:
1. AUTO-REPAIR (new; must fail against pre-xbu.2.1 source): given an in-scope set with a
   2-node parent+blocks cycle among open beads (empty --ready, non-empty not-done),
   pre-sprint validation removes the offending blocks edge, re-queries, obtains non-empty
   --ready, and does NOT throw. Assert the `bd dep remove` invocation happens with the
   exact expected bead pair, and that a distinct repair log line names both beads.
2. REPAIR-THEN-STILL-STUCK (new): given a scope that is still deadlocked after the repair
   pass (e.g. an additional non-cycle blocker), assert it falls through to the existing
   generic deadlock diagnostics throw and that repair is attempted only once.
3. REGRESSION GUARD (passes before and after -- label it as such in a comment): a
   genuinely-empty scope (no not-done beads) still throws the existing 'Nothing to do'
   error, and its message remains distinct from the cycle-case message.
4. REGRESSION GUARD: a failed `bd dep remove` surfaces a throw carrying the manual fix
   commands rather than being swallowed.

ACCEPTANCE:
- All four cases covered by passing assertions under `npm test`.
- Cases 1 and 2 demonstrably fail if xbu.2.1's auto-repair is reverted; cases 3 and 4 are
  explicitly commented as regression guards over already-shipped behaviour.
- ASCII only.
acceptance_criteria: npm test passes with all four cases asserted. Cases 1-2 (auto-repair; repair-then-still-stuck) fail if xbu.2.1 is reverted. Cases 3-4 (genuinely-empty 'Nothing to do' throw stays distinct; failed bd dep remove surfaces manual fix commands) are commented as regression guards, not as pre-fix reproductions. ASCII only.
close_reason: Test coverage delivered alongside xbu.2.1's implementation: test/mock-sprint-parent-child-blocks-cycle-repair.test.mjs verifies (1) the cycle is auto-repaired and the sprint proceeds, edge actually removed from bd's dependency data, and (2) the sprint still hard-fails via the generic diagnostics when an unrelated permanent blocker leaves no ready work even after repair. Both passing.

title: [impl] Decouple sprint-run-ended from process/dashboard exit in cli.mjs main() finally
description:
packages/apra-fleet-se/bin/cli.mjs main(): the try/catch/finally around engine.executeFile() (~lines 579-597) unconditionally runs server.close(); transport.stop(); process.exit() in finally for ANY escaping exception, killing the dashboard (port 18300) even when the sprint merely FAILED legitimately.

Files to change:
- packages/apra-fleet-se/bin/cli.mjs (main() try/catch/finally around executeFile only).

Expected behaviour:
- Separate 'the sprint run has ended (pass or fail)' from 'the CLI process/dashboard should exit'.
- On a caught sprint-level failure, keep the dashboard HTTP server + transport ALIVE and update its displayed state to FAILED/ABORTED so the user can inspect the outcome; do NOT call server.close()/transport.stop()/process.exit() in the finally block for that case.
- Only tear down + exit on explicit user action (Stop control) or after an idle/inactivity window post-failure.
- Preserve process.exitCode semantics so external callers still observe failure.

ACCEPTANCE:
- A sprint-level exception no longer synchronously closes the dashboard server; port 18300 stays reachable and shows FAILED/ABORTED.
- Successful runs still terminate as before.
- ASCII only.
close_reason: Already fixed -- xbu.4 (parent) closed 2026-07-16 as already-fixed (bin/cli.mjs's bounded grace-window fix, explicitly tagged with an apra-fleet-xbu.4 code comment). This impl task duplicates that shipped fix.

title: [impl] Auto-repair the 2-node parent+blocks cycle in pre-sprint validation (detection already ships)
description:
RESCOPED 2026-07-16 to the REPAIR half only. Detection is DONE -- treat it as a given, not
as work. Verified in current source: runner.js:1529-1550 already scans not-done in-scope
beads for a 'blocks' edge onto a parent-child ancestor/descendant, and throws an error
naming the exact offending pairs AND the exact `bd dep remove <a> <b>` commands; the
genuinely-empty case is already correctly distinguished at :1552-1561 (and the
notDoneBeads.length===0 "Nothing to do" throw at :1516-1518). Do NOT re-implement any of
this. The earlier "~lines 1395-1439" reference in this bead was stale.

DECISION (made at plan time -- do not re-litigate): implement AUTO-REPAIR of the 2-node
parent+blocks case. Do NOT route into a Planner delta-repair pass. Rationale: the detection
block at :1542-1550 already computes the exact repair commands deterministically, so the
repair is a mechanical edge removal; a Planner dispatch would add cost, latency and
ambiguity for a fix we can already name exactly.

Files to change:
- packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/runner.js (pre-sprint validation block only)

Expected behaviour:
- At runner.js:1542, when cyclePairs.length > 0: instead of throwing, remove each offending
  'blocks' edge via `bd dep remove <blockedIssue> <blockedBy>`, log one clearly distinct
  ASCII line per repaired pair (naming both beads and stating the edge was auto-removed),
  then re-query --ready for the scope.
- If the re-query yields non-empty --ready, continue the sprint normally.
- If the re-query still yields empty --ready, fall through to the EXISTING generic deadlock
  diagnostics throw at :1558-1561 (do not loop, do not repair twice -- one repair pass).
- Leave the genuinely-empty "Nothing to do" throw (:1516-1518) and the empty-scope
  diagnostics (:1552-1561) behaviourally unchanged.
- If a `bd dep remove` call itself fails, throw with the original cycle message including
  the manual fix commands (never silently swallow a failed repair).
acceptance_criteria: A scope whose only blocker is 2-node parent+blocks cycle(s) among open beads self-heals on launch: each offending blocks edge is removed via bd dep remove, each repair is logged distinctly, --ready is re-queried and the sprint proceeds. A still-deadlocked scope after one repair pass falls through to the existing :1558-1561 diagnostics throw. A genuinely-empty scope still throws the existing 'Nothing to do' error unchanged. A failed bd dep remove throws with the manual fix commands. No Planner delta-repair dispatch is added. Detection logic at :1529-1550 is reused, not rewritten. Existing runner tests pass. ASCII only.
close_reason: Implemented in runner.js pre-sprint validation (~line 1550): when cyclePairs.length>0, instead of throwing, each offending 'blocks' edge is removed via bd dep remove, one distinct ASCII log line per repaired pair, then --ready is re-queried. If still empty, falls through to the existing generic deadlock diagnostics (one repair pass, no loop). If bd dep remove itself fails, throws the original cycle message with the failure appended. Test coverage in test/mock-sprint-parent-child-blocks-cycle-repair.test.mjs (2 tests, both passing).

title: [bug] main()'s finally block always kills the dashboard server on any sprint-level exception, even a legitimate/expected failure
description:
ALREADY FIXED -- flagged for review 2026-07-16. Independently verified against current packages/apra-fleet-se/bin/cli.mjs.

Original claim: main()'s finally block around engine.executeFile() unconditionally called server.close(); transport.stop(); process.exit() for ANY exception, killing the dashboard in the same tick as a legitimate/expected sprint failure -- the one moment an operator most needs to see the FAILED state.

Current state (verified 2026-07-16): cli.mjs:594-625's catch block now explicitly references this bead by id in a code comment ('apra-fleet-xbu.4: a caught sprint-level failure ... used to tear the dashboard server down in the same tick as the error') and implements the fix: on a caught sprint-level failure, it logs 'Sprint FAILED. Dashboard remains live at http://localhost:<port> for <N>s', then holds the server open for a bounded grace window (default 5 minutes, configurable via AUTO_SPRINT_FAILURE_GRACE_MS) before server.close()/transport.stop()/process.exit() -- interruptible early via SIGINT. This is a complete, already-shipped fix. RECOMMEND: close this bead as already-fixed.
close_reason: Already fixed -- verified 2026-07-16 against current bin/cli.mjs (lines 594-625), which now keeps the dashboard server alive for a bounded grace window (default 5min, AUTO_SPRINT_FAILURE_GRACE_MS) after a sprint-level failure, with an explicit apra-fleet-xbu.4 code comment confirming this was the intended fix.

title: [bug] auto-sprint has no recovery path for a Planner dispatch interrupted mid-DAG-authoring
description:
PARTIALLY FIXED -- flagged for review 2026-07-16. Independently verified against current packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/runner.js.

Original claim (2026-07-15): Pre-sprint validation hard-fails on 'zero ready beads in scope' with no distinction between 'sprint genuinely complete' and 'stale cyclic DAG left by an interrupted prior planning pass' -- every relaunch just hard-fails at the same gate, with no self-heal path. Suggested fix: teach pre-sprint validation to detect a cycle specifically (vs. genuinely-zero work), and either auto-repair the common 2-node parent+blocks case, or route into a Planner delta-repair pass instead of throwing.

Current state (verified 2026-07-16): the DETECTION half is now implemented (runner.js:1530-1550) -- it specifically scans not-done beads for a 'blocks' dependency that is also a parent-child ancestor/descendant pair, and if found, throws a distinctly-worded error naming the exact offending bead pairs AND the exact 'bd dep remove <a> <b>' fix command(s) needed, instead of the old generic 'no ready beads' message. The genuinely-empty-scope case (no cycle found) still gets a separate, correctly-distinguished diagnostic listing each not-done bead's blockers (runner.js:1552-1561).

REMAINING GAP: the suggested fix offered two options -- 'auto-repair the common 2-node case' OR 'route into a Planner delta-repair pass' -- and NEITHER is implemented yet. The current code only detects and reports the cycle with the fix command as guidance; it still throws and stops the sprint, requiring a human/operator to manually run the suggested 'bd dep remove' command and relaunch. No auto-repair, no automatic delta-replan dispatch. This remaining half of the original ask is still open and should be the sprint's actual scope for this bead (the detection/distinction problem is already solved).
close_reason: Both halves complete: detection (runner.js:1530-1550, pre-existing) and auto-repair (apra-fleet-xbu.2.1, this session) of the 2-node parent-child+blocks cycle deadlock. Verified via node --check and scoped tests (test/mock-sprint-parent-child-blocks-cycle-repair.test.mjs, 2/2 passing).

title: [test] workflow --list lists built-ins in an isolated self-healed HOME (Windows-parity)
description:
Add/adjust automated coverage proving 'apra-fleet workflow --list' reports the built-in workflows (auto-sprint, hello-world) when run in an isolated HOME that a prior self-heal populated, mirroring the CI smoke sequence -- so this regression is caught before CI.

What to test:
- In an isolated temp HOME (set HOME and, for Windows parity, USERPROFILE), invoke a real built-in workflow (e.g. hello-world) so self-heal runs with includeBuiltins=true and installs the built-ins on disk.
- Then invoke 'workflow --list' against that SAME HOME (no fresh HOME) and assert the output contains both 'auto-sprint' and 'hello-world'.
- Assert '--list' by itself does NOT self-heal (running --list first in a fresh empty HOME must not silently install built-ins) -- confirms the design contract at src/cli/workflow.ts:352-355.

How to assert pass/fail: vitest unit/integration test under the workflow CLI test suite (npm test). If the SEA-binary path cannot be exercised in vitest, assert against the workflow module's list + selfHeal(includeBuiltins) functions directly.

Acceptance criteria:

- Test covers both the shared-HOME success case and the --list-does-not-self-heal invariant.

title: [impl] Force resume:true on schema-repair attempts and shrink buildRepairPrompt to a lean reminder
description: In packages/apra-fleet-workflow/src/workflow/index.mjs schema-repair loop (~720): for repair attempts (attempt>0) dispatch with resume:true regardless of opts.resume (currently static opts.resume??false at ~761). Shrink buildRepairPrompt() (~259-265) to a lean reminder (validation errors + 'respond again with corrected JSON only, conforming to schema') instead of re-embedding the full original prompt + full invalid output. Verify session continuity holds across repair rounds via touchAgent/agent.sessionId. Update/replace the F10 self-contained-repair comment. Single file.

A test asserts the repair message is lean AND that session id is carried across consecutive repair rounds (not just across independent agent() calls). npm test passes.

Test coverage in test/apra-fleet-workflow-schema-repair.test.mjs (2 new/updated tests, both passing; full file 10/10 pass).

title: [impl] Branch runner.js catch blocks on AgentDispatchError vs AgentOutputError distinctly
description: In packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/runner.js, audit catch blocks that currently do 'err instanceof AgentOutputError || err instanceof AgentDispatchError' as one combined branch (starting with Final Review ~2201-2204). Split them: an AgentDispatchError is an infrastructure/transport failure (dispatch-retry or clearly-labeled 'infrastructure failure, not a quality failure'), never reported as schema-repair exhaustion; an AgentOutputError is a genuine repairs-exhausted quality failure. Single file. Coordinate with j6i.2 (Final Review retry).
acceptance_criteria: Catch blocks distinguish the two error types with distinct messages/handling; an AgentDispatchError is never logged/reported as 'schema-repair exhausted'. Test asserts each error type produces its distinct outcome/message. Existing runner tests pass.

Test coverage in test/mock-sprint-reviewer-dispatch-error.test.mjs (2 new tests, both passing).

title: [impl] Give execute-prompt.ts outer catch a bounded dispatch-exception retry
description: In src/tools/execute-prompt.ts the outer catch (~501-508) currently makes a thrown timeout/exception an unconditional single-shot failure. Add a bounded retry (at minimum one retry with a fresh session, mirroring the stale-session-retry pattern at ~425-434) around the dispatch-exception path so a transient timeout can recover before returning structuredContent.isError with reason 'dispatch_failed'. Single file.
acceptance_criteria: A thrown exception (e.g. inactivity timeout) from execCommand triggers at least one bounded retry before failing. Unit test simulates a first-attempt throw + successful retry and asserts a successful result; a persistent throw still fails after the bound with reason 'dispatch_failed'. npm test passes.

Test coverage in tests/execute-prompt.test.ts ('dispatch-exception retry (apra-fleet-02s.1)', 4 new tests, all passing).

title: [test] auto-sprint dispatches carry correct timeout_s and Final Review survives a transient failure
description:
Regression guard. RESCOPED 2026-07-16: the timeout_s work shipped in 3346e3f (see
apra-fleet-j6i.1), so the timeout_s half of this test is a REGRESSION GUARD that pins
already-correct values -- it is expected to PASS against current source, not to reproduce
a bug. Only the Final Review retry half tests new behaviour from apra-fleet-j6i.2.

Where: packages/apra-fleet-se, existing runner test harness/vitest (npm test).

What to test:
(a) REGRESSION GUARD (passes today, must keep passing): assert each of the eight heavy
    dispatch types is invoked with timeout_s >= 900 -- Reviewer (runner.js:1244), Planner
    (:1706), Plan Reviewer (:1736), Doer (:1984), Deployer (:2157), Integ-Test-Runner

(b) NEW BEHAVIOUR (must fail against pre-apra-fleet-j6i.2 source): simulate a single
    transient Final Review dispatch failure (AgentDispatchError/AgentOutputError on the
    first attempt) followed by a valid second attempt; assert the real verdict is returned
    and the sprint is NOT forced to verdict:FAIL, and that the retry is visible in the log.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Both (a) and (b) covered by passing assertions under `npm test`.
- (a) is explicitly framed in a comment as a regression guard over shipped behaviour, not
  as a reproduction of an open bug.
- (b) demonstrably fails if apra-fleet-j6i.2's retry wrapper is reverted.
- ASCII only.
acceptance_criteria: npm test passes. (a) asserts timeout_s>=900 on all 8 heavy dispatches and asserts Streak Assignment passes no timeout_s; framed in-comment as a regression guard. (b) asserts one transient Final Review dispatch failure is retried, yields the real verdict rather than FAIL, and logs the retry; fails if j6i.2's wrapper is reverted. ASCII only.
close_reason: Added packages/apra-fleet-se/test/mock-sprint-finalization-review-retry.test.mjs -- 2 tests: (1) Final Review survives one transient dispatch failure via the retry-once wrapper, succeeds with the real retry verdict; (2) falls back to hardcoded FAIL only when both the original and retry attempts fail. Both pass.

title: [impl] Add retry-once wrapper around Final Review dispatch before hardcoding FAIL
description:
Line refs refreshed 2026-07-16 against current source (they had drifted).

In runner.js Final Review dispatch (packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/runner.js:2370-2396)
and its catch block (:2397-2404): the catch currently does
`if (err instanceof AgentOutputError || err instanceof AgentDispatchError) { ...
finalVerdictResult = { verdict: 'FAIL', ... } }` with NO retry attempt. This is the most
costly remaining instance: one transient timeout on the LAST dispatch of an otherwise
fully-successful sprint flips the whole outcome to FAIL.

Fix: wrap the Final Review dispatch in a retry-once, mirroring the Planner retry-once
wrapper at runner.js:1717-1723 or the doer-streak retry-once at :1989-2002, before falling
back to verdict:FAIL. Single file.

Scope boundary: this task ONLY adds the retry. The fuller AgentDispatchError-vs-
AgentOutputError type distinction across all 6 combined-catch sites is apra-fleet-02s's
scope -- do not do it here, but leave the retry structured so 02s can layer onto it.
acceptance_criteria: Final Review retries once on AgentDispatchError/AgentOutputError before setting verdict:FAIL. A simulated first-attempt transient failure followed by a valid second attempt yields the real verdict, not FAIL. The retry is visible in the log. Existing runner tests pass. ASCII only.
