title: CI: workflow --list smoke test fails on Windows (HOME not shared between smoke-test steps)
description:
POSSIBLY-ALREADY-FIXED -- flagged for review 2026-07-16. Independently verified against current .github/workflows/ci.yml (build-binary job).

Original claim: on the Windows leg, the 'workflow does-not-exist' smoke-test step self-heals into a HOME not shared with the later 'workflow --list' step, so --list fails to see built-ins.

Current state (verified 2026-07-16): commit 474ed88 ("fix(ci): share isolated HOME between workflow hello-world and arg/error-surface smoke steps") already fixed exactly this. In current ci.yml:
- "Smoke test - workflow hello-world (no system Node) (Windows)" (line 272) self-heals into a per-run $FAKEHOME and persists it via `Add-Content -Path $env:GITHUB_ENV -Value "FAKEHOME=$FAKEHOME"` (line 316).
- "Smoke test - workflow arg/error surface (Windows)" (line 343) is a SINGLE step that runs BOTH `workflow does-not-exist` (line 352) AND `workflow --list` (line 369), reusing `$env:FAKEHOME` set at lines 347-349. They are the same step -- there is no separate, non-sharing HOME between them.
- A code comment at lines 318-322 explicitly documents why: "`workflow --list` never self-heals by design ... so this step MUST reuse the FAKEHOME that the hello-world step above already self-healed."
- src/cli/workflow.ts:352-354 confirms `--list` just calls `formatWorkflowList(deps)` with no self-heal, consistent with the "by design" note (bead originally cited :352-355, drifted by 1 line, immaterial).

This is the same failure MODE the bead describes, and it is fixed on the current branch (feat/fleet-reorg contains 474ed88). No residual gap found in the current Windows leg. RECOMMEND: close this bead as already-fixed rather than feeding it into the sprint, unless CI has actually re-failed on a run AFTER 474ed88 landed (in which case get the run ID/job ID and re-open with that evidence -- the bead's originally-cited run 29385103194/job 87259968014 predates or coincides with the fix and should be re-checked against 474ed88's merge time).
close_reason: Already fixed by commit 474ed88 (fix(ci): share isolated HOME between workflow hello-world and arg/error-surface smoke steps) -- verified 2026-07-16 against current .github/workflows/ci.yml, the described HOME-sharing gap no longer exists.

title: [bug] execute_prompt timeouts bypass retry entirely; runner.js conflates dispatch vs schema errors; schema-repair must resume session instead of re-sending full context (wastes tokens)
description:
TRIAGED 2026-07-16: all three findings verified still present in current source; only line numbers have drifted (unrelated commits added lines above these regions since filing).

Follow-up investigation (originally 2026-07-15) after diagnosing apra-fleet-j6i's Final Review timeout/FAIL. THREE distinct, confirmed findings: two are bugs (server-side retry-bypass, client-side error-type conflation), one is a decided fix (schema-repair should use session resume, not fresh self-contained re-asks -- confirmed by user 2026-07-15: "must use resume, else we are wasting tokens").

1) SERVER-SIDE BUG (execute-prompt.ts) -- CONFIRMED, line unchanged: the outer catch block (now verified at execute-prompt.ts:506-508) still sets structuredContent:{isError:true, reason:'dispatch_failed'} for a genuine command timeout/thrown exception, structurally separate from and unreachable from the two retry mechanisms in the preceding try block (stale-session retry, server/overloaded-error retry) -- both of which only fire on a non-throwing nonzero exit, never on a thrown exception. A genuine timeout throws, so it reaches neither retry path -- single-shot, unconditionally-unretried, for every dispatch site system-wide.

2) CLIENT-SIDE BUG (runner.js) -- CONFIRMED, now 6 combined-catch sites (was cited as "at minimum Final Review's, ~line 2201"; verified 2026-07-16 the same `err instanceof AgentOutputError || err instanceof AgentDispatchError` combined-branch pattern exists at runner.js:1249, 1740, 2161, 2218, 2398 (Final Review, was ~2201), and 2462). packages/apra-fleet-workflow/src/workflow/index.mjs still correctly types AgentDispatchError vs AgentOutputError distinctly (confirmed: index.mjs's doc comments and throw sites unchanged in intent), but runner.js's catch blocks still discard that distinction, reporting both as "schema-repair exhausted, treating as FAIL" regardless of which actually occurred.

3) DECIDED FIX -- schema-repair must use session resume, not a fresh self-contained re-ask (packages/apra-fleet-workflow/src/workflow/index.mjs) -- CONFIRMED, lines verified: repair loop at index.mjs:720 (`for (let attempt = 0; attempt <= maxRepairs; attempt++)`), `isRepair` computed at :728, still dispatches with a STATIC `resume: opts.resume ?? false` at :761 regardless of isRepair. The "F10" self-contained-re-ask rationale comment is still present (now ~:759-761 region). maxRepairs itself computed at :625 (`opts.schemaRetries ?? 2`).

SUGGESTED FIX (full scope, unchanged from original investigation): (1) execute-prompt.ts's outer catch (dispatch exception path) should have its own bounded retry (at minimum one retry with a fresh session, mirroring the existing stale-session-retry pattern) rather than being an unconditional single-shot failure. (2) runner.js's 6 combined-catch sites should branch on the error TYPE (AgentDispatchError vs AgentOutputError) and handle each distinctly -- an AgentDispatchError should trigger a dispatch-retry or a clearly-labeled "infrastructure failure, not a quality failure" outcome, never be conflated with schema-repair exhaustion. (3) switch index.mjs's schema-repair loop to force resume:true when isRepair===true and shrink buildRepairPrompt() to a lean reminder-only message (validation errors + "please respond again with corrected JSON only" -- not re-embedding the full original prompt/invalid output) -- verify session continuity holds specifically across repair rounds before shipping.
close_reason: All 4 sub-tasks (02s.1-02s.4) implemented and tested: (1) execute-prompt.ts outer catch now retries once on a genuine dispatch exception; (2) runner.js's 6 combined-catch sites now distinguish AgentDispatchError vs AgentOutputError in logging; (3) schema-repair re-asks force resume:true and use a lean reminder prompt, per user's 2026-07-15 decision. Verified via node --check, npx tsc --noEmit, and scoped test runs (no full-suite run per 'run sparingly' instruction).

title: [bug] 6 of 9 auto-sprint dispatch types missing timeout_s override (still default 300s); Final Review also has zero retry
description:
TRIAGED 2026-07-16: the timeout_s gap this bead originally reported is now FIXED. Commit 3346e3f ("fix(auto-sprint): add timeout_s:900 to 6 dispatch types missing it (apra-fleet-j6i)") added timeout_s:900 to Planner (runner.js:1706), Plan Reviewer (:1736), Deployer (:2157), Integ-Test-Runner (:2213), Final Review (:2393), and Harvester (:2454), each tagged with an "apra-fleet-j6i" comment. Combined with the pre-existing Reviewer (:1244) and Doer (:1984) timeout_s:900, ALL 8 dispatch types now have the override except Streak Assignment (runner.js:1856-1875), which remains intentionally excluded per the original user decision (lightweight classification task, 300s default is fine). Verified live in current source 2026-07-16 -- no remaining timeout_s gaps.

Separately, commit 33552b4 ("fix(auto-sprint): retry-once wrapper around Planner dispatch (apra-fleet-j6i)") added a retry-once wrapper around the Planner dispatch (runner.js:1717-1723) as an interim mitigation for Planner's previously-uncaught AgentDispatchError, explicitly noting in its own comment that apra-fleet-j6i.2/j6i.3 still cover "the fuller dispatch-vs-schema-error distinction this really deserves" (i.e. apra-fleet-02s's scope).

REMAINING, STILL-OPEN ISSUE (this bead's scope should narrow to just this): Final Review (runner.js:2370-2404) still has ZERO retry on dispatch failure. Its catch block (2397-2404) still does `if (err instanceof AgentOutputError || err instanceof AgentDispatchError) { ... finalVerdictResult = { verdict: 'FAIL', ... } }` with no retry attempt at all -- confirmed unchanged in current source. This is still the most costly instance: a single transient timeout/dispatch failure on the LAST dispatch of an otherwise fully-successful sprint can still flip the whole sprint's outcome to FAIL, exactly as originally described. Unlike Planner, Final Review never got its own retry-once wrapper.

SUGGESTED FIX (narrowed scope): add a retry-once wrapper around the Final Review dispatch (runner.js:2370-2396), mirroring the Planner pattern at :1717-1723 or the doer-streak retry-once pattern (:1989-2002), before falling back to hardcoded FAIL.
close_reason: Parent bug fully resolved: all 9 dispatch types now have timeout_s (commit 3346e3f, 8/9 by design excludes Streak Assignment) and Final Review now has retry-once (commit 50fff06 / apra-fleet-j6i.2). Both sub-tasks (j6i.2, j6i.3) closed with verified test coverage.

title: Viewer/workflow: cost always $0 -- _resolveCost/calculateCost read wrong usage field names (prompt_tokens/completion_tokens vs input_tokens/output_tokens)
description:
Confirmed live via /state API (2026-07-13): every agent activity shows real, non-null token usage (e.g. {input_tokens:43, output_tokens:11797, total_tokens:11840}) but cost is always exactly 0 (not null/unknown -- unknownCostCount stays 0 too), even though model tier ('premium') and token counts now render correctly in the UI (today's earlier fixes for 7b0/13o/0ak worked for those).

Root cause: packages/apra-fleet-workflow/src/workflow/index.mjs _resolveCost() (lines 563-582, added in d9852d7 'consume get_member_model_pricing for real per-member cost') reads usage.prompt_tokens / usage.completion_tokens. The ACTUAL usage object shape produced by execute-prompt.ts's structuredContent (and confirmed live in /state) is {input_tokens, output_tokens, total_tokens} -- different field names entirely. Both reads silently default to 0 via '|| 0', so cost always computes to exactly (0/1e6)*price + (0/1e6)*price = 0, a valid-looking number rather than null, so it's never counted as 'unknown'.

Same bug exists in the fallback path: packages/apra-fleet-workflow/src/workflow/pricing.mjs calculateCost() line 67 also checks/reads usage.prompt_tokens / usage.completion_tokens.

Not a missing feature -- this is a straightforward field-name mismatch. Likely pre-existing since d9852d7 but previously unreachable/masked because usage was always null before today's 7b0/13o fix started threading real usage through -- now that real usage flows in, this bug is actually exercised on every single agent call.

Fix: change both _resolveCost() and calculateCost() to read usage.input_tokens / usage.output_tokens (matching the actual shape), or normalize the usage object's field names once at the point execute-prompt.ts's structuredContent is consumed so both consumers don't need duplicate awareness of the shape.

title: Viewer: activity tree squishes/compresses instead of scrolling -- missing flex-shrink:0 on .tree-group
description:
Confirmed live via browser DOM testing (2026-07-13, cloneNode stress test on the real .tree-group/.group-body/.phase-body structure inside #stream-list).

packages/apra-fleet-workflow/src/viewer/index.mjs: #stream-list is 'display:flex; flex-direction:column; flex:1; overflow-y:auto' (correctly bounded per the apra-fleet-m0c fix). But its direct children, .tree-group (line ~41) -- and by extension .group-body (line 46) and .tree-phase/.phase-body (lines 49/54) nested inside them -- never set flex-shrink:0. Default flex-shrink:1 means once total content height exceeds #stream-list's bounded clientHeight, flexbox proportionally SHRINKS all .tree-group items to fit rather than letting them keep natural height and overflow -- so the scrollbar never actually engages and content is visually compressed/squished instead.

Reproduced: appended 45 additional clones of the real (open) .tree-group nodes into a live #stream-list and measured scrollHeight === clientHeight even with 48 groups present (would be ~1700-1900px of natural content vs a 1119px container) -- conclusive proof of shrink-to-fit rather than scroll-to-overflow. Matches the user's exact reported symptom: 'only way to view items is my collapsing some, while the expectation is that user must be able to scroll within the client area.'

This is a DIFFERENT, additional root cause from apra-fleet-m0c/5lj (already fixed, real but insufficient on its own -- that fix made #stream-list's overflow:auto correctly bounded, this bug is why it still never triggers in practice).

Fix: add flex-shrink: 0 to .tree-group (and verify .tree-phase / individual details.event-activity if they're also direct flex children needing the same treatment).
close_reason: Consolidated into apra-fleet-eft.43 (in-sprint scroll bug; carries this bead's flex-shrink:0 diagnosis). Filed outside the eft epic so no sprint ever targeted it.

title: Viewer: dashboard still not auto-refreshing live (root cause not yet confirmed)
description:
User reports (2026-07-13, second observation pass, same sprint run that surfaced apra-fleet-80b): dashboard still requires manual F5 to see updates, even on binary v0.3.5_08105e which already contains the apra-fleet-m0c/5lj CSS fix (tab-content.active flex) and today's other viewer fixes.

Code inspection of packages/apra-fleet-workflow/src/viewer/index.mjs shows the auto-refresh mechanism (EventSource('/events') -> onmessage -> poll() -> fetch('/state')) lives in a SEPARATE <script> tag from the broken dashboard-extension script (apra-fleet-80b) -- browsers parse/execute each <script> tag independently, so 80b's parse error should NOT prevent this one from running. This is therefore likely a distinct, still-unconfirmed root cause, not simply 80b again.

Needs live verification (not yet done -- recording only per current instruction):
- Does the browser actually open a live EventSource connection to /events, or does it fail/never connect?
- Does the server side (viewer/index.mjs's '/events' SSE handler) actually push events during a real sprint run, or only on certain event types?
- Is the user looking at a page loaded before the server was restarted with today's fixes (stale tab, not a stale server)?

File this instead of closing it as a duplicate of 80b or m0c/5lj -- those are real but did not touch the EventSource/poll() code path.
notes: CORRECTION per user clarification (2026-07-13): the dashboard DOES update on real events (activity completion, phase start) via the existing EventSource('/events') -> poll() wiring -- confirmed working, not broken. The actual, milder ask: during quiet periods with no new events, the user wants a periodic heartbeat (every 5-10s) so it's visible the dashboard/sprint is still alive, not stalled. This is a feature request (add a client-side setInterval fallback poll, e.g. every 5-10s, independent of the EventSource message-driven poll() calls), not a bug fix. Downgrading urgency framing accordingly; root cause of full non-update was never confirmed and is now believed not to exist as originally suspected.

title: Viewer: dashboard-extension <script> tag broken by double-escaped \n, breaks beads tab entirely
description:
packages/apra-fleet-workflow/src/viewer/index.mjs:141 (committed 2026-07-11 in 1fe5af95, predates today's m0c/5lj/aqq fixes, untouched by them):

  ${dashboardExtensions.map(ext => `<script>\n${ext.js}\n</script>`).join('\n')}

The \n inside the template literal is a DOUBLE-escaped backslash-n -- it renders into the HTML output as the literal two characters backslash+n, not a real newline. Confirmed live: curl of http://localhost:8090/ shows line 138 as literal '<script>\n' followed by the extension JS starting on the next output line. Browser console reports 'Uncaught SyntaxError: Invalid or unexpected token (at (index):138:11)' -- backslash-n outside a string is not valid JS, so this whole <script> tag (the dashboard-extension script supplied by packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/viewer-extensions.mjs, which defines renderBeadsHtml/escapeHtml for the Beads Tasks tab) fails to parse and NONE of it executes.

This plausibly explains why the Beads Tasks tab / tree has appeared empty across multiple sprint runs (previously misattributed entirely to apra-fleet-m0c, a real but separate CSS flex/scroll bug that was already fixed and did NOT touch this line).

Fix: change both \n occurrences in that map/join to a single backslash-n (\n), matching the actual-newline convention used elsewhere in the same file (e.g. the .join('\n') calls elsewhere are already correct).
close_reason: Fixed in 144a4df: changed the double-escaped \n to a real newline in the dashboardExtensions <script> template (viewer/index.mjs:141). Verified: extracted the rendered extension script and confirmed it parses via new Function(); apra-fleet-workflow suite 112/112 passing.

title: Auto-sprint doer dispatch: near-instant deterministic JSON-parse failures (distinct from the ~300s-timeout variant), 3/4 items failed outright in one round
description:
Observed live during the apra-fleet-7pm auto-sprint run (Develop C1 R2, member fleet-reorg). Distinct from the previously filed apra-fleet-jkw / the ~300s-timeout-shaped failures noted on 7pm.1+7pm.2 and the Review C1 R1 reviewer (those were genuine long LLM calls that self-healed on schema repair). This is a DIFFERENT failure mode:

In Develop C1 R2, 4 streaks were dispatched: [apra-fleet-7pm.12], [apra-fleet-7pm.6], [apra-fleet-7pm.3], [apra-fleet-7pm.4]. Three of them (.6, .3, .4) failed COMPLETELY -- both schema-repair attempts (1/2, 2/2) AND the top-level 'retrying once' pass all exhausted -- and every single attempt/repair activity recorded a duration of well under 100ms (e.g. 49ms, 50ms, 51ms initial attempts; 3ms, 6ms, 6ms repair-1; 6ms, 6ms, 14ms repair-2; then a second full top-level retry cycle repeating the same sub-100ms pattern). That is far too fast to be an actual LLM turn completing -- something is failing before any real model response, and the resulting text (which starts with the literal characters '❌ Failed to' or '❌ execute_') is being fed directly into the structured-output JSON parser as if it were the agent's final answer, rather than being recognized as a tool/dispatch-level error and handled distinctly.

The '❌ ...' text shape strongly resembles MCP tool-error/failure text (e.g. from execute_command's error formatting) rather than free-form LLM prose, which suggests this may be the same class of issue as the already-filed apra-fleet-1cb (some apra-fleet-se code paths conflate a shell command's nonzero exit / MCP-level tool error with the agent's final structured response, rather than treating it as a distinct error path that should either retry the underlying command or surface a real error).

Why this is higher priority than the timeout variant: the timeout-shaped failures are flaky and self-heal via repair/retry (observed recovering on repair 2 or the second top-level attempt). This near-instant variant is fully deterministic -- every repair and retry hit the identical sub-100ms failure with no variance -- meaning ANY task whose doer dispatch trips this path will keep failing identically forever, with no chance of self-healing through the existing retry/repair mechanism. In this round it took out 3 of 4 dispatched items in one shot, which threatens the sprint's ability to make further progress on the epic's remaining tasks.

Reported live during the run at user's request to log distinctly and at higher priority; not yet investigated -- record only.
notes: FIXED (pending final verification/commit): root-caused to two compounding issues. (1) doerPool[index % doerPool.length] round-robins streaks across the doer pool, but in single-member mode every streak resolves to the SAME member; parallel() then dispatches them concurrently, colliding with the fleet server's per-member inFlightAgents busy guard in execute_prompt every time (deterministic, not flaky). Fixed in packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/runner.js: added a per-member serialization lock (memberLocks Map) so same-member streaks queue instead of colliding; different-member streaks remain fully concurrent (no behavior change for true multi-member fleets). (2) Separately, execute_prompt's dispatch-level failure text (busy-guard rejection, non-zero exit, transport exception -- all starting with the literal characters the server prints) was being fed into the JSON-schema extractor as if it were the LLM's own output, burning schema-repair attempts and misreporting as 'LLM failed to return parseable JSON'. Fixed: src/tools/execute-prompt.ts now returns {text, structuredContent:{isError,reason}} on these paths (same additive-channel pattern as apra-fleet-7ll's execute_command fix); packages/apra-fleet-workflow/src/workflow/index.mjs's agent() now checks structuredContent.isError FIRST and throws a new typed AgentDispatchError immediately (never retried via schema repair) instead of misclassifying. New error class in errors.mjs; all 7 runner.js catch sites that already special-cased AgentOutputError now also catch AgentDispatchError for the same graceful-fallback treatment.
close_reason: fixed, commit 51df121 on feat/fleet-reorg (merged from feat/fleet-workflow-subsystem); see commit body for details

title: [impl] Phase 2 task 6: self-heal extraction in workflow.ts
description:
Per docs/workflow-subsystem-plan.md Section 3 ("Self-heal") and Section 10 Phase 2 task 6.

Files: src/cli/workflow.ts (extend), and extraction helpers factored out of src/cli/install.ts into a shared module WITHOUT changing install.ts's own behavior (pure refactor-extract, install.ts continues to call the same logic it did before).

Work: if ~/.apra-fleet/workflows/ or ~/.apra-fleet/node_modules/ is missing when `apra-fleet workflow <name>` is invoked and <name> is a recognized built-in (auto-sprint, hello-world), extract the shared runtime + schemas + built-in workflows from SEA assets on demand, using the SAME extraction code path the installer uses (the factored-out helper) -- print one line noting the on-demand extraction happened. A requested name that is NOT a built-in must NOT trigger extraction of itself; it should still trigger extraction of the shared runtime/schemas if those are missing (since a user workflow needs the runtime too), but the launcher does not fabricate a workflow directory for an unknown user-named workflow.

Acceptance criteria: starting from an empty ~/.apra-fleet/ (simulated via a temp HOME in tests), `apra-fleet workflow hello-world` extracts runtime+schemas+built-ins, prints exactly one self-heal notice line, and then runs hello-world successfully; requesting a non-built-in unknown name still gets the standard "not found" error (Phase 2 task 5's error path) and does NOT create a workflows/<unknown-name>/ directory; install.ts's own install step (Phase 1 task 3) is verified unchanged by a diff/behavioral check (same console output, same files written) after the extraction-helper refactor.
close_reason: Implemented self-heal extraction in workflow.ts, factored install.ts extraction logic into shared src/cli/workflow-assets.ts; all tests pass (2281).

title: [impl] Phase 2 task 5: src/cli/workflow.ts launcher + src/index.ts dispatch case
description:
Per docs/workflow-subsystem-plan.md Section 3 (CLI surface), Section 1 step 2-3 (launcher mechanics), and Section 10 Phase 2 task 5. MUST follow docs/adr-workflow-server-resolution.md -- the R13 resolution-order decision written by apra-fleet-7pm.6. Read that ADR before implementing the server-reachability half of this task. It is binding: (a) resolution order = APRA_FLEET_TRANSPORT override -> checkRunningInstance() HTTP-singleton probe (attach via StreamableHttpTransport, spawn nothing) -> stdio self-spawn fallback (existing 4 tiers); (b) this logic is ONE shared helper at packages/apra-fleet-client/src/client/server-resolution.mjs (subpath export @apralabs/apra-fleet-client/server-resolution), NOT duplicated -- src/cli/workflow.ts calls it and must not copy resolveFleetServerCommand(); (c) never merge launcher and MCP server into one process.

Files (new): src/cli/workflow.ts. Files (edit): src/index.ts (add a lazy-imported dispatch case for the `workflow` subcommand, matching how other subcommands are wired).

Work: implement `apra-fleet workflow <name> [args...]`: (1) resolve ~/.apra-fleet/workflows/<name>/workflow.json -> entry (relative path; reject entries that resolve outside the workflow directory, i.e. reject `..` escapes); if no workflow.json, fall back to the first existing of main.mjs, index.mjs, runner.js. (2) Set APRA_FLEET_SERVER_BIN and APRA_FLEET_SE_SCHEMAS_DIR as environment defaults ONLY when not already set by the caller -- user overrides always win. Apply docs/adr-workflow-server-resolution.md's resolution order (via the shared @apralabs/apra-fleet-client/server-resolution helper) for how the launcher/eventual workflow process reaches the fleet server. (3) Rewrite process.argv to [execPath, <entryAbsPath>, ...passthroughArgs] and `await import(pathToFileURL(entryAbsPath))`; if the imported module did not self-execute (no side effects observed and it exports main/run/default), call that export with the raw pass-through args array. (4) Launcher-owned flags `--list` (list installed workflows: name, description, builtin/user) and `--help` (launcher's own help; does NOT swallow `<name> --help`) are recognized only before `<name>` or when no name is given. (5) Errors: unknown `<name>` exits 1 with `Error: workflow "<name>" not found in ~/.apra-fleet/workflows/.` plus the --list output; missing/invalid workflow.json entry exits 1 naming the file; a thrown error from the workflow itself is a non-zero exit with the stack on stderr. (6) [R10/R9, added by plan-reviewer] Before importing the entry, compare the running binary's version against workflows/.installed.json's recorded install version (apra-fleet-7pm.5); on mismatch, warn and suggest `apra-fleet install` (R10) rather than proceeding silently. If the SEA asset manifest lacks the workflow sections entirely (old binary built before this epic), print an actionable "rebuild/reinstall" error instead of a raw resolution failure (R9).

Acceptance criteria: unit tests (with an injected fs/env seam, no real ~/.apra-fleet/ needed) cover: successful resolution via workflow.json, fallback-convention resolution, env-var respect (existing env values never overwritten), the `..`-escape rejection, unknown-name error text, invalid-entry error text, --list/--help behavior, the version-mismatch warning (R10), and the missing-SEA-asset-sections actionable error (R9); `apra-fleet workflow auto-sprint --issue X --members m1` (manual/integration check) reaches auto-sprint's own parseCliArgs() with the pass-through args completely untouched.
close_reason: Closed

title: [design] Resolve R13: launcher fleet-server resolution order (HTTP singleton vs. stdio self-spawn)
description:
Per docs/workflow-subsystem-plan.md Section 1 (the "Known gap ... flag for the task breakdown, not yet resolved by this doc" paragraph) and risk register row R13.

Problem: resolveFleetServerCommand() in packages/apra-fleet-se/bin/cli.mjs currently implements ONLY the stdio-self-spawn branch (4 resolution tiers, all producing `run --transport stdio` args). It has no HTTP-mode branch. streamableHTTP is the actual product default (install.ts:484-485 documents HTTP as default, stdio as legacy) with a persistent local singleton server already running as an installed OS service, discoverable via ~/.apra-fleet/data/server.json ({pid, url}) and validated via src/services/singleton.ts's checkRunningInstance() (pid-alive check + GET <url>/health). A launcher that naively "mirrors" cli.mjs's stdio-only function would always self-spawn a private stdio server even when a healthy HTTP singleton is already running -- defeating the "attach to what's already there" behavior users expect and doubling running server processes.

Files: no code changes in this task -- this is a design/decision task. Output is a written decision (as a short addendum section in docs/workflow-subsystem-plan.md, or a new docs/adr-workflow-server-resolution.md if preferred) that the src/cli/workflow.ts implementation task (this epic) and any future auto-sprint cli.mjs changes must follow.

Work: decide and document the launcher's resolution order: (a) when transport is HTTP/default, probe checkRunningInstance() (reusing src/services/singleton.ts's pid+/health check against ~/.apra-fleet/data/server.json) FIRST; on success, connect via packages/apra-fleet-client's already-implemented StreamableHttpTransport directly -- no server spawn; (b) fall back to the existing stdio self-spawn path (mirroring cli.mjs's 4-tier resolution) only if no healthy HTTP singleton is found (e.g. before `apra-fleet install`/`start` has ever run, or with APRA_FLEET_TRANSPORT=stdio forced). Also decide and document: does this resolution-order logic live in ONE shared helper that both `apra-fleet workflow` (src/cli/workflow.ts) and auto-sprint's cli.mjs both call, or is it duplicated in each? Record the tradeoff and the chosen answer explicitly -- do not leave it implicit.

Acceptance criteria: a written decision document exists (addendum to the plan doc or a new ADR file) that: (1) states the exact resolution order above; (2) states explicitly whether the logic is a shared helper or duplicated, and where the shared helper (if chosen) would live; (3) is referenced by name from the src/cli/workflow.ts implementation task's description before that task starts implementation. Task authors must NOT propose merging the launcher and MCP server into one process (explicitly out of scope per Section 1) -- the decision is only about HOW the client reaches the server, never whether they are separate processes.
close_reason: Closed

title: [impl] Phase 1 task 3: install.ts -- additive workflow-install step, dev-manifest parity, --workflows flag, install-config persistence
description:
Per docs/workflow-subsystem-plan.md Section 6 (install.ts) and Section 10 Phase 1 task 3.

Files: src/cli/install.ts, src/cli/config.ts.

FILE OWNERSHIP (exclusive): this task is the SOLE owner of src/cli/install.ts and of the WRITE side of src/cli/config.ts (writeInstallConfig). The Windows EBUSY retry loop lands entirely here. No other task in this epic edits install.ts or the writeInstallConfig write path; apra-fleet-7pm.10 consumes what this task writes (read-back only) and must not re-implement it.

Work: append ONE new install step after the existing agents step (existing steps 1-8 stay untouched/unreordered), gated by a new --workflows <all|none> flag defaulting to all (mirrors the existing --skill flag pattern; extend --help text). The step does: (1) write ~/.apra-fleet/node_modules/ from manifest key workflowRuntime using extract-to-temp-then-rename per package dir, with the EBUSY retry pattern from commit f66e621 on Windows -- this retry loop must be written so it also covers the update-time re-extraction case where a built-in workflow directory is locked by a running apra-fleet process (warn + skip that directory rather than failing the whole install); (2) write ~/.apra-fleet/schemas/ from manifest key agentSchemas; (3) for each built-in workflow name ("auto-sprint", "hello-world"): clearDirSync ONLY that named subdirectory under ~/.apra-fleet/workflows/, then extract it -- NEVER clearDirSync the workflows/ root itself; update workflows/.installed.json with the built-in list and installed version. Extend the AssetManifest TypeScript interface (install.ts:76-84) with optional workflowRuntime/agentSchemas/builtinWorkflows keys (already declared upstream by the gen-sea-config.mjs task this depends on) -- if keys are absent (older SEA build), skip the step with a warning instead of erroring, for forward/backward compatibility. buildDevManifest() (install.ts:118-158) gains matching collection logic so `node dist/index.js install` behaves identically from a dev checkout.

Also in config.ts (write side): persist the chosen --workflows mode (all|none) into install-config.json via writeInstallConfig as a `workflowsMode` field, exactly the way skillMode is already persisted, and extend the install-config TypeScript type accordingly. Reading that field back and using it during update is apra-fleet-7pm.10's job, not this task's.

Acceptance criteria:
- Fresh SEA install produces exactly the Section 2.1 layout (~/.apra-fleet/node_modules, /schemas, /workflows/{auto-sprint,hello-world}, /workflows/.installed.json).
- Existing install steps' console output and behavior are byte-identical when --workflows none is passed.
- A pre-existing ~/.apra-fleet/workflows/user-x/ directory survives the install untouched.
- An old manifest (new keys absent) causes the new step to skip with a warning rather than crash.
- install-config.json written by a `--workflows none` install contains workflowsMode: "none"; a default install contains workflowsMode: "all" (assert via a writeInstallConfig unit test).
- A simulated EBUSY lock on one built-in workflow directory during extraction produces a warning + skip of that directory, and the install still exits 0.
- Unit tests using the existing _setManifestOverride seam cover both the old-manifest and new-manifest shapes.
close_reason: Verified: install.ts workflow-install step, --workflows flag, config.ts workflowsMode persistence, and dev-manifest parity already implemented (committed in prior wip checkpoints ffa55f1/8bf8601). All 8 acceptance criteria covered by tests/install-workflows.test.ts (13 tests). Full build + test suite (155 tests across install*.ts/uninstall.test.ts) passes.

title: [impl] Phase 1 task 4: auto-sprint workflow packaging shape (workflow.json + preserved directory layout)
description:
Per docs/workflow-subsystem-plan.md Section 2.1 and Section 10 Phase 1 task 4.

Files: packages/apra-fleet-se/ (add workflow.json at the package root).

Work: add packages/apra-fleet-se/workflow.json = `{"name":"auto-sprint","entry":"bin/cli.mjs","description":"Multi-cycle sprint: plan -> develop -> test -> harvest"}`. Verify (do not change) that the package's existing directory shape is preserved end-to-end so cli.mjs's relative `../auto-sprint/runner.js` resolution and resolveRunnerScriptPath()'s dev branch keep working unmodified once this tree is copied verbatim into ~/.apra-fleet/workflows/auto-sprint/ by the installer (task in this epic that consumes this shape). package.json's existing "type": "module" must be preserved in the installed copy -- it is REQUIRED because runner.js has a .js extension and is ESM; if the installer copy step (companion task) would drop package.json, flag it as a blocking coordination note in that task instead of silently reordering scope here.

Acceptance criteria: packages/apra-fleet-se/workflow.json exists with exactly the shape above and is valid JSON; a manual copy of packages/apra-fleet-se/ (minus test/, docs/, scripts/) to a scratch directory, run as `node bin/cli.mjs --help` from that scratch dir with a system Node (dev-only sanity check, not the shipped launcher), prints cli.mjs's usage text with no schema dev-fallback warning on stderr -- proving the package's own layout is self-sufficient before the installer/launcher get involved.
close_reason: Closed

title: [impl] Phase 1 task 2: gen-sea-config.mjs -- embed workflow runtime, schemas, built-in workflows as SEA assets
description:
Per docs/workflow-subsystem-plan.md Section 2.2 and Section 7 (build pipeline changes), Section 10 Phase 1 task 2.

Files: scripts/gen-sea-config.mjs.

Work: using the existing collectFiles() helper, add three new manifest sections to dist/sea-manifest.json: workflowRuntime (packages/apra-fleet-workflow/ + packages/apra-fleet-client/ + node_modules/ajv and its 4 deps fast-deep-equal, fast-uri, json-schema-traverse, require-from-string -- ~2.4 MB / ~200 files), agentSchemas (vendor/apra-pm/agents/schemas/*.json -- currently 14 files, ~48 KB; the glob is authoritative, do not hardcode the count), builtinWorkflows (packages/apra-fleet-se/ minus test/, docs/, scripts/ -- plus examples/workflows/hello-world/, sourced from the prerequisite task that authors it). Exclude test/, docs/, scripts/, examples/ from package trees generally and exclude ajv's docs. Hard-fail the build (loud error, non-zero exit) if node_modules/ajv or vendor/apra-pm/agents/schemas is missing, mirroring the existing submodule guard pattern at gen-sea-config.mjs:51-55. Emit new counts in the build log plus a blob-size-delta line (total asset bytes) for CI eyeballing. AssetManifest's TypeScript interface (install.ts:76-84) gains these three keys as OPTIONAL so older manifests / existing tests are unaffected.

Acceptance criteria: dist/sea-manifest.json gains workflowRuntime, agentSchemas, builtinWorkflows sections covering exactly the sources listed above; build fails loudly (non-zero exit, clear message naming the missing path) if any source directory is absent; the new asset byte total is logged; existing manifest consumers/tests that don't know about the new keys still pass unmodified (keys are additive/optional).
close_reason: Closed

title: [impl] Phase 2 task 7 (moved earlier as an asset-source prerequisite): author examples/workflows/hello-world
description:
Per docs/workflow-subsystem-plan.md Section 4 and Section 10 Phase 2 task 7. NOTE on sequencing: the original doc lists this as a Phase 2 task, but Section 10 Phase 1 task 2 (gen-sea-config.mjs) requires examples/workflows/hello-world to already exist as a build-time asset source ("Sources at build time: ... plus a new examples/workflows/hello-world/ in-repo source dir", Section 2.2). This task is therefore scheduled early and is a hard dependency of the gen-sea-config.mjs asset-embedding task, not the other way around -- this reflects an ordering fix identified during planning, not a change to the doc's design.

Files (new): examples/workflows/hello-world/workflow.json, examples/workflows/hello-world/main.mjs.

Work: workflow.json = exactly `{ "name": "hello-world", "entry": "main.mjs", "description": "Minimal example workflow" }`. main.mjs (self-executing ESM, ~30 lines) must: (1) read its args from process.argv.slice(2); (2) prove the shared runtime resolves by doing `const { WorkflowEngine } = await import('@apralabs/apra-fleet-workflow/engine').catch(() => ({}))` -- a DYNAMIC import wrapped in catch, NOT a static `import { ... } from ...`, because a static import throws ERR_MODULE_NOT_FOUND before any code runs and cannot ever produce the `engine=missing` output required below; (3) print exactly `[OK] hello-world: args=<comma-joined args> engine=<resolved|missing>` (resolved iff WorkflowEngine is defined) and exit 0. No fleet server connection -- this keeps the eventual CI smoke test (Phase 4) hermetic.

Acceptance criteria: `node examples/workflows/hello-world/main.mjs a b` run from a dev checkout with the workspace's @apralabs/apra-fleet-workflow resolvable (e.g. via a node_modules symlink or workspace install) prints `[OK] hello-world: args=a,b engine=resolved` and exits 0; running with the engine package genuinely unresolvable (e.g. NODE_PATH/node_modules manipulated so the dynamic import rejects) prints `engine=missing` and still exits 0 (proves the check is real and non-fatal, not hardcoded, and confirms the dynamic-import-with-catch requirement above is actually implemented); workflow.json is valid JSON matching the exact shape above.
close_reason: Closed

title: [spike] Phase 1.1: Prove dynamic import() of on-disk ESM from inside a SEA main script on all 3 OS
description:
Risk gate (R1 in the risk register) -- this is the mandatory Phase 1 spike; per docs/workflow-subsystem-plan.md Section 1, "the spike lands before any dependent task starts". No other task in this epic may begin implementation until this task closes with a recorded decision.

Files: throwaway scripts/spike-sea-import.mjs (deleted or kept as a documented fixture at the author's discretion -- not shipped code), a temporary CI step or local runs on win/linux/mac, and an addendum written back into docs/workflow-subsystem-plan.md Section 1 recording the outcome.

Work: build a minimal SEA binary (reuse the existing scripts/gen-sea-config.mjs / scripts/build-sea.mjs pipeline, current config with useCodeCache: true as set in gen-sea-config.mjs:130) whose injected main does `await import(pathToFileURL(path-to-an-on-disk-.mjs-fixture))`, where that fixture itself imports a bare specifier ('@scope/pkg') resolved from a sibling node_modules directory (mirrors the real ~/.apra-fleet/node_modules/ layout Section 2 will install). Run the packaged binary on Windows, Linux, and macOS (or documented CI runners standing in for them) and confirm the import succeeds and the bare specifier resolves.

If it fails on any OS: measure and record the fallback options named in Section 1 -- (a) flip useCodeCache off and re-measure startup delta, or (b) as a last resort, module.createRequire() + a tiny on-disk ESM bootstrap started via node:worker_threads. Write down which option is adopted; this decision is binding for tasks 4 and 5 below (gen-sea-config.mjs asset embedding and install.ts's install step).

Acceptance criteria: a packaged SEA binary (built with current useCodeCache: true) successfully import()s an on-disk .mjs that itself imports a bare specifier from a sibling node_modules, on all 3 OS -- OR the fallback decision (useCodeCache off / worker-thread bootstrap) is explicitly written down in docs/workflow-subsystem-plan.md as an addendum and adopted as the binding design for the rest of this epic. Either outcome closes this task; a silent "it mostly worked" without a written decision does not.
close_reason: Closed

title: [EPIC] apra-fleet workflow subsystem: SEA-binary workflow runner (import-trampoline launcher, on-disk runtime tree, built-in workflows)
description:
Ship 'apra-fleet workflow <name>' as a new SEA-binary subcommand that runs workflow scripts (auto-sprint, hello-world, and future user-authored workflows) with ZERO system Node required. Full design: docs/workflow-subsystem-plan.md (this repo, feat/fleet-reorg).

Architecture decision (fixed -- do not relitigate in child tasks; see plan Section 1): "import trampoline + on-disk runtime tree" (option c). The SEA bundle gains a tiny launcher (src/cli/workflow.ts) that resolves the workflow under ~/.apra-fleet/workflows/<name>/, sets APRA_FLEET_SERVER_BIN / APRA_FLEET_SE_SCHEMAS_DIR only when unset, rewrites process.argv, and does an in-process dynamic import() of the entry file. The installer populates ~/.apra-fleet/node_modules/ with verbatim copies of @apralabs/apra-fleet-workflow, @apralabs/apra-fleet-client, and vendored ajv (+4 deps) so Node's normal upward node_modules walk resolves bare specifiers with NO loader hooks and NO dual-copy of error classes (avoids the instanceof-breakage hazard called out as the decisive constraint in Section 1). sea-bundle.cjs itself contains ZERO workflow-engine code.

Hard invariant: 'apra-fleet workflow <name>' is ALWAYS a separate client process from the apra-fleet MCP server it talks to (Section 1). The only open question is HOW the client reaches the server -- self-spawned stdio vs. attaching to an already-running HTTP singleton -- see R13 (a dedicated child task in this epic resolves it; do not merge launcher and server processes).

Known predecessor bead: apra-fleet-3ns.6 ('SEA binary: embed new auto-sprint as SEA assets', open, P3) asked exactly the question this plan answers (extraction destination, server resolution via APRA_FLEET_SERVER_BIN). This epic supersedes/absorbs it -- do not create duplicate parallel scope; 3ns.6 should be closed by whoever executes this epic's Phase 1 tasks, referencing this epic in the close reason. Left open (not closed) by this planning pass per planning-agent rules (never close issues while planning).

File-level collision note (Section 0): apra-fleet-dv5's children touch packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/runner.js and pricing.mjs. This epic's Phase 1 install-payload tasks install verbatim copies of those same files -- no rework required either way, but avoid two concurrent sprints editing runner.js at the same time.

Scope: docs/workflow-subsystem-plan.md Sections 1-10 in full (architecture, file/asset inventory, CLI surface, hello-world authoring contract, schema installation fix, install/uninstall/update integration, build pipeline changes, CI verification, risk register, phased task breakdown). Out of scope: merging the launcher and MCP server into one process (explicitly rejected in Section 1); anything under vendor/apra-pm/** unrelated to schemas; publishing @apralabs/apra-fleet-workflow/-client to npm standalone.

Done looks like: all Section 10 phases (1-4) complete; 'apra-fleet workflow hello-world' runs with zero system Node on all 3 OS in CI; auto-sprint is runnable as a built-in workflow via the launcher; docs/authoring-workflows.md exists; existing command surface (install/uninstall/run/--version) unchanged per the Phase 4 regression guard.

title: [impl] Confirm dist/agents/schemas as the bundled-layout schema source for dist/auto-sprint.mjs (no new copy step)
description:
Size: S. Coordination decision from apra-fleet-bun.1 (do not relitigate): the root-bundled layout (dist/auto-sprint.mjs) resolves vendored schemas from the sibling dist/agents/schemas/ directory that scripts/vendor-pm.mjs ALREADY populates today via root prepublishOnly (cpSync of vendor/apra-pm/agents, which includes agents/schemas/). apra-fleet-3ns.2's esbuild bundling step does NOT need to create or copy a new schema directory for this layout -- it only needs to (a) run after (or alongside) scripts/vendor-pm.mjs in the build pipeline so dist/agents/schemas exists before/independently of dist/auto-sprint.mjs, and (b) NOT exclude/externalize contracts.mjs's fs-based schema resolution when bundling cli.mjs (contracts.mjs's resolveSchemasDir() from apra-fleet-bun.1 does path resolution + fs.existsSync at runtime, not a build-time import of schema JSON -- so esbuild bundling contracts.mjs's CODE is safe; the schema JSON files themselves are never bundled into dist/auto-sprint.mjs, they stay as loose files in dist/agents/schemas/, read at runtime).

Work: verify (and add a one-line comment in the new esbuild script noting) that dist/agents/schemas/ is a build-order dependency for a working dist/auto-sprint.mjs, i.e. document the ordering requirement (vendor-pm.mjs before or independent of the esbuild step, both before pack/publish) in whatever new build script apra-fleet-3ns.2 introduces for the esbuild bundling.

Acceptance criteria: build script or its comments explicitly document that dist/agents/schemas/ (populated by scripts/vendor-pm.mjs) is the schema source dist/auto-sprint.mjs resolves at runtime; no new schema-copying code added by this task; a fresh 'npm run build' from a clean dist/ produces both dist/agents/schemas/*.json and a working dist/auto-sprint.mjs in either order without error.

title: [test] Simulated standalone install resolves bundled schemas correctly (no ancestor vendor/)
description:
Size: M. New integration test (node:test, alongside the other contracts-schema-*.test.mjs files, e.g. contracts-schema-packaging.test.mjs) proving apra-fleet-bun's acceptance criterion end-to-end: a package directory with NO ancestor vendor/ present still resolves correct, non-stale vendored schemas.

Test plan:
  1. Build a temp directory tree with no vendor/ anywhere in its ancestry (e.g. a Windows/POSIX temp dir via node:fs.mkdtempSync, NOT a subdirectory of this repo).
  2. Copy contracts.mjs's real resolution targets into that temp tree in two scenarios and assert both resolve correctly via the APRA_FLEET_SE_SCHEMAS_DIR env override AND via direct exercise of resolveSchemasDir()'s path candidates (from apra-fleet-bun.1):
     a. dist/agents/schemas/ present (simulates the root-bundled dist/auto-sprint.mjs layout) -- resolveSchemasDir() must find it.
     b. packages/apra-fleet-se/vendor/schemas/ present, dist/agents/schemas/ absent (simulates a standalone apra-fleet-se package install after its own build step ran) -- resolveSchemasDir() must find it.
  3. Assert a third scenario: neither bundled location present, no monorepo vendor/apra-pm ancestor either -- resolveSchemasDir() returns null and warnIfVendorFileUnexpectedlyMissing does NOT throw (existing graceful no-op-with-optional-warning contract preserved), and SCHEMAS.* fall back to the hand-written literals from apra-fleet-bun.3, still producing a working ajv-compiled validator (VALIDATORS export still fully populated, no crash at import time).
  4. Assert drift detection: run scripts/vendor-schemas.mjs --check (apra-fleet-bun.2) against a deliberately modified copy of the real submodule schemas and confirm it exits 1 and reports the specific changed file.

Framework: node:test + node:assert, matching every other file in packages/apra-fleet-se/test/. No new dependencies.

Acceptance criteria: all four scenarios above pass as separate subtests; test fails if apra-fleet-bun.1's path-precedence order is violated (e.g. dev fallback silently wins over a present bundled dir); test is part of the existing test/*.test.mjs glob so npm test picks it up automatically.
acceptance_criteria: New test file with 4 passing subtests covering both bundled layouts, the none-found fallback-to-literal case, and vendor-schemas.mjs --check drift detection; runs as part of npm test.
close_reason: Implemented and verified as part of the coordinated bun+3ns implementation pass; full apra-fleet-se suite green (220/220).

title: [impl] New build script: copy vendor/apra-pm schemas into packages/apra-fleet-se/vendor/schemas + --check drift detection
description:
Size: S. New file packages/apra-fleet-se/scripts/vendor-schemas.mjs. Reuse the existing pure helpers from packages/apra-fleet-se/scripts/regen-vendor-schema-fixtures.mjs (readVendorSchemaFiles, diffAgainstDest, writeFixtures) by importing them -- do not reimplement copy/diff logic.

Behavior (mirror regen-vendor-schema-fixtures.mjs's CLI shape):
  node packages/apra-fleet-se/scripts/vendor-schemas.mjs [--source <dir>] [--check]
  - Default source: this repo's vendor/apra-pm/agents/schemas (3 levels up from packages/apra-fleet-se/scripts/).
  - Default dest: packages/apra-fleet-se/vendor/schemas/ (the NEW destination decided in apra-fleet-bun.1 for the standalone/dev-package-tree layout).
  - --check: read-only, exits 1 with a diff report if dest is out of sync with source (added/changed/removed), for CI drift detection -- same contract as regen-vendor-schema-fixtures.mjs --check.
  - No --check: writes source into dest (writeFixtures), creating the dest dir if needed.

Wiring: add a packages/apra-fleet-se/package.json script, e.g. "vendor-schemas": "node scripts/vendor-schemas.mjs", and invoke it from the root prepublishOnly pipeline (root package.json's existing "prepublishOnly": "node scripts/vendor-pm.mjs && npm run build" -- add this as an additional step, e.g. "node scripts/vendor-pm.mjs && npm run vendor-schemas --workspace=@apralabs/apra-fleet-se && npm run build", or an equivalent that keeps vendor-pm.mjs's existing behavior unchanged). Do not commit the generated packages/apra-fleet-se/vendor/schemas/ output to git (gitignore it) -- it is a build artifact, same treatment as dist/, not a checked-in fixture (contrast with test/fixtures/vendor-apra-pm-schemas/, which IS committed).

Acceptance criteria: running the script against the real submodule populates packages/apra-fleet-se/vendor/schemas/*.json byte-for-byte identical to vendor/apra-pm/agents/schemas/*.json; --check is read-only and exits 1 on a deliberately introduced diff, 0 when in sync; wired into prepublishOnly; new dest dir added to .gitignore; suite green.
acceptance_criteria: New script exists, reuses regen-vendor-schema-fixtures.mjs helpers, --check works read-only with correct exit codes, wired into prepublishOnly, dest gitignored.

title: [impl] contracts.mjs: layout-aware, bundled-location-first schema resolution
description:
Size: M. Rewrite the schema-resolution section of packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/contracts.mjs (currently the single VENDOR_SCHEMAS_DIR constant at lines ~124-134, plus the stale TEMPORARY STATE comment block at lines ~93-134).

Decided destination paths (coordinated with apra-fleet-3ns.2, do not relitigate):
  1. Root-bundle layout: <dirname-of-this-module-when-bundled>/agents/schemas -- i.e. when this code ends up bundled into dist/auto-sprint.mjs, the sibling dist/agents/schemas/ directory that scripts/vendor-pm.mjs ALREADY populates today at root prepublishOnly (cpSync of vendor/apra-pm/agents -> dist/agents, which includes its schemas/ subdir). No new copy step needed for this layout -- reuse the existing artifact.
  2. Standalone/dev-package-tree layout: packages/apra-fleet-se/vendor/schemas/*.json -- a NEW location inside apra-fleet-se's own directory tree, populated by the new build script from apra-fleet-bun.2 (scripts/vendor-schemas.mjs). This is what a standalone npm install of @apralabs/apra-fleet-se (if it ever happens) or dev-mode-after-build resolves.
  3. Dev-monorepo fallback (existing behavior, kept, now explicitly a fallback not the primary path): vendor/apra-pm/agents/schemas/ three levels up from this file (path.join(__dirname, '..', '..', '..', 'vendor', 'apra-pm', 'agents', 'schemas')). Only reached when both 1 and 2 are absent. Emit a console.warn when this branch is used (new -- today it is silent), since it means neither bundled copy exists.

Resolution order (function resolveSchemasDir(), replaces the single VENDOR_SCHEMAS_DIR const):
  a. process.env.APRA_FLEET_SE_SCHEMAS_DIR if set (renamed from APRA_FLEET_SE_VENDOR_SCHEMAS_DIR_TEST_OVERRIDE -- no longer test-only in name/spirit, general override for any deployment; still fine for tests to set it). Used as-is, no further fallback attempted if set.
  b. Try each of paths 1, 2, 3 above via fs.existsSync, in that order; use the first that exists as a directory. Path 3 additionally emits the new warning.
  c. If none exist, resolveSchemasDir() returns null (same absent-directory semantics contracts.mjs already treats as expected/gradual -- warnIfVendorFileUnexpectedlyMissing's dirExists check already handles a null/absent dir as the quiet case). loadVendorSchema keeps returning null per-file in this case, same as today.

Also: rewrite the TEMPORARY STATE comment block (lines ~93-134) -- it currently describes a not-yet-bumped submodule state that is now stale (vendor/apra-pm IS initialized and bumped in this repo per the task context). Replace with an accurate description of the three-tier resolution order above and why it exists (packaging portability, apra-fleet-bun).

Acceptance criteria: resolveSchemasDir() unit-testable in isolation (exported); with APRA_FLEET_SE_SCHEMAS_DIR unset and no packages/apra-fleet-se/vendor/schemas/ present, falls through correctly to the vendor/apra-pm three-up path and warns once; with a fixture dir passed via the env var, uses it exclusively; existing SCHEMAS/VALIDATORS/loadVendorSchema/validateRoleInput behavior and export names unchanged; suite green.
acceptance_criteria: resolveSchemasDir() exported and unit-tested for all four branches (env override, dist/agents/schemas, package vendor/schemas, monorepo three-up-with-warning, none-found); no change to any existing export's name or shape; full apra-fleet-se suite green.
close_reason: Implemented and verified as part of the coordinated bun+3ns implementation pass; full apra-fleet-se suite green (220/220).

title: [ci] Root CI never runs the packages/* test suites -- vitest include pattern misses test/*.test.mjs node:test suites
description:
Findings: .github/workflows/ci.yml build-and-test runs only root 'npm test' = 'vitest run'; vitest.config.ts:7 includes ['tests/**/*.test.ts', 'packages/*/tests/**/*.test.ts'] -- but every workspace package keeps its tests in test/ (singular) as *.test.mjs run via node --test (see packages/apra-fleet-se/package.json test script, likewise -workflow and -client). Net effect: no apra-fleet-se / apra-fleet-workflow / apra-fleet-client test ever executes in CI. Shipping apra-fleet-se via the epic without CI coverage of its suites would be flying blind.

Scope (files): .github/workflows/ci.yml (build-and-test job), possibly root package.json scripts, possibly vitest.config.ts comment cleanup.

Work: add an explicit workspace test step (npm test --workspaces --if-present, or per-package steps) to build-and-test on all three OS legs; verify it passes on windows-latest (note prior Windows EBUSY teardown fix f66e621 -- the retry loop exists for exactly this); either remove or fix the dead 'packages/*/tests/**/*.test.ts' vitest include so it does not imply coverage that does not exist.

Acceptance criteria: a deliberately failing assertion temporarily added to a packages/apra-fleet-se test fails CI (verified locally via act or by reasoning through the workflow diff + a real CI run on the PR); all three OS legs green with the step added; no dead include pattern left implying phantom coverage.
close_reason: Implemented as part of the coordinated bun+3ns implementation pass; verified locally (real npm pack + clean-temp-prefix smoke test, full apra-fleet-se suite 220/220 green).

title: [impl] Bundle apra-fleet-se CLI into the root package: esbuild -> dist/auto-sprint.mjs, runner.js dist asset, root bin entry, prepublish + CI pack verification
description:
The core delivery child of the epic (design fixed there -- read apra-fleet-3ns first).

Scope (files): root package.json (bin, scripts), a new build script (or extension of scripts/vendor-pm.mjs / a sibling scripts/bundle-se.mjs), .github/workflows/ci.yml npm-publish job (dry-run pack verification greps), tests.

Acceptance criteria: npm pack tarball installed into a clean directory (no monorepo, no vendor/) exposes a working auto-sprint bin whose --help succeeds and whose server resolution finds the co-packaged dist/index.js; pack verification fails CI if any of the three artifacts is missing from the tarball; dev-mode (node packages/apra-fleet-se/bin/cli.mjs from the monorepo) still works unchanged; suite green.
