title: [impl] cli.mjs resolveFleetServerCommand: layout-aware fleet-server resolution + fail-loud when dist/index.js is absent
description:
Findings: packages/apra-fleet-se/bin/cli.mjs:45-46 -- when neither APRA_FLEET_SERVER_CMD nor APRA_FLEET_SERVER_BIN is set, the default resolves the fleet MCP server as path.resolve(__dirname,'..','..','..') + '/dist/index.js', i.e. three levels up from bin/, assuming the package sits at <repoRoot>/packages/apra-fleet-se inside a built monorepo checkout. Same bug class as apra-fleet-bun. In any installed context (npm global: node_modules/@apralabs/apra-fleet-se/bin -> three-up is the npm lib dir or a consumer project root; or the epic's bundled dist/auto-sprint.mjs where __dirname is <pkg>/dist) that path does not exist and StdioTransport.start() fails with an unhelpful spawn error before any sprint work begins. There is no existence check and no mention of the env overrides in the failure path.

Scope (files): packages/apra-fleet-se/bin/cli.mjs (resolveFleetServerCommand, and the engine.executeFile scriptPath at ~line 534 which has the sibling problem of resolving ../auto-sprint/runner.js relative to __dirname -- must work in both layouts too) + tests.

Work: (a) resolution order becomes: APRA_FLEET_SERVER_CMD > APRA_FLEET_SERVER_BIN > same-package sibling (bundled layout: path.join(__dirname,'index.js') when running from dist/) > dev-monorepo three-up fallback; (b) fs.existsSync check on the resolved entry BEFORE constructing StdioTransport, with an actionable error naming both env overrides and the expected locations; (c) make the runner.js scriptPath resolution layout-aware the same way (dev: ../auto-sprint/runner.js; bundled: the dist asset location decided by the epic's bundle child).

Acceptance criteria: unit tests cover all four resolution branches plus the missing-entry error message; simulated installed layout (temp dir with dist/auto-sprint.mjs + dist/index.js, no packages/ or monorepo root) resolves the sibling server; dev monorepo behavior unchanged; suite green.
close_reason: Implemented as part of the coordinated bun+3ns implementation pass; verified locally (real npm pack + clean-temp-prefix smoke test, full apra-fleet-se suite 220/220 green).

title: [EPIC] Ship apra-fleet-se auto-sprint via the apra-fleet npm package (packaging + install design)
description:
Problem: packages/apra-fleet-se (the NEW auto-sprint: bin/cli.mjs exposed as the 'auto-sprint' binary, auto-sprint/runner.js engine script, workspace deps @apralabs/apra-fleet-workflow + @apralabs/apra-fleet-client) is not part of any shipped artifact today. No files field, no publish workflow references it, not a dependency of the root @apralabs/apra-fleet package; docs/install.md only covers the OLD Claude-only auto-sprint (vendor/apra-pm/.claude/workflows/auto-sprint.js copied to ~/.claude/workflows/ -- OUT OF SCOPE here, untouched). Its production code contains monorepo-shape assumptions that break silently the moment it is installed outside a full git clone: contracts.mjs REPO_ROOT three-up vendor/apra-pm schema resolution (apra-fleet-bun, reparented under this epic) and bin/cli.mjs:45-46 resolving the fleet MCP server as <three-up>/dist/index.js.

Design (decided -- do not relitigate in child tasks):

- Delivery: bundle the new auto-sprint INTO the root @apralabs/apra-fleet npm package, NOT standalone npm publish of @apralabs/apra-fleet-se (+workflow+client). Rationale: (1) auto-sprint is inoperable without the apra-fleet MCP server -- cli.mjs's first act is spawning it over stdio -- so co-shipping in one package removes the server-resolution problem structurally (sibling dist/index.js); (2) avoids publishing and version-locking three additional npm packages whose only consumer is apra-fleet-se (workflow/client are currently unpublished, no files/publishConfig, deps declared as '*'); (3) reuses the existing, proven prepublishOnly vendoring precedent (scripts/vendor-pm.mjs copies vendor/apra-pm content into dist/ so it travels with the tarball) and the existing ci.yml npm-publish job (version lockstep, dry-run pack verification) -- extend, don't build a second pipeline. Standalone publish of the workspace packages remains possible later; nothing in this design precludes it.
- Mechanism: a build step (esbuild, already a devDependency, node22 target, ESM, shebang) bundles packages/apra-fleet-se/bin/cli.mjs plus its workspace dependencies into dist/auto-sprint.mjs; root package.json gains bin: {"auto-sprint": "dist/auto-sprint.mjs"}; dist/ is already in the root files whitelist. IMPORTANT: runner.js is loaded at runtime via engine.executeFile(path.join(__dirname,'../auto-sprint/runner.js')) (cli.mjs:534), i.e. read from disk and fed to the workflow engine, NOT importable -- the bundle step must ship runner.js (and anything executeFile needs) as a dist asset alongside the bundle, and cli.mjs's scriptPath resolution must work in both layouts.
- Vendored schemas: copied by the same prepublish pipeline (extend scripts/vendor-pm.mjs) into a location inside the shipped tree; contracts.mjs resolution reworked per apra-fleet-bun (prerequisite child of this epic). apra-fleet-bun owns the contracts.mjs resolution strategy + build-time drift detection; this epic owns where the bundled copy lives and wiring it into prepublishOnly/CI pack checks -- coordinated so the two do not duplicate.
- SEA binary embedding of auto-sprint: deferred child (P3) -- npm delivery first; the SEA asset-manifest mechanism (gen-sea-config.mjs) exists as precedent.

Out of scope: vendor/apra-pm/** and the old auto-sprint install path; server-side changes; publishing @apralabs/apra-fleet-workflow / -client to npm.
close_reason: 6/7 children closed: apra-fleet-se auto-sprint now bundles into the root @apralabs/apra-fleet npm package (dist/auto-sprint.mjs via esbuild, dist/auto-sprint-runner.mjs runner asset, dist/agents/schemas already-existing artifact reused). cli.mjs's server/runner resolution and contracts.mjs's schema resolution are both layout-aware (dev monorepo / bundled / standalone). CI extended: pack verification for the 3 new artifacts, a real clean-temp-prefix install smoke test, and the previously-missing packages/* node:test suite execution. Workspace packages marked private. Docs updated (install.md, npm-packaging.md, cli-reference.md). Full apra-fleet-se suite green (220/220). Remaining child apra-fleet-3ns.6 (SEA binary embedding) is explicitly deferred (P3) -- npm delivery ships first per the epic's own design; not a blocker.

title: apra-fleet-se's contracts.mjs resolves vendor/apra-pm outside its own package directory -- breaks when packaged/installed standalone
description:
Findings: contracts.mjs resolves the vendored schema directory as path.join(__dirname, '..', '..', '..', 'vendor', 'apra-pm', 'agents', 'schemas') -- three levels up from packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/, reaching outside packages/apra-fleet-se's own directory tree to the monorepo root. This only resolves correctly when apra-fleet-se is run from inside a full monorepo git clone with vendor/apra-pm checked out as an adjacent submodule.

Confirmed today apra-fleet-se is NOT part of the shipped apra-fleet installer (no files field in its package.json, no publish workflow references it, not a dependency of the root apra-fleet package; docs/install.md only describes the OLD vendor/apra-pm/.claude/workflows/auto-sprint.js being copied to ~/.claude/workflows/). So this is not live-broken in production today. But it IS a real, unresolved design gap for this package's intended future: the moment apra-fleet-se is npm-published standalone or bundled into an installer, vendor/ will not travel with it (npm publish never includes files outside a package's own directory tree via ../../..). At that point contracts.mjs's whole-directory-absence case -- currently treated as expected, silent, permanent graceful degradation to the hand-written fallback schemas (by design, from apra-fleet-unw2.5's observability work, which only warns on a specific missing file, not whole-directory absence) -- would silently and permanently run on stale fallback schemas forever in any installed deployment, with no warning that it has diverged from the real vendored contract.

Scope (files): packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/contracts.mjs (schema resolution strategy), packages/apra-fleet-se/package.json (packaging config), likely a new build step, tests.

Work: design and implement a packaging-safe schema resolution strategy. The standard pattern is to bundle/copy the real vendored schema JSON files INTO packages/apra-fleet-se's own directory tree at build time (not resolve them from vendor/apra-pm at runtime), so they travel with the package regardless of how/where it's installed. This requires: (a) a build step that copies vendor/apra-pm/agents/schemas/*.json into a location inside packages/apra-fleet-se at build/publish time, with drift detection against the live submodule (build-time, not runtime); (b) contracts.mjs updated to resolve schemas from that bundled location instead of reaching out to vendor/apra-pm directly; (c) decide whether the hand-written fallback literals (section 3) are still needed once schemas are reliably bundled at build time, or whether they become dead weight; (d) coordinate with whatever future work brings apra-fleet-se into the actual apra-fleet installer, since this fix is a prerequisite for that.

Acceptance criteria: a simulated standalone install (package directory with no ancestor vendor/ present) still resolves the correct, non-stale vendored schemas; build-time drift detection catches a submodule bump that changes schema content; suite green.
notes: 2026-07-12 scope alignment (packaging epic apra-fleet-3ns): reparented under apra-fleet-3ns, which owns the overall delivery design (bundle into the root @apralabs/apra-fleet npm package; see epic description -- decided, do not relitigate here). Division of labor to avoid duplication: THIS bead owns (1) contracts.mjs schema-resolution strategy (bundled-location-first, layout-aware, env override APRA_FLEET_SE_SCHEMAS_DIR retained; dev-monorepo vendor/ fallback with warning) and (2) the build-time copy of vendor/apra-pm/agents/schemas/*.json into the shipped tree WITH drift detection -- implement it as an extension of the existing scripts/vendor-pm.mjs prepublishOnly precedent, and (3) the fate of the hand-written fallback literals. Epic child apra-fleet-3ns.2 owns WHERE the bundled copy lives in dist/, the esbuild bundling of cli.mjs itself, and the CI pack-verification greps -- coordinate the destination path with that bead before implementing. This bead blocks apra-fleet-3ns.2.
close_reason: Standalone-packaging schema resolution bug fixed: contracts.mjs's resolveSchemasDir() is now layout-aware and bundled-location-first (env override -> dist/agents/schemas -> package-local vendor/schemas -> monorepo dev fallback with warning -> hand-written literals). New build script scripts/vendor-schemas.mjs populates the package-local copy with --check drift detection. Fallback literals kept as a documented permanent last-resort, not a temporary state. All 4 pre-existing tests updated for the renamed APRA_FLEET_SE_SCHEMAS_DIR env var; new standalone-install integration test added. Full apra-fleet-se suite green (220/220).

title: [impl] runner.js: replace literal Claude model names with tier keywords (FIXED_ROLE_TIER + planner prompt, no migration shim)
description:
REVISED 2026-07-12: dropped the legacy-model normalization shim (no in-flight beads need it -- confirmed via `bd list`, zero sprint-task beads carry a legacy literal model value from a live auto-sprint run) and corrected the "literal model ID" framing (see epic apra-fleet-dv5 description for the full corrected reasoning: a literal like a Gemini/OpenCode model string is legitimate and meaningful to its own provider, not "non-portable" -- the flaw was Claude-specific literals used as if they were universal).

Scope (files): packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/runner.js only (plus its direct unit-test seams; full test updates are the sibling [test] task).

Work:
(a) Replace FIXED_ROLE_MODEL (runner.js ~87) with FIXED_ROLE_TIER: planner='premium', 'plan-reviewer'='premium', reviewer='premium', deployer='standard', 'integ-test-runner'='standard', harvester='standard'. Update the N10 doc comment block above it: explain tiers resolve per member server-side (execute-prompt.ts resolveModelForTier), keep the budget-caveat pointer but update it to reference the new get_member_model_pricing mechanism (sibling task) rather than declaring exact costing impossible.
(b) NO normalization/legacy-mapping layer. opts.model for fixed roles is simply the FIXED_ROLE_TIER value. For doer dispatches, modelByBeadId (~1617) and the streak model resolution (~1651-1655) pass the bead's metadata.model value straight through UNCHANGED -- it is already either a tier keyword (planner-authored, the expected case) or, if a human/other tool wrote a literal model ID directly into metadata, that literal passes through as-is (still meaningful to whichever provider the target member actually is -- not rejected, not rewritten). A bead with no model metadata still resolves to undefined (unpriced dispatch), unchanged.
(c) Fix buildPlannerPrompt (~511-518): instruct the planner to write EXACTLY one of 'cheap', 'standard', 'premium' as the metadata model value (fixes the current 'cheap-tier, standard-tier, premium-tier' wording, which matches neither the server tier keywords nor pricing.mjs's keys).
(d) All six fixed-role dispatch sites (~1125, 1441, 1453, 1582, 1825, 1851, 2033, 2085) keep passing opts.model -- now always a tier keyword, no other change needed (agent() already passes opts.model through verbatim).

Acceptance criteria: no literal 'opus'/'sonnet'/'haiku'/'fable' string remains in runner.js outside comments/examples explaining the old convention for historical context; FIXED_ROLE_TIER contains only 'cheap'/'standard'/'premium' values; planner prompt contains the three exact keywords 'cheap', 'standard', 'premium' and none of 'cheap-tier'/'standard-tier'/'premium-tier'; a doer bead with metadata model='minimax-abab' (an OpenCode-meaningful literal) still dispatches with opts.model='minimax-abab' unchanged -- no rewriting, no warning, no rejection; a bead with no model metadata still dispatches unpriced (undefined) exactly as today.
close_reason: runner.js FIXED_ROLE_TIER + planner prompt wording landed in commit 59a5958

title: [EPIC] Tier-based model selection in apra-fleet-se auto-sprint (de-hardcode literal Claude model names)
description:
REVISED 2026-07-12 per project-owner corrections (see bd comment log on this issue for the correction text). This description supersedes the original.

Problem (unchanged): packages/apra-fleet-se auto-sprint selects models using hardcoded literal Claude family names ('fable', 'opus', 'sonnet', 'haiku') in runner.js (FIXED_ROLE_MODEL, per-bead --metadata '{"model": ...}' passthrough) and pricing.mjs. The fleet's transport layer (execute_prompt, src/tools/execute-prompt.ts:293-313) resolves the tier keywords 'cheap'/'standard'/'premium' per member via register_member model_tiers -- that abstraction is what makes a mixed-provider fleet (Claude, Gemini, Codex, Copilot, OpenCode, ...) work. By passing literal Claude names, auto-sprint bypasses the tier layer: a hardcoded 'opus' dispatched to a non-Claude member is passed through verbatim as a literal model ID that means nothing to that provider.

CORRECTED framing (was wrong in v1): a literal (non-tier-keyword) model string is NOT inherently "non-portable" or a mere power-user escape hatch -- it is a perfectly legitimate, meaningful identifier for whichever provider actually uses that model family (e.g. a Gemini "flash-2.0" string is meaningful to a Gemini member, a "minimax-abab" string is meaningful to an OpenCode member backed by that model). The actual flaw is narrower and more precise: auto-sprint hardcodes CLAUDE-SPECIFIC literal names as if they were universal tier-independent identifiers, with zero awareness of which provider a given dispatch target actually is. The fix is to stop emitting Claude-specific literals and use the provider-agnostic tier vocabulary instead -- not to declare literals unportable in general.

Design (revised, decided -- do not relitigate in child tasks):
- Canonical per-bead metadata value is a tier keyword: 'cheap' | 'standard' | 'premium' (the exact strings execute_prompt resolves per member). Field name stays 'model' (vendored planner.md/plan-reviewer.md read the 'model' metadata key; vendor/apra-pm is out of scope, untouched).
- CLEAN CUTOVER, no migration shim: `bd list` confirmed zero in-flight sprint-task beads carrying a legacy literal value from a live auto-sprint planner run (the only beads found with metadata model=opus/sonnet/haiku/fable are meta-level beads about THIS fix itself and unrelated packaging epics -- not auto-sprint-planner-authored sprint tasks). No normalizeModelTier() legacy-mapping layer. The planner and FIXED_ROLE_TIER simply emit/use tier keywords directly, full stop.
- FIXED_ROLE_MODEL becomes FIXED_ROLE_TIER: planner/plan-reviewer/reviewer -> 'premium' (vendored contract literally says "always use model: premium"), deployer/integ-test-runner/harvester -> 'standard'.
- 'fable' collapses into 'premium', now CONFIRMED (not just inferred): the project owner confirmed fable is never dispatched as a fleet-member model through execute_prompt at all -- it's used directly by the orchestrating Claude Code session, never as something a fleet member runs. No fourth tier.
- Literal model IDs remain a valid, DOCUMENTED-AS-SUCH input to opts.model for a caller who already knows the target member's provider and wants a specific model family from it (matches the client API's own tier-keyword-OR-model-ID contract). Auto-sprint's planner/FIXED_ROLE_TIER just never emits Claude-specific literals as if they were tier-independent defaults; that's the whole fix.

Cost/budget design -- SUPERSEDED, real per-member pricing now in scope (was: "impossible, honest tier-band estimate only"):
Per-member tier->concrete-model resolution (src/tools/execute-prompt.ts's resolveModelForTier(), ~line 115, and each provider's modelTiers()/modelForTier()) is DETERMINISTIC, config-driven, and known to the server ahead of any dispatch -- it does not require a per-dispatch echo-back to know what "member X's premium tier" resolves to. This means real (not estimated) cost lookup is achievable client-side by asking the server, once (or periodically/cached), "what does this member's tier resolve to and what does that cost". Verified: src/services/cloud/cost.ts exists but is EC2-instance hourly-rate cost estimation (GPU/general-purpose AWS instance types), unrelated domain -- no existing LLM token-pricing infrastructure in src/ to build on; a new small module is needed.
New design:
1. New server module (src/services/model-pricing.ts or similar): a PROVIDER_MODEL_PRICING table (provider -> concrete model id -> $/1M-token prompt/completion), same "unknown model returns null, never fabricate" discipline as apra-fleet-workflow's pricing.mjs. Exposes getMemberModelPricing(agent, provider) that resolves each of the member's three tiers to a concrete model (reusing resolveModelForTier's exact resolution order) and looks up its price, returning `{ cheap: {model, promptPrice, completionPrice} | null, standard: {...} | null, premium: {...} | null }` per member.
2. New MCP tool `get_member_model_pricing` (wired via src/services/tool-registry.ts, same server.tool()/wrapTool() pattern as the existing `list_members`), taking member_id or member_name, returning the above structure as JSON. apra-fleet-client's existing generic `McpClient.callTool(name, args)` (packages/apra-fleet-client/src/client/client.mjs:143) needs zero new plumbing to call it.
3. apra-fleet-workflow budget tracking calls `get_member_model_pricing` once per member per run (cacheable -- tier->model resolution is static for the run's duration), and prices real dispatches against the ACTUAL resolved model's real rate instead of a tier-band guess. Falls back to the existing tier-band estimate (pricing.mjs) only when the tool call fails, the member/provider has no priced entry, or (older/offline) fleet servers don't expose the tool yet -- so this degrades gracefully rather than hard-requiring the new tool.
4. This does NOT require or depend on per-dispatch echo-back (that remains a separate, still-unimplemented idea for a different problem -- confirming what a provider ACTUALLY ran when a member's config could theoretically diverge from its registered tiers -- but is no longer necessary for the primary cost-accuracy goal, since tier resolution is deterministic from registered config).

Out of scope: vendor/apra-pm/** (the old auto-sprint.js there is Claude-only by design). Small additions to the apra-fleet SERVER (src/**) ARE in scope for this cost-resolution mechanism only (explicit project-owner permission) -- do not otherwise wander into unrelated server-side changes.
close_reason: All six children (tier-based model selection, real per-member pricing) closed on feat/fleet-reorg

comments.author: Azure Pipeline
comments.text: Revised 2026-07-12 per project-owner corrections: (1) dropped legacy-model migration shim -- no in-flight beads need it; (2) corrected 'literal model ID = non-portable' framing to 'provider-specific, meaningful only if caller knows target provider'; (3) replaced 'impossible, tier-band estimate only' cost design with a real per-member pricing mechanism (new get_member_model_pricing MCP tool backed by deterministic tier->model resolution, confirmed no existing model-costs.json/pricing infra in src/cost.ts which is EC2-only). fable-collapses-to-premium is now CONFIRMED by the owner (fable is never fleet-dispatched) rather than inferred. Full text in description.

title: [B/N4] Multi-member topology: branch-ensure on every pool member + precondition validation + honest support statement
description:
Findings: feedback-reassessment.md N4 (HIGH -- branch ensured on orchestrator member only; doers/reviewers on other members work on wrong branches; beads state assumed shared).

Decision (made in plan.md, do not re-decide): ensure-everywhere + validate-shared-state; NO cross-member sync layer this round (deferred -- needs a real multi-member fleet to validate).

Scope (files): packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/runner.js, packages/apra-fleet-se/bin/cli.mjs, packages/apra-fleet-se/test/advanced-mock-runner-test.mjs, docs.

Work: (a) dispatch the git branch-ensure to EVERY member in the union of orchestrator/doer/reviewer pools before the first doer round (and re-ensure after reopen rounds); (b) CLI-precondition topology check: compare git rev-parse / beads DB identity across configured members and refuse to start on mismatch unless single-member; (c) document that single-member (or verified shared-workspace) is the only SUPPORTED real-fleet mode until cross-member sync exists; (d) extend the mock so git/gh commands and beads state can be modeled per-member, and add a 2-member regression test that fails on the pre-fix single-member branch-ensure.

Acceptance criteria: 2-member mock sprint shows branch-ensure dispatched to both members; topology-mismatch precondition test refuses to start with a clear message; docs updated; all existing tests green.
notes:
Adversarial review PASSED at worktree C:/akhil/git/wt-unw2-4, branch tmp/unw2-4-multi-member-topology, HEAD a168336c88557944ec81cf5d79e1d32a41a51147 (verified via rev-parse, matches doer's report).

Verified independently (not from doer's self-report):
1. branchEnsureMembers = dedup union of orchestratorMember + getMembersForRole(ROLE_DOER) + getMembersForRole(ROLE_REVIEWER) (runner.js ~L788-792), dispatched via a sequential for-loop to EVERY member in that set in "Sprint Setup / Ensure Sprint Branch" (runner.js ~L819-828), before the cycle/doer loop begins.
2. reEnsureBranchOnMembers() uses non-destructive `git checkout <branch>` with failSoft:true, called at the top of cycle>1 only. Confirmed the failSoft path in FleetWorkflow.command() (workflow/index.mjs L941-947) returns {ok:false,error} instead of throwing, but still emits console.error + activity:end(success:false), so a silent-checkout-failure isn't fully invisible (goes to logs/journal even though runner.js doesn't add its own extra warning). Reasoning for non-destructive-only is sound given the explicit no-sync-layer decision in plan.md and is honestly caveated in code comments.
3. checkMemberTopology({members, getIdentity}) traced: single-member short-circuits to ok without calling getIdentity; 2+ members compare git rev-parse HEAD (wired in cli.mjs); refuses with a message naming all members/signals on mismatch, and refuses (treating as unresolved) if any getIdentity throws. Wired into cli.mjs step 4 (after member validation), exits via process.exit(1) on !topology.ok -- confirmed exit code path, not just a console.error.
4. Adversarial "coincidental same-commit, independent checkouts" scenario: doc comments in runner.js (checkMemberTopology header) AND docs/architecture.md AND auto-sprint-diagram.md all explicitly state this is a "best-effort shared-workspace heuristic, NOT a guarantee of ongoing shared state" -- honestly scoped, not oversold.
5. Mock harness: commandLogDetailed and memberGitState genuinely model per-member git state (regex-parsed from actual dispatched commands); bd DB remains a single shared tempDir regardless of member count (confirmed via grep -- no per-member DB branching), so the existing ~140 single-workspace tests are unaffected by construction.
6. Reproduced the tripwire myself: reverted runner.js to orchestrator-only ensure in the worktree, ran advanced-mock-runner-test.mjs directly -- it failed with exactly the expected two assertions ("Expected the sprint-branch ensure to be dispatched to member 'm2' too" and "Expected member 'm2' git state to have the sprint branch ensured, got: null"). Restored via `git checkout --`, confirmed `git status --porcelain` clean, and reran to confirm PASS again.
7. checkMemberTopology tests: 4 distinct scenarios confirmed (single-member trivial-pass with getIdentity never called; agreeing signals pass; disagreeing signals refuse+name both members/signals; unresolved signal (thrown error) refuses+names failing member/reason).
8. Full apra-fleet-se suite: 147 tests / 146 pass / 0 fail / 1 skip (exact match to expected), via `npm test` in packages/apra-fleet-se. Repo `npm run build` also green.
9. Docs (architecture.md, auto-sprint-diagram.md) honestly state single-member / verified-shared-workspace as the ONLY supported real-fleet mode, explicitly call out independent-checkouts as NOT supported, and flag the topology check as best-effort-at-start rather than an ongoing guarantee.
10. Scope check: diff touches exactly the 5 stated files (docs/architecture.md, packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/docs/auto-sprint-diagram.md, packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/runner.js, packages/apra-fleet-se/bin/cli.mjs, packages/apra-fleet-se/test/advanced-mock-runner-test.mjs). No cross-member sync layer was quietly added -- matches plan.md's N4 decision (ensure-everywhere + validate-shared-state, no sync this round).

Minor (non-blocking) observation for a future round: reEnsureBranchOnMembers() discards the failSoft {ok,error} result without runner.js-level logging of which member failed re-checkout; relies entirely on the underlying workflow engine's console.error/activity:end for visibility. Not a correctness defect given the explicit no-sync-layer scope decision, just worth a follow-up if per-member checkout failures need operator-facing surfacing.

Verdict: APPROVED. Ready for merge into feat/fleet-reorg from worktree C:/akhil/git/wt-unw2-4 (branch tmp/unw2-4-multi-member-topology, SHA a168336c88557944ec81cf5d79e1d32a41a51147). Orchestrator to merge -- reviewer did not merge.

title: [B/N3] Close shell-injection channel: reviewer newTasks -> bd create interpolation
description:
Findings: feedback-reassessment.md N3 (HIGH -- LLM output interpolated into a member shell; A7 class reintroduced by the A3 fix).

Scope (files): packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/runner.js (newTasks handling, ~lines 1039-1047) + tests.

Work: validate BEFORE interpolation -- priority must match /^P[0-4]$/ (typed/logged rejection otherwise); title and description allowlisted to a safe character class (allowlisting over escaping, given Windows/POSIX member-shell divergence); a rejected newTask is logged and surfaced in the run summary, never executed; nothing outside the allowlist ever reaches command().

Acceptance criteria: regression test hand-constructs payloads with $(...), backticks, and trailing backslash in title/description and a bogus priority, and asserts no shell metacharacters reach command() and the sprint continues (rejection is non-fatal). Adversarial reviewer must independently construct at least one payload not in the doer's test set.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: Azure Pipeline
comments.text:
Adversarial review APPROVED. Independently re-verified SHA 4d15923414c09ef0f38ba4c6317f19c2bcd9db7d (git rev-parse HEAD in worktree matches report). Confirmed validateNewTask() gates title/description against /^[A-Za-z0-9 .,:;!?()'_/-]+$/ and priority against /^P[0-4]$/ strictly BEFORE interpolation into command(); rejections are logged, collected in rejectedNewTasks, surfaced in the Final Review evidence prompt, and non-fatal (loop continues via `continue`). Traced the actual call path in the verdict.newTasks loop -- confirmed only validated {title, description, priority} reach command(), never the raw newTask object.

Reviewed newtasks-validation.test.mjs (13 unit tests: $(...), backticks, trailing backslash, literal double-quote, bare $, bogus priorities) and the new advanced-mock-runner-test.mjs scenario (6 unsafe + 1 safe newTask from a mocked reviewer), asserted via a real commandLog spy on executeCommand (not just "no throw") that no dangerous payload/metacharacter ever reaches a dispatched command.

Per the adversarial-reviewer requirement, independently constructed and ran my OWN payloads (not in the doer's test set) directly against the real validateNewTask() in the worktree:
- Windows cmd.exe metacharacters: "Task & del /f /q C:\*", "Task | whoami", "run %USERPROFILE%\evil.bat", "Task ^& calc", ">"/"<" redirects -- all REJECTED (allowlist design defeats these by construction, despite them not being backtick/$()-shaped)
- POSIX single-quote escape trick: "'; rm -rf ~ #" -- REJECTED
- Embedded NUL byte -- REJECTED
- Unicode RTL override control char (U+202E) spoofing -- REJECTED
- Fullwidth dollar-sign lookalike (U+FF04) -- REJECTED
- (non-blocking note) 200,000-char title was ACCEPTED -- no length cap; DoS-adjacent, not an injection vector, out of scope for N3 but worth a follow-up if desired.

Ran the full apra-fleet-se suite myself: tests 147, pass 146, fail 0, skipped 1 -- matches the doer's claim exactly. git status --porcelain clean. Confirmed only 3 files touched: runner.js, test/advanced-mock-runner-test.mjs, test/newtasks-validation.test.mjs.

Worktree: C:\akhil\git\wt-unw2-3, branch tmp/unw2-3-newtasks-injection-fix, SHA 4d15923414c09ef0f38ba4c6317f19c2bcd9db7d. Ready for orchestrator to merge into feat/fleet-reorg (reviewer does not merge).

Roles with no input schema are no-ops by design. Harvester: include with an explicit expected-fail/skip marker referencing the N12 wiring issue (apra-fleet-unw2.10) -- flip to green there.

title: [A/N1] Realign runner.js to the vendored role contracts: planner --metadata model convention, plan-reviewer scope, doer branch

What is CORRECT (verified independently against the real vendored files at wt-unw13/vendor/apra-pm, tmp/unw13-vendor-agent-defs):

1. buildPlanReviewerPrompt({ targetIssues, goal }) supplies a STRING scope ("Sprint root / scope to review ... sprint root issue id(s) <ids>, goal priority <goal>. Review only the features and tasks under this scope."), matching plan-reviewer-input.json's `required: ["scope"], scope: {type: "string"}` exactly. Call site at the plan-reviewer dispatch now uses this builder instead of the old context-free 'Review the plan per your agent contract.' string. Golden transcript seq 4/7 confirm this text is actually emitted.
2. buildDoerPrompt now takes and forwards `branch` (bound to validated.branch, which is validated as a required non-empty string + branch-name-shape-checked earlier in the file). This satisfies doer-input.json's required "branch" (string) key. Golden transcript seq 12/22 confirm "Sprint track branch to work on: auto-sprint/mock-sprint" is actually in the dispatched prompt.
3. Mock plan-reviewer/doer changes in advanced-mock-runner-test.mjs are REAL contract gates, not cosmetic: mock plan-reviewer returns CHANGES_NEEDED with taskAssignments:[] when scope/epic id is absent from the prompt; mock doer returns BLOCKED/closedIds:[] when the "Sprint track branch to work on:" marker is absent. New assertions on dispatched prompt content (planReviewerDispatches, doerDispatches loops) would fail on revert.
4. Golden transcript diff is exactly 6 lines (2 planner, 2 plan-reviewer, 2 doer dispatch prompts), text is sensible, no garbling, no debug leakage, wrapUntrustedBlock-wrapped feedback blocks are untouched.
5. Own test suite (packages/apra-fleet-se, `npm test`) run independently on this worktree: 95/95 PASS, 27 suites, exit 0.

BLOCKING FINDING -- planner --metadata fix (divergence 1) is NOT fully clean, and the N13 tripwire (this issue's own stated acceptance criterion: "the N13 tripwire issue ... passes against this fix") does NOT go fully green:

I brought test/runner-role-input-contract.test.mjs (merged separately into feat/fleet-reorg via apra-fleet-unw2.2, not present in this worktree's history) into a SCRATCH merge of this branch (05c3e6c) + feat/fleet-reorg tip (eefdf8a), per the review instructions, and ran it with `node --test`. Result: 7/9 suites pass (plan-reviewer divergence-2 subtest PASSES, doer branch divergence-3 subtest PASSES), but the **planner suite FAILS**:

  not ok 2 - buildPlannerPrompt uses the --metadata model-tier convention, not --notes (N1 divergence 1)
  error: 'planner prompt must NOT use the old --notes model-tier convention ...; it still does -- N1 divergence 1.'

Root cause (confirmed by reading runner.js:296-303 directly): buildPlannerPrompt's new instructional sentence is functionally correct (it DOES instruct `--metadata '{"model": "<tier>"}'`) but its own explanatory clause still contains the literal substring "--notes":

  '... this is the ONLY location the model tier is recorded (not --notes, not a METADATA-section comment), per planner.md Step 3.'

This is not a JS comment -- it is inside the `lines.push(...)` call, i.e. it is part of the ACTUAL text dispatched to the planner (confirmed in the golden transcript diff, seq 3/6: "...this is the ONLY location the model tier is recorded (not --notes, not a METADATA-section comment), per planner.md Step 3."). The N13 tripwire's assertion (test/runner-role-input-contract.test.mjs:174-178) does a straightforward substring check for "--notes" anywhere in the buildPlannerPrompt function body/output and correctly flags this residual mention, regardless of surrounding negation. Whether or not a real planner LLM would misread "(not --notes...)" as an instruction to use --notes, the issue's own stated acceptance criterion is that the N13 tripwire test passes against this fix, and it currently does not -- 1 of 9 subtests red.

Fix needed: reword the planner prompt's explanatory clause so the string "--notes" (or "--notes=") does not appear at all, e.g. drop the parenthetical entirely, or phrase it without the flag literal ("...this is the ONLY location the model tier is recorded, per planner.md Step 3." with the "not X, not Y" caveat removed or paraphrased without the literal flag token). Everything else in this fix (plan-reviewer scope shape/schema match, doer branch wiring, mock contract enforcement, golden transcript regeneration, file hygiene) is correct and does not need rework -- only the planner prompt's residual "--notes" substring needs a one-line wording fix.
