title: [test] mock integ report with stale/absent part-2 SHA is rejected by engine report validation
description:
Regression pin for eft.55 / impl eft.55.2. Framework: existing mock-sprint / unit test suite in packages/apra-fleet-se.

What to test / how to assert: feed the engine's integ report validation a mock integ report whose part-2 (smoke test) result cites a stale SHA (not the deploy-verified SHA) and, separately, one that lacks the SHA entirely. Assert both are REJECTED as inconclusive (the phase fails distinctly from pass/fail), not accepted as valid evidence. Assert the happy-path report (part-2 SHA matches the deploy-verified SHA) is accepted unchanged.

Done / acceptance criteria:
- A mock integ report with a stale part-2 SHA is rejected as inconclusive.
- A mock integ report with an absent part-2 SHA is rejected as inconclusive.
- A matching-SHA report is accepted unchanged. ASCII only.
acceptance_criteria: Engine report validation rejects a mock integ report citing a stale part-2 SHA and one lacking the SHA as inconclusive (distinct from pass/fail); a matching-SHA report is accepted unchanged.
close_reason: Added full-schema mock integ report regression pin (stale SHA, absent SHA, matching SHA) in packages/apra-fleet-se/test/part2-sha-freshness.test.mjs; all 15 tests pass.

title: [test] newTask with '=' and '&' round-trips intact; residual-failure finding appended to parent notes; injection lands as literal argv
description:
Regression pin for eft.56 / impl eft.56.1. Framework: existing unit-test suite (vitest / node:test) in packages/apra-fleet-se.

What to test / how to assert: (1) a newTask whose description contains '=' and '&' round-trips into bd create successfully via the --body-file/stdin path with content intact. (2) A finding that still fails validation is appended to the target parent bead's notes verbatim instead of being dropped, and the run log says so. (3) Injection regression: a description containing '$(rm -rf /)' and backticks lands as LITERAL TEXT in the created bead and is never executed -- proven by asserting the spawned command argv (no shell-string interpolation of the description).

Done / acceptance criteria:
- '=' and '&' description round-trips intact via --body-file/stdin.
- Residual-validation-failure finding is appended verbatim to the parent bead notes and the run log records it.
- Injection payload lands as literal argv text (asserted on spawned argv), never executed. ASCII only.
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests: '='/'&' description round-trips intact via --body-file/stdin; residual-failure finding appended verbatim to parent notes and logged; '$(rm -rf /)'+backticks land as literal argv (asserted on spawned argv), never executed.
close_reason: Added test/newtask-body-file-roundtrip.test.mjs pinning '='/'&' description round-trip via --body-file, verbatim notes-fallback on residual validation failure (with log assertion), and injection payload landing as literal argv/file text (never in the constructed command string). All tests pass alongside existing newtasks-validation.test.mjs, dispatch-safety-guard.test.mjs, and mock-sprint-develop-injection.test.mjs.

title: [test] mock-sprint: diverged-beads D-pull aborts before any git branch/PR, main log names remediation, terminal state carries reason
description:
Regression pin for eft.58 / impl eft.58.1. Framework: existing mock-sprint node:test suite in packages/apra-fleet-se.

What to test / how to assert: a command() harness whose 'bd dolt pull' returns the exact conflict error (child_counters/dependencies/issues merge conflict) must abort BEFORE any git branch/PR command is issued -- assert no ensure-branch command ran. Assert a main-log line matching /beads DB diverged/ that names the remediation. Assert the persisted terminal state message carries the same reason string. Prove (grep/log assertion, not a stderr assertion) that the cause is present in the main log, not only the raw stack. Assert the happy path is unchanged (existing mock-sprint suites stay green).

Done / acceptance criteria:
- Conflict-returning D-pull aborts before any git branch/PR command (assert no ensure-branch command ran).
- Main-log line matches /beads DB diverged/ and names remediation; terminal state message carries the same reason.
- Cause proven present via a log assertion (not stderr). Happy path unchanged. ASCII only.
acceptance_criteria: Mock-sprint test: conflict D-pull aborts before any git branch/PR command; main-log /beads DB diverged/ line names remediation; terminal state carries the reason; cause proven via log assertion (not stderr); happy-path suites stay green.
close_reason: Added mock-sprint end-to-end regression test (mock-sprint-beads-health-gate-diverged.test.mjs) pinning eft.58.1's pre-flight beads-health gate: conflict D-pull aborts before any git branch/PR command, main-log /beads DB diverged/ line names remediation, terminal state carries the same reason, cause proven via log (not stderr) assertion. Verified against related suites (happy-path, ensure-branch-fetch-failure, pre-sprint-validation-stale-clone all green; one pre-existing unrelated failure in dolt-sync-brackets.test.mjs confirmed present before this change too).

title: [impl] Engine integ dispatch passes deploy-verified SHA; report validation rejects mismatched/absent part-2 SHA as inconclusive
description:
Files: the auto-sprint engine integ-dispatch + integ-report-validation code in packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint.

Behaviour: (1) the engine's integ dispatch passes the deploy-verified SHA to the runner. (2) The runner's report must echo that SHA; report validation compares it against the deploy-verified SHA. A mismatch, or a part-2 result lacking the SHA, fails the phase as INCONCLUSIVE (not pass/fail) rather than accepting stale evidence.

Done / acceptance criteria:
- Engine integ dispatch supplies the deploy-verified SHA to the runner.
- Report validation treats a part-2 result whose SHA is absent or mismatched as inconclusive, failing the phase distinctly from pass/fail.
- Happy path (matching SHA) is accepted unchanged. ASCII only.
acceptance_criteria: Engine integ dispatch passes the deploy-verified SHA; report validation marks a part-2 result with absent/mismatched SHA as inconclusive (distinct from pass/fail); matching-SHA happy path accepted unchanged.
close_reason: Engine hands runner deploy-verified SHA; validatePart2Evidence/extractPart2Sha reject absent/mismatched part-2 SHA as INCONCLUSIVE; matching-SHA happy path unaffected. Unit tests added (part2-sha-freshness.test.mjs), dispatch-safety-guard command-count bumped 29->30. Targeted + golden-transcript + contract test suites pass (135+ tests).

title: [impl] integ-test-runner contract requires a fresh part-2 run this cycle + record deployed SHA
description:
Files: agents/integ-test-runner.md (or the equivalent integ-test-runner agent contract).

Problem (run 19 Integ C5): a resumed integ-test-runner session reused a part-2 (smoke test) result executed earlier in the same session, BEFORE the cycle's fixes were deployed, then issued verification verdicts from that stale evidence. The contract does not require part 2 to be (re)run fresh within the current cycle.

Behaviour: update the contract to require part 2 to run in THIS cycle against THIS cycle's deployed tip, and to record the deployed version SHA in its report. Make explicit that a resumed-session part-2 result from a prior deploy does not satisfy the playbook.

Done / acceptance criteria:
- The contract text requires part 2 to run fresh this cycle against the current deployed tip and to record the deployed SHA in the report.
- The contract states that a part-2 result lacking the current-cycle SHA is not acceptable evidence.
- ASCII only.
acceptance_criteria: integ-test-runner contract requires a fresh part-2 run this cycle against the deployed tip and records the deployed SHA; a part-2 result lacking the current-cycle SHA is explicitly not acceptable evidence.
close_reason: Closed

title: [impl] Write newTask descriptions via bd create --body-file/stdin; shrink allowlist; notes-fallback on residual validation failure
description:
Files: the orchestrator Reviewer/Final-Review newTask -> bd create path in packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint (the safe-character allowlist /^[A-Za-z0-9 .,:;!?()'_/-]+$/ and the bd create invocation).

Problem (run 19 Final Review): FAIL-finding follow-up tasks whose descriptions contain '=', '+', '&' etc. are REJECTED by the shell-injection allowlist and silently dropped -- 3 of 4 newTasks lost, including the P1 unblock task for eft.48.

Behaviour: (1) stop interpolating the description into a shell command line at all -- write the description to a temp file and use 'bd create --body-file' (or pass via stdin), removing the injection surface so the allowlist can shrink to validating only the ID/title fields it is actually needed for. (2) A rejected/residual-validation-failure finding must NEVER vanish: on any residual failure, append the raw finding verbatim into the parent bug's notes as a fallback, and log that it did so.

Done / acceptance criteria:
- newTask descriptions are passed to bd create via --body-file/stdin (no shell-string interpolation of the description).
- The allowlist no longer rejects legitimate technical characters in descriptions ('=', '&', '+', quotes, backticks-as-text).
- On residual validation failure the raw finding is appended to the parent bead's notes verbatim and the run log records it.
- ASCII only.
acceptance_criteria: Descriptions round-trip via --body-file/stdin with no shell interpolation; technical chars ('=','&','+') no longer rejected; residual-failure findings are appended verbatim to the parent bead notes and logged.
close_reason: Closed

title: [impl] Pre-flight beads-health gate: probe D-pull before setup mutation, emit one-line cause + remediation, persist reason to run state
description:
Files: the auto-sprint engine setup/sync-bracket code in packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint (the setup D-pull bracket and the terminal-state/verdict persistence).

Problem (run 20): a diverged local beads DB vs the shared Dolt remote aborts correctly with a typed DoltSyncError, but (a) the cause appears only as a raw dolt stack in the stderr file while the main log jumps straight to 'Sprint FAILED' with no reason line; (b) the check runs after setup mutations (branch ensure, finalizeAbort PR noise) have begun; (c) no remediation guidance.

Behaviour: (1) add a PRE-FLIGHT gate that runs the D-pull probe BEFORE any setup mutation (before branch ensure / any PR command); on divergence, abort with a single clear main-log line naming the workspace path, the conflicting tables, and the remediation ('local beads DB diverged from <remote>; resolve or re-init from the shared remote, then relaunch') -- same fail-fast philosophy as the arg-contract checks. (2) The terminal state/verdict persisted to the run-state JSON must carry that reason string so the dashboard shows WHY, not just FAILED. (3) stderr may keep the full dump, but the main log must contain the one-line cause.

Done / acceptance criteria:
- On divergence the gate aborts before any git branch/PR command is issued.
- The main log carries a one-line cause matching /beads DB diverged/ naming workspace path, conflicting tables, and remediation.
- The persisted terminal run-state carries the same reason string.
- Happy path unchanged; existing mock-sprint suites stay green. ASCII only.
acceptance_criteria: Divergence aborts before any git branch/PR command; main log has one /beads DB diverged/ line with workspace path + conflicting tables + remediation; terminal run-state carries the same reason; happy path unchanged.
close_reason: preflightBeadsHealthGate() added, called at the top of Sprint Setup before branch-ensure/PR commands; on divergence composes a one-line /beads DB diverged/ cause (workspace path via best-effort pwd probe, conflicting table(s) via new extractConflictingTables(), remediation text) that becomes the re-thrown DoltDivergedError's message -- persisted verbatim into terminal run-state by existing typed-abort handling. Non-divergence failures pass through unchanged. Added 12 unit tests (dolt-sync-brackets.test.mjs) for extractConflictingTables/preflightBeadsHealthGate; updated mock-sprint-happy-path.test.mjs commandLog assertions + golden-transcript fixtures for new dispatch ordering; bumped dispatch-safety-guard command-count 30->31. Verified: dolt-sync-brackets (41/41), dispatch-safety-guard, golden-transcript(-3bead), mock-sprint-happy-path, dolt-sync-discipline, pre-sprint-validation-stale-clone, dispatch-sync-bracket-coverage all green (21+41 tests). Committed c2ac35df, pushed to auto-sprint/eft-service.

title: [engine] Diverged orchestrator beads DB kills the sprint with a raw stderr dump -- needs a pre-flight beads-health gate with actionable remediation
description: Observed run 20 launch (2026-07-21): the orchestrator workspace's local beads DB had diverged from the shared Dolt remote (merge conflicts in child_counters/dependencies/issues). The engine's setup D-pull bracket failed correctly (typed DoltSyncError, run aborted, state saved) but the operator experience is unacceptable: (a) the actual cause appears only as a raw dolt error + stack in the stderr file, while the main log jumps from 'Phase: Ensure Sprint Branch' straight to 'Sprint FAILED' with no reason line; (b) the check happens after setup work has begun (branch ensure, finalizeAbort PR noise fires first); (c) no remediation guidance. Fix: (1) PRE-FLIGHT gate before any setup mutation: run the D-pull probe first; on divergence, abort with a single clear main-log line naming the workspace path, the conflicting tables, and the remediation ('local beads DB diverged from <remote>; resolve or re-init from the shared remote, then relaunch') -- same fail-fast philosophy as the arg-contract checks; (2) the terminal state/verdict persisted to the run state JSON must carry that reason string so the dashboard shows WHY, not just FAILED; (3) stderr may keep the full dump but the main log must contain the one-line cause.
acceptance_criteria: Machine-verifiable: (1) mock-sprint test: a command() harness whose 'bd dolt pull' returns the exact conflict error aborts BEFORE any git branch/PR command is issued (assert no ensure-branch command ran), with a main-log line matching /beads DB diverged/ naming the remediation, and a terminal state whose message carries the same reason; (2) the happy path is unchanged (existing mock-sprint suites stay green); (3) grep proves the raw stack is not the only carrier of the cause (log assertion, not stderr assertion).

title: [engine] Reviewer/Final-Review newTask allowlist rejects legitimate technical descriptions (e.g. '=' in ENV_VAR=off) and silently drops the finding
description: Observed run 19 Final Review C5 (2026-07-21): a FAIL finding's follow-up task was REJECTED (not sent to bd create) because its description contains '=' (the string APRA_FLEET_BD_MOCK=off), which the safe-character allowlist /^[A-Za-z0-9 .,:;!?()'_/-]+$/ forbids. The allowlist exists as shell-injection defense for orchestrator-applied bd create calls, but technical descriptions legitimately need '=', '"', backticks-as-text, '&', '%', '#', '+', '[', ']'. Dropping the finding loses reviewer output silently (this instance was survivable only because it duplicated existing eft.54). Fix direction: stop interpolating descriptions into a shell command line at all -- write the description to a temp file and use bd create --body-file (or pass via stdin), which removes the injection surface and lets the allowlist shrink to validating the ID/title fields it is actually needed for; alternatively escape-and-quote properly. A rejected finding must never vanish: on any residual validation failure, persist the raw finding into the parent bug's notes as a fallback.
acceptance_criteria: Machine-verifiable: (1) unit test: a newTask whose description contains '=' and '&' round-trips into bd create successfully (via --body-file/stdin path) with content intact; (2) unit test: a finding that still fails validation is appended to the target parent bead's notes verbatim instead of being dropped, and the run log says so; (3) injection regression: a description containing '$(rm -rf /)' and backticks lands as LITERAL TEXT in the created bead, never executed -- proven by asserting the spawned command argv (no shell string interpolation).
notes: SEVERITY UPDATE (run 19 Final Review, full tail): 3 of 4 FAIL-finding newTasks were dropped by the allowlist, not 1 -- task descriptions containing '+' (accessToken + synthesized; golden-transcript.test.mjs + golden-transcript-3bead) and '=' (APRA_FLEET_BD_MOCK=off) all rejected; only 1 of 4 persisted. The dropped set INCLUDED the P1 unblock task for eft.48 (canary smoke auth) -- i.e. the highest-priority handoff from Final Review to the next run was lost to character filtering. Raises urgency: without the notes fallback, a FAIL verdict's actionable plan mostly evaporates. (Run 19's loss is mitigated manually: the dropped tasks' content already exists on eft.48/eft.50/eft.54/eft.17 notes.)

title: [integ] Integ runner resumed-session reuse: part 2 smoke evidence inherited from a pre-fix run, verification verdicts went stale
description: Observed run 19 Integ C5 (2026-07-21): the integ-test-runner session was resumed across cycles, and its part 2 (smoke test) 'result' for C5 was the part-2 run executed earlier in the same session -- BEFORE the cycle's fixes (eft.48.6) were merged/deployed. It then issued verification verdicts ('eft.48 still reproduces') from that stale evidence, so a likely-fixed P1 stayed open into the next planning round and the run's completion gate. The runner did verify teardown cleanliness, which masked the staleness. Root cause: the integ agent contract does not require part 2 to be (re)run fresh within the current cycle; a resumed session satisfies the playbook textually while testing stale code.
acceptance_criteria: Machine-verifiable: (1) integ-test-runner agent contract (agents/integ-test-runner.md or equivalent) requires part 2 to run in THIS cycle against THIS cycle's deployed tip, recording the deployed version SHA in its report; (2) the engine's integ dispatch passes the deploy-verified SHA and the runner's report must echo it -- a mismatch (or a part-2 result lacking the SHA) fails the phase as inconclusive rather than pass/fail; (3) regression test: a mock integ report citing a part-2 result with a stale/absent SHA is rejected by the engine's report validation.

title: [test] runStart branch-selection: non-default instance direct-spawns and never calls svcMgr.start()
description:
Verification for bug eft.51 / impl eft.51.1. Unit test with a spawn-spy on runStart's branch selection.

How to assert pass/fail: must FAIL against pre-eft.51.1 code (runStart calls svcMgr.start() for a sandboxed non-default instance) and PASS after the fix. Assert on branch selection via spies, not on real process spawning.

Done / acceptance criteria:
- With svcMgr reporting installed=true AND a non-default APRA_FLEET_PORT (or APRA_FLEET_DATA_DIR override), the test proves runStart takes the direct-spawn branch and svcMgr.start() is never called.
- With no sandbox override (default instance) and installed=true, svcMgr.start() IS called -- existing default-instance behavior unchanged.
- Runs in the existing test suite. ASCII only.
close_reason: Verified: test coverage already added in eft.51.1 commit (348d636d) satisfies all acceptance criteria. Confirmed by temporarily reverting src/cli/start.ts and src/paths.ts to pre-fix versions: new 'direct-spawns...non-default instance' test fails (svcMgr.start called when it shouldn't be); with fix restored, all 19 tests in tests/cli-verbs.test.ts pass, including default-instance case still using svcMgr.start().

title: [impl] runStart() prefers direct-spawn for non-default instances so a sandbox never pokes the machine-global service
description:
Decomposition of open bug eft.51 (had no task children; doers can only claim task-type beads). src/services/service-manager/windows.ts isInstalled() runs 'schtasks /query /tn ApraFleet' against the per-user MACHINE Task Scheduler registry, which is invisible to a HOME/USERPROFILE override. In the smoke-test sandbox (fresh HOME, APRA_FLEET_PORT=18700) 'node dist/index.js start' finds the OPERATOR'S real ApraFleet task, reports 'starting via service manager', pokes the REAL service, and never spawns the sandbox server -- Setup fails at its status check. macOS is immune (isInstalled() is fs.existsSync of the plist under os.homedir()).

Fix: runStart() must prefer the direct-spawn branch whenever the invocation targets a non-default instance (APRA_FLEET_PORT set non-default, OR APRA_FLEET_DATA_DIR override present) -- a sandboxed instance must never call svcMgr.start()/touch the machine-global service registration. Default-instance behavior (no sandbox override) must be unchanged: svcMgr.start() still used.

Files to change (<=3): the runStart() branch-selection logic (start command handler) and any small helper for detecting a non-default instance. ASCII only.

Done / acceptance criteria:
- With a service manager reporting installed=true AND a non-default APRA_FLEET_PORT or APRA_FLEET_DATA_DIR override, runStart() takes the direct-spawn branch and never calls svcMgr.start().
- With no sandbox override (default instance), runStart() still uses svcMgr.start() when installed=true -- existing behavior unchanged.
- ASCII only.
close_reason: Closed

title: [integ] Windows 'start' escapes the smoke-test sandbox: schtasks service check is user-global, not HOME-scoped
description: Found by the Windows smoke-test rehearsal (stabilization Issue 43, hole 2). src/services/service-manager/windows.ts isInstalled() runs 'schtasks /query /tn ApraFleet' -- the Task Scheduler registry is per-user MACHINE state, invisible to a HOME/USERPROFILE override. In the smoke-test sandbox (fresh HOME, APRA_FLEET_PORT=18700) 'node dist/index.js start' therefore finds the OPERATOR'S real ApraFleet task, reports 'starting via service manager', pokes the REAL service (observed live: only the real server's singleton guard prevented a second real instance), and never spawns the sandbox server -- Setup fails at its status check. macOS is immune: isInstalled() there is fs.existsSync of the plist under os.homedir(), which honors the overridden HOME. Fix shape: runStart() must prefer the direct-spawn branch whenever the invocation targets a non-default instance (APRA_FLEET_PORT set non-default, or APRA_FLEET_DATA_DIR override) -- a sandboxed instance must never touch the machine-global service registration; alternatively scope the task name by data-dir hash.
acceptance_criteria: Machine-verifiable: unit test (spawn-spy on runStart's branch selection) proves that with a service-manager reporting installed=true AND a non-default APRA_FLEET_PORT/APRA_FLEET_DATA_DIR, runStart takes the direct-spawn branch and never calls svcMgr.start(); existing default-instance behavior unchanged (svcMgr.start() still used when no sandbox override is present).
close_reason: integ-test-runner (2026-07-21, verification-closure pass): CLOSED -- re-ran tests/cli-verbs.test.ts (the eft.51.2 regression suite, spawn-spy on runStart's branch selection) independently this cycle: 19/19 tests pass, including 'non-default instance direct-spawns and never calls svcMgr.start()' and the unchanged default-instance svcMgr.start() case. This matches eft.51.2's own verification (reverted fix -> test fails; fix restored -> passes). Defect no longer reproduces; underlying fix (348d636d) holds.

title: [impl] Smoke test adopts the e2e beads technique: JSONL-only (no-db), no bd bootstrap, no neutralize dance, hardcoded canary
description:
SCOPE NARROWED (re-planning 2026-07-21, per plan-review criterion 4): this task is now ONLY the integ-test-playbook.md prose rewrite (single file). The guard-script retarget and the retirement of superseded test files have been split out to sibling tasks eft.18.6 and eft.18.7; end-to-end verification is eft.18.8. The authoritative hybrid design remains in this bead's NOTES (SPEC AMENDED 2026-07-21) and its SAFETY INVARIANT is NON-NEGOTIABLE.

DO (edit integ-test-playbook.md ONLY):
(1) Setup: seed the sandbox beads DB from the git-committed .beads/issues.jsonl via 'bd init --from-jsonl --prefix gh-toy' (SANDBOX toy clone only -- never a real project DB). Delete the 'bd bootstrap --yes' recovery path and BOTH neutralize blocks (the YAML sed AND the 'bd dolt remote remove' Dolt-level block). Wire the sandbox-local throwaway file:// Dolt remote and the sandbox-local git origin mirror per the NOTES hybrid design so D-push/D-pull exercise real workflow sync with zero shared-remote exposure and set sync.remote to the file:// URL from the start.
(2) Test scenario step 2: replace the integ-canary tag lookup + self-provision fallback + the eft.18.3 JSONL-reconcile step with the HARDCODED canary reference gh-toy-4ef.
(3) Reset section: rewrite to the e2e-pattern reset (git fetch + git reset --hard origin/main + git clean -fdx, then rm -rf .beads/embeddeddolt if present + bd init --from-jsonl --prefix gh-toy).

OUT OF SCOPE (moved): scripts/check-sandbox-sync-remote.mjs and its unit tests -> eft.18.6; retiring eft.18.4/eft.39.2/eft.47.x test assertions -> eft.18.7; the smoke-flow [test] -> eft.18.8.

ACCEPTANCE:
- integ-test-playbook.md is the ONLY file changed by this task.
- Setup contains zero 'bd bootstrap --yes' and zero neutralize blocks; the real fleet-e2e-toy remote URL appears nowhere in the documented sandbox git or beads config at any step.
- Setup documents JSONL seed (bd init --from-jsonl --prefix gh-toy), the sandbox-local file:// throwaway Dolt remote, and the sandbox-local git origin mirror, with sync.remote set to the file:// URL from the start.
- Test scenario step 2 references the hardcoded canary gh-toy-4ef (no tag lookup, no self-provision fallback, no JSONL reconcile).
- Reset section documents the e2e-pattern reset.
- Playbook prose still points readers at scripts/check-sandbox-sync-remote.mjs as the pre-sprint isolation guard (its retarget lands in eft.18.6).
notes:
SPEC AMENDED (user, 2026-07-21) -- supersedes the 'no Dolt at all' aspect of
the description. The e2e suite skips Dolt because its sprint needs no
coordination; the SMOKE test must keep Dolt sync under test because the
auto-sprint workflow's D-push/D-pull brackets are real product machinery.
Revised design (hybrid):
1. SEED like e2e: sandbox beads DB from the git-committed .beads/issues.jsonl
   (bd init --from-jsonl --prefix gh-toy, sandbox toy clone only); canary
   gh-toy-4ef HARDCODED; delete the bd bootstrap recovery path and the
   integ-canary tag lookup + self-provision fallback as originally specced.
2. COORDINATE against a THROWAWAY remote, not the shared one: during Setup,
   create a sandbox-local bare Dolt remote (file:// URL inside the sandbox
   dir), push the freshly-initialized DB to it once, and set sync.remote to
   that URL from the START. D-push/D-pull then exercise the real workflow
   sync machinery with zero shared-remote exposure.
3. Isolation becomes STRUCTURAL: the sandbox config never references the
   real fleet-e2e-toy remote at any point, so the neutralize dance
   (YAML sed + bd dolt remote remove) is deleted, and
   check-sandbox-sync-remote.mjs simplifies to asserting sync.remote and
   the Dolt-level remote both point INSIDE the sandbox path (keep the
   script, retarget the assertion -- do not blind the guard).
ACCEPTANCE ADDENDUM: the smoke sprint's D-push/D-pull log lines show pushes
succeeding against the file:// throwaway remote (not skipped, not real);
teardown deletes the throwaway with the sandbox.
SAFETY INVARIANT (user-emphasized, 2026-07-21; NON-NEGOTIABLE): the smoke test must make corruption of the shared fleet-e2e-toy state impossible, not merely unlikely. Known trap (Issue 31 / eft.30 history): bd AUTO-PROVISIONS a Dolt remote from the git origin on any push attempt -- so the throwaway sync.remote alone is insufficient. Required layers, all four: (1) sandbox git origin points at a sandbox-local mirror (eft.47 fix pattern -- fetch works, real URL absent), so auto-adoption can only derive a sandbox-local remote; (2) sync.remote = the file:// throwaway from the start; (3) check-sandbox-sync-remote.mjs keeps ALL FOUR checks, retargeted to assert every git and Dolt remote resolves INSIDE the sandbox path, run before the sprint as today; (4) the engine's fail-closed D-push pre-gate (eft.35) remains untouched. The real remote URL must not appear anywhere in the sandbox's git or beads config at any point in the flow.
close_reason: integ-test-playbook.md Setup/Reset/Test-scenario-step-2 rewritten to JSONL-only seed + hardcoded canary gh-toy-4ef, per hybrid design in NOTES; verified bd CLI sequence by hand

title: [impl] Isolate the two dolt-heavy planner dispatch-watchdog tests from concurrent real-bd contention in run-integ-suites.mjs
description: Root cause: mock-sprint-planner-dispatch-dead-pid.test.mjs (180s budget) and mock-sprint-planner-dispatch-stalled-session.test.mjs (650s budget) FAIL via node:test timeout under concurrency=8 -- not a correctness regression (dead-pid produced the correct AGENT_DISPATCH_FAILED error just ~46ms after its own kill), but real per-retry dolt/bd child-process latency contended with 7 other files pushes them past their purpose-computed ceilings. Prefer the low-risk fix direction (isolate/serialize) over blanket timeout inflation, which would risk re-masking the eft.28 hang. Files (<=3): scripts/run-integ-suites.mjs (scheduler) and, if needed, a lane/marker in the two test files' config. Change: run these two dolt-heavy watchdog tests in an isolated/serial lane (reduced concurrency, e.g. their own low-concurrency group) so their per-attempt dolt overhead is not contended with the rest of the suite, WITHOUT raising the tests' own hang-detecting timeouts beyond a maintainer-justified margin. DONE when both files complete within their declared timeouts on a full real-bd suite run and the eft.28 hang-detection assertions still fire (fix logic unchanged). ACCEPTANCE: node scripts/run-integ-suites.mjs full run records both files as PASS within their declared durationMs budgets in integ-suite-status.json.
close_reason: Closed

title: [integ] real-bd suite: planner dispatch-watchdog tests (eft.28.2/28.4) now exceed their own timeout budgets and FAIL under concurrency=8
description:
Feature: N/A (real-bd regression suite, part 1 of integ-test-playbook.md)
Expected: mock-sprint-planner-dispatch-dead-pid.test.mjs (timeout 180000ms) and mock-sprint-planner-dispatch-stalled-session.test.mjs (timeout REAL_TIME_CEILING_MS+30000=650000ms, a budget the test's own header comment deliberately computes with margin over its ~560s theoretical floor) both complete within their declared timeouts and assert the eft.28 dispatch-hang fix surfaces a terminal, logged+persisted error.
Actual: both FAILED this run with node:test 'test timed out after Nms' (dead-pid at 180001.9ms, stalled-session at 650003.4ms). For dead-pid, the log shows the expected AGENT_DISPATCH_FAILED/dispatch_failed error WAS produced -- but only ~46ms AFTER the 180s node:test kill fired, i.e. the underlying fix logic worked, it just ran past its test's fixed timeout. For stalled-session, no error/log line was captured before the kill at all, so it's unproven whether it would have resolved shortly after too or was still genuinely stuck.
Likely root cause (same family as apra-fleet-eft.17): both tests deliberately pay real per-retry-attempt dolt/bd child-process latency (withGitSync's 'bd config get sync.remote --json' pre-attempt gate, per the stalled-session test's own header comment) on top of the PLANNER_DISPATCH_RETRY_DELAYS_MS backoff; under this run's concurrency=8 real-bd contention that per-attempt overhead pushed even the stalled-session test's already-generous, purpose-computed ~650s ceiling into failure. eft.17 tracks the general 'files run slower than expected under real bd' pattern but is explicitly scoped to pass-but-slow files (no correctness impact) -- these two are a step worse: literal test failures, so filed separately.
Test: node scripts/run-integ-suites.mjs (full 85-file real-bd pass); integ-suite-status.json / integ-suite-run.log entries for mock-sprint-planner-dispatch-dead-pid.test.mjs and mock-sprint-planner-dispatch-stalled-session.test.mjs, run starting 2026-07-21T07:48:58Z.
Repro: node scripts/run-integ-suites.mjs --start; --status --wait=45 until complete; inspect the two files' pass/fail + durationMs in integ-suite-status.json.
Fix direction: either raise both tests' timeouts further (risks masking a real hang again, the exact eft.28 failure mode) or reduce concurrency/isolate these two dolt-heavy watchdog tests from the rest of the concurrent run so their real per-attempt dolt overhead is not contended with 7 other files at once. Needs a maintainer decision, not a mechanical fix.
close_reason: integ-test-runner (2026-07-21): CONFIRMED FIXED -- this cycle's full 85-file real-bd suite pass (node scripts/run-integ-suites.mjs) shows BOTH dedicated tests now passing within their declared timeouts under concurrency=8: mock-sprint-planner-dispatch-dead-pid.test.mjs passed at durationMs=142376 (well under its 180000ms timeout), and mock-sprint-planner-dispatch-stalled-session.test.mjs passed at durationMs=592508 (under its 650000ms timeout). Neither appears in this run's failed-files list (only golden-transcript/golden-transcript-3bead (eft.17.3) and a new viewer-perf-regression-e2e.test.mjs failure did). The eft.46.1 isolation-from-concurrent-contention fix is confirmed working end to end.

title: [refactor] Add 4th model tier 'best' above premium -- cross-cutting tier-enum expansion (apra-fleet + apra-pm)
description:
USER SPEC (2026-07-20): the 3-tier model system (cheap/standard/premium) gains
a 4th tier 'best': the highest-capability model available for a provider,
cost no object. For Claude it maps to Fable -- but the CONCRETE model choice
stays OUTSIDE code (config/server-side resolution), exactly like today's
tiers. Deliberately filed OUTSIDE the eft epic: this is a high-impact,
repo-spanning refactoring (apra-fleet AND upstream apra-pm) and must not be
absorbed into the eft sprint scope.

SEMANTICS
- Ordering: cheap < standard < premium < best.
- 'best' is additive and OPT-IN: no existing role/prompt/policy auto-upgrades;
  defaults remain unchanged. Existing 3-tier metadata everywhere stays valid.
- Guidance for use: reserve for the hardest judgment work (architecture,
  final review, adversarial verification), consistent with the cost ladder in
  the pm skill's model-tier guidance ('when in doubt, prefer cheaper').

PER-PROVIDER DEFAULT 'best' (resolution config, not code):
- claude: Fable (claude-fable-5) -- per user.
- agy (Antigravity): TBD -- needs a defined default; decide with user.
- opencode: TBD -- depends on configured models (only mimo-v2.5-free is
  validated today); may legitimately alias to premium where no stronger
  model exists. Aliasing best->premium must be an explicit, logged fallback
  for ANY provider lacking a distinct top model (codex, copilot, gemini too).

IMPACT INVENTORY (verified by sweep 2026-07-20, apra-fleet reorg tree):
- src: types.ts (tier type/enum), tools/execute-prompt.ts (model param
  validation), tools/register-member.ts + update-member.ts, cli/register-
  member.ts, services/{model-pricing,tool-registry,user-config}.ts, ALL
  providers (claude/agy/codex/copilot/gemini/opencode/none + provider.ts),
  utils/opencode-model-validation.ts.
- packages/apra-fleet-client: client/api.mjs + docs/api-reference.md.
- packages/apra-fleet-se: auto-sprint/runner.js (planner/plan-reviewer prompt
  contract lists bare tier words -- plan reviewer REJECTS unknown tiers, so
  prompts must learn 'best' or member-created beads carrying it get bounced),
  test/model-tier-normalization.test.mjs, golden transcripts + fixtures
  (fixtures/vendor-apra-pm-schemas/doer-input.json enum), budget-live test.
- vendor/apra-pm (UPSTREAM CHANGES -> separate apra-pm PR + submodule bump,
  out-of-sprint per repo convention): agents/planner.md, agents/plan-
  reviewer.md, agents/schemas/doer-input.json (model enum), skills/pm/
  {SKILL,cost,sprint,beads,doer-reviewer-loop}.md.
- Installed fleet skill 'Model Tiers' section (source in this repo) + README
  + docs mentioning the tier triple.
- Pricing: model-pricing.ts / get_member_model_pricing must price 'best' per
  provider or fall back with a warning, never crash on the new word.

ROLLOUT ORDER
1. apra-pm upstream PR (schemas + agent prompts + pm skill docs accept
   'best' additively).
2. apra-fleet: enum + validation + providers + pricing + client + docs;
   vendor submodule bump to the merged apra-pm.
3. se: prompt contracts + normalization + goldens refresh.
4. Config: define agy/opencode 'best' defaults with user sign-off.

ACCEPTANCE SKETCH
- execute_prompt model:'best' resolves on every provider (real model or
  explicit logged premium-alias), 3-tier callers unaffected.
- Plan rounds accept beads whose metadata carries model:'best'.
- model-tier-normalization + goldens green; doer-input schema round-trips.
- Docs/skills list four tiers with the cost-ladder guidance intact.
