(2) src/services/relay-strategy.ts: RelayStrategy implements AgentStrategy via relay-request.ts's submitAndAwaitResult + relay-context.ts's process-wide singleton; getStrategy() dispatches to it. transferFiles/receiveFiles/deleteFiles honestly throw not-yet-supported rather than fabricate. (3) src/cli/spoke.ts: apra-fleet spoke <origin-member-id>, the real runnable entrypoint composing hub-client+relay-executor+relay-request via composeEnvelopeHandler, reading hub-credentials.json. origin-member-id is an explicit required arg since which member a multi-member machine acts as when originating is a genuine unresolved product question, left honest not guessed.

Building the end-to-end test (tests/spoke-e2e.test.ts, real HTTP hub + real pg-mem + real child process, two spoke instances) found and fixed a THIRD real bug in already-shipped code: relay-executor.ts never addressed its result envelopes back to the originator (to.member_id always null), which the real hub's admission check would have rejected outright -- earlier unit tests never asserted on  so this went uncaught. Fixed by threading origin_member_id through.

Explicitly deferred, not silently dropped: register_member's CLI schema still only accepts local/remote (creating a relay agent isn't wired into that flow -- relay agents are meant to be materialized from hub-known joined machines, which is its own still-unbuilt registry-sync feature); file transfer over relay isn't wired into RelayStrategy; per-member outbound identity is a flat CLI arg, not a richer model. 19 new tests, 2118 total, tsc --noEmit clean.

title: Relayed execute_command: spoke reuses LocalStrategy to fulfill hub-relayed command requests
description: Once the hub-client (its own task) can receive execute_command.request envelopes over SSE, this task wires them into src/services/strategy.ts's existing LocalStrategy execution engine and posts back an execute_command.result envelope (or long_running_update envelopes for the long-running case), matching today's SSH-based execute_command feature set. Depends on the hub-client task landing first.
acceptance_criteria: A relayed execute_command.request against a fake hub-client harness actually invokes LocalStrategy and posts a correctly-shaped execute_command.result envelope back; long-running commands emit long_running_update envelopes with the right correlation_id.
close_reason: Implemented src/services/relay-executor.ts (commit 2368221): createRelayExecutor() wires execute_command.request envelopes from the hub-client into the real LocalStrategy execution engine, posting back execute_command.result (and execute_command.long_running_update on PID capture) with correlation_id set to the originating request. member_id -> local Agent resolution is caller-supplied and honestly reports member_not_found rather than fabricating an agent. Other relay kinds are a documented no-op, not silently absorbed. 6 tests: real end-to-end success/error/nonzero-exit paths via the actual LocalStrategy (real child process, OS-appropriate command for whichever host runs the suite), plus an injected fake strategy for the PID-capture wiring (genuinely OS-specific -- Windows cleanExec doesn't wrap FLEET_PID the way linux/macos do). 2076 tests total, tsc --noEmit clean.

title: Usage/cost ledger at tier 3 (per project/member, rolled up to workspace)
description:
New tier-3 scope surfaced by the fleet-dashboard spec: track cost and token usage per (project, member), rolled up to the workspace, for the dashboard's Cost view. Honesty contract to preserve: session-cumulative only until this ledger exists; 7d/30d windows stay visibly-disabled until real persistence backs them -- never fake time windows. Usage records are append-heavy and time-series-shaped -- feeds directly into the persistence ADR (apra-fleet-us9.3) as a concrete workload to evaluate against, reinforcing Postgres-class storage as the system of record. Depends on the persistence ADR and the hub service. See docs/hub-spoke-master-plan.md Addendum item 3.

Model: Sonnet -- scoped data-model + aggregation implementation once the persistence choice is made; moderate complexity from correctness (rollup math, honesty-contract enforcement), not from novel design.
close_reason: Implemented the usage/cost ledger's real read+write data layer: recordUsage() (write side) and getCostResponse() (SUM/GROUP BY rollup per project+member, workspace total), wired to GET /ws/:id/cost behind the workspace-boundary auth gate. Honesty contract preserved exactly (CostResponseSchema.window literal 'session', no fake time windows; project-less rows excluded from breakdown but counted in total). Formally depends on us9.4 (still open/in-progress as an epic-level item), but the specific prerequisites this issue needed from it -- the persistence ADR (us9.3, closed) and the hub schema/pool existing -- are satisfied; the real callers that would populate this ledger for actual dispatches (us9.6/us9.7) are separate, correctly-unblocked-by-this work. Proven via pg-mem tests (rollup math, cross-workspace isolation, no-LLM 0-cost members, contract-validated against CostResponseSchema) plus an HTTP integration test. Full suite green (138 files/1977 tests), tsc clean.

title: No-LLM member type support (plain executor)
description:
Per the fleet-dashboard spec, a member can be any LLM OR no LLM at all (a plain command executor). Today's register-member.ts llm_provider enum (claude|gemini|codex|copilot|agy|opencode) has no such option. Needs: a null-object ProviderAdapter (or equivalent no-LLM path) that supports execute_command but never execute_prompt in either mode from apra-fleet-us9.8; register_member schema gains --no-llm; cost tracking renders 'compute only' instead of token cost for these members. See docs/hub-spoke-master-plan.md Addendum item 1.

Model: Sonnet -- scoped addition of a new, well-specified member type across a few existing call sites (adapter selection, register_member, cost display); no novel protocol design.
close_reason: Implemented no-LLM member type: NoneProvider (null-object ProviderAdapter, throws on prompt-related methods by design, harmless empty values on capability queries), register_member gains llm_provider:'none' with CLI/auth verification skipped, execute_prompt rejects 'none' members immediately with a clear execute_command pointer, and check-status/member-detail render 'compute only' instead of a token count. execute_command needed zero changes (already fully provider-agnostic). Full test coverage across all of the above. Full suite green (129 files/1913 tests), tsc clean.

title: Web dashboard MVP (fleet.apralabs.com console)
description:
Real design source found 2026-07-04: a hi-fi UX spec + clickable prototype at C:\akhil\git\fleet-dashboard (BUSINESS_MISSION.md, README.md, Fleet Cloud.html, cloud/*.jsx, hifi/ui.jsx) -- this SUPERSEDES the earlier Netlify prototype/Discussion #188 reference. Build against its documented API sketch (OAuth callback, workspace/project/member CRUD, JWT issuance/rotation, activity SSE, cost endpoint, admin provisioning). Scope is broader than 'token issuance UI': it includes OAuth (Google + Microsoft) human sign-in, a pending-approval + super-admin RBAC provisioning workflow (Member/Admin/Super-admin roles with workspace assignments) that is a SEPARATE auth system from member JWT/workspace_id auth, per-project/per-member cost rollup with an explicit honesty contract (session-only cost until a usage ledger exists; no faked 7d/30d windows), and per-OS installer downloads. Depends on the hub service (apra-fleet-us9.4) and cloud JWT issuance (apra-fleet-us9.5). See docs/hub-spoke-master-plan.md Addendum (2026-07-04).

Model: Sonnet -- scoped frontend/backend-integration implementation against an already-detailed UX spec and API contract; not open-ended design.
close_reason: Superseded: discovered that C:\akhil\git\fleet-dashboard is NOT a static design reference (as us9.10's description assumed) but a fully separate, actively-developed project -- its own git history (branch sprint-0/scaffold-ci, dozens of real commits), its own beads tracker, and critically its own DynamoDB/serverless backend (aws/, backend/serverless.yml) already implementing member CRUD, installers, add-member wizard UI, and more. This is a different persistence architecture entirely from apra-fleet-reorg's Postgres-based hub-service (docs/adr-hub-persistence.md). Building a second dashboard backend inside apra-fleet-reorg per us9.10's original scope would duplicate or conflict with fleet-dashboard's active development. User confirmed (2026-07-05): close as superseded rather than build a competing implementation. If apra-fleet-reorg's hub-service needs to expose anything specific for fleet-dashboard's backend to consume, that should be scoped as its own narrow integration-contract issue after reviewing fleet-dashboard's actual API expectations -- not assumed from this bead's original (stale) description.

title: execute_prompt two-mode formalization (one-shot vs long-running injection)
description:
Formalize execute_prompt as exactly two modes: (a) one-shot headless spawn ("claude/agy/opencode -p prompt"), the universal baseline for all six providers, and (b) prompt injection into a long-running session (the interactive send_message-based channel apra-fleet-2xs.7/.8 already plan). Mode-selection logic must be decided tier-2-locally (against the local session registry), never from caller-side state, so it survives hub relay unchanged. Depends on apra-fleet-2xs.7 (status lifecycle) and apra-fleet-2xs.8 (execute_prompt interactive routing) landing, and on the interactive-injection capability survey (H9) confirming which providers actually support mode (b). See docs/hub-spoke-master-plan.md section 8.

Model: Sonnet -- scoped formalization of already-planned work, moderate judgment on mode-selection placement.
close_reason: Formalized execute_prompt's two modes: (a) one-shot subprocess (universal, unchanged) and (b) interactive injection (built in 2xs.8). Real gap found and fixed: mode (b) was gated only on 'does a live session exist', not on provider capability -- but us9.9's survey confirms mode (b) is Claude-only (Gemini/Codex confirmed FAIL, others TBD). A non-Claude member with a live session (from fnz.1-3's registerMcpEndpoint) would have silently burned the full timeout waiting for a response its provider can never send. Fixed: interactive routing now requires agent.llmProvider === 'claude' in addition to a live session. New test proves a Gemini member with a live session correctly falls through to the subprocess path. Full suite green (127 files/1906 tests), tsc clean.

title: execute_command SSH-to-relay migration + SSH deprecation inventory
description:
Build the hub-relayed execution path across all six providers and three OSes (including long-running commands), matching today's SSH-based execute_command capability (src/tools/execute-command.ts). Once proven out, deprecate the SSH stack: ssh.ts, sftp.ts, known-hosts.ts, RemoteStrategy, setup_ssh_key, and roughly 8 associated test files. Depends on spoke mode (H6) existing and being relay-capable.

Model: Sonnet -- scoped migration of well-understood existing functionality onto a new transport; moderate risk from breadth (6 providers x 3 OSes), not from novelty.
close_reason: Built the request/response correlation half of the hub-relayed execution path: src/services/relay-request.ts (PendingRelayRequests registry, submitAndAwaitResult, composeEnvelopeHandler), complementing relay-executor.ts's (apra-fleet-cgg) fulfillment side. Also wrote docs/ssh-relay-migration-inventory.md, a verified-by-grep list of the SSH-stack files/tests (ssh.ts, sftp.ts, known-hosts.ts, RemoteStrategy, setup-ssh-key.ts, ~8 test files) that would eventually be removed, explicitly gated on 4 things not yet true in this repo (no RelayStrategy/agentType decision, no spoke-mode CLI entrypoint, no member_id<->local-Agent mapping, no production/integration proof) -- nothing deleted. Filed apra-fleet-jfn for the remaining integration work (RelayStrategy + agentType + entrypoint wiring), since that requires a registry/CLI design decision with a wide blast radius not appropriate to bundle into this issue. 11 new tests, 2097 total, tsc --noEmit clean.

title: Persistence ADR: Postgres-only vs Postgres+Redis vs Postgres+NATS for fleet.apralabs.com
description:
Evaluate hub datastore options against the responsibilities fleet.apralabs.com must serve: presence, at-least-once message relay (plain pub/sub is disqualified -- it drops messages to briefly-disconnected spokes), JWT issuance/revocation state, workspace/member CRUD, and the audit/activity log for the web dashboard. Redis Streams fit presence + relay well but Redis cannot do auth/JWT issuance and is the wrong system of record for CRUD/audit (durability + query + compliance needs a real database). Recommendation to evaluate: Postgres as system of record, +LISTEN/NOTIFY at early scale, +Redis or +NATS if/when relay throughput demands it. Weigh self-host parity (a stated product value). See docs/hub-spoke-master-plan.md section 6.

Model: Fable -- open-ended infrastructure research and trade-off analysis before any code exists; needs broad investigation of managed-service options and self-host parity constraints.
close_reason: Added docs/adr-hub-persistence.md: Postgres-only MVP decision with benchmarked escape hatch to Redis Streams/NATS JetStream

title: WAN-readiness review: bind/TLS/discovery changes needed for Journey C, without changing the data model
description:
Journey C (WAN) needs a configurable bind + TLS (currently 127.0.0.1-only, already flagged in the SSE plan as Phase-1-acceptable/future-must-change) and a non-LAN discovery/relay path for the enrollment token. This is a review/design-note task now (not implementation): confirm nothing in O1-O4 bakes in a LAN-only or single-machine assumption that would force a breaking change later. See docs/member-onboarding-journey.md section 4, Journey C.

Model: Opus -- architectural forward-compatibility review across the whole onboarding design; requires judgment about what would and would not require a breaking change later.
close_reason: WAN-readiness review complete, no code changes needed. Confirmed nothing in O1-O4 bakes in a LAN-only/single-machine assumption: enrollment (fnz.4/us9.5) is hub-mediated and outbound-only (apra-fleet join calls out to the hub, no inbound exposure needed anywhere); hub bind default is already 0.0.0.0 (network-facing by design, distinct from the local spoke's 127.0.0.1-only default); machines.hostname is a display-only label, never used for network addressing/discovery. One real gap found: hub-service-deployment.md never documented TLS termination expectations for a public deployment -- fixed by adding a TLS/WAN deployment section recommending a reverse-proxy/load-balancer in front of the plain-HTTP hub process. No breaking change is needed for WAN; this was a documentation gap, not an architecture gap. 2045 tests + tsc --noEmit clean after the doc change.

title: Confirm/implement OpenCode's registerMcpEndpoint() path
description:
OpenCode's MCP config shape ({type:'remote', url}) is already referenced in the SSE plan and in prior opencode-exploration findings; confirm it handles bearer-auth headers correctly and wire it into opencode.ts's registerMcpEndpoint(). Lower risk than AGY since prior exploration already exists.

Model: Sonnet -- mostly confirmation/wiring against already-known findings, not fresh investigation.
close_reason: Closed

title: Investigate and implement AGY's native MCP registration mechanism
description:
AGY's MCP config surface is unknown to this analysis -- config file? CLI verb? env-var? Investigate live, then implement registerMcpEndpoint() for agy.ts. Blocks full provider parity for the onboarding journey. See docs/member-onboarding-journey.md section 5, open question 2.

Model: Fable -- open-ended investigation of an unfamiliar provider's config mechanism, needs broad research before implementation.
close_reason: Closed

title: Fix stale-close unregister race in http-transport (onsessionclosed)
description:
onsessionclosed unregisters by member_id captured at initialize time. If a member reconnects (new sid) and the OLD session closes afterward, the close handler unregisters the NEW session's registry entry. Guard with sessionRegistry.get(member_id)?.sessionId === sid before unregister. Plan Addendum A2 item 12.

Model: Haiku -- precise, fully-specified one-condition guard fix in a single handler.
close_reason: Guarded onsessionclosed unregister paths with sessionRegistry.get(workspace_id, member_id)?.sessionId === sid; added regression test; 1802/0 tests passing

title: Fix suite-wide test pollution from shared fixed-path fixtures under concurrent runs
description:
tests/setup.ts points every run at the SAME fixed os.tmpdir()/apra-fleet-test-data dir, never cleaned between runs; fileParallelism:false only serializes within one vitest process, not across two concurrent processes. Root cause of the 2026-07-02 git-loss incident's test failures (two overlapping process trees from one orchestrator dispatch). Hardening: per-run unique APRA_FLEET_DATA_DIR via os.tmpdir()+PID/random suffix, cleaned in teardown. Plan section 2.2, step 10.

Model: Sonnet -- root cause is already diagnosed; the fix is a scoped test-harness change (unique dir + teardown), not new design.
close_reason: Added tests/global-setup.ts computing unique per-run test data dir via provide/inject with teardown cleanup; verified concurrent invocations no longer collide

title: execute_prompt interactive routing via send_message for connected members
description:
execute_prompt should internally route via send_message + wait-for-response for connected Claude members per architecture Section 6/12; today subprocess-only. Gated by F5 (config surface). High risk -- protocol design. Plan section 2.3.10, step 9.

Model: Opus -- protocol/routing design with high blast radius (execute_prompt is the primary dispatch path); explicitly flagged high risk, and depends on getting 2.3.2's project-aware registry right first.
notes: Scope note per docs/hub-spoke-master-plan.md section 8 and beads issue apra-fleet-us9.8: interactive-vs-subprocess mode selection must be decided tier-2-locally, against the LOCAL session registry on the machine running apra-fleet.exe -- never from caller-side/hub-side state -- so the logic is unaffected by whether execute_prompt is invoked directly (Phase 1) or relayed through the hub (later).
close_reason: Implemented execute_prompt's dual-path routing per docs/cloud-fleet-architecture.md section 6: a member with a live interactive MCP session gets routed via send_message + wait-for-response (new pending-responses.ts msgid correlation + respond_to_message tool) instead of a subprocess spawn; every other member is completely unaffected (falls through to the unchanged existing path). Mode selection is decided tier-2-locally against this machine's own sessionRegistry, per the issue's own scope note. Shares the same inFlightAgents concurrency guard and busy/idle statusline lifecycle as the subprocess path. Proven via execute-prompt-interactive.test.ts (full round trip, concurrency guard, timeout, and confirmed fallthrough for non-interactive members) plus unit tests for the new modules. Full suite green (127 files/1905 tests), tsc clean.

title: Status lifecycle + response/ack path (busy -> online/idle, member completion reporting)
description:
send_message sets member busy and nothing ever sets it back; no response/ack path from member to PM. Architecture doc Section 6 Step 7 unimplemented. High risk -- protocol design. Plan section 2.3.6, step 8.

Model: Opus -- new bidirectional protocol design (ack/response path) with wide blast radius across every member interaction; explicitly flagged high risk in the plan.
close_reason: Closed the busy->online/idle status loop with a new report_status tool: a connected member's own interactive session reports 'online'/'idle' after responding, with identity resolved entirely from the live MCP session (extra.sessionId -> sessionRegistry.findBySessionId), no member_id parameter needed. Deliberately scoped to what was actually broken (send_message sets busy, nothing clears it) rather than inventing a new envelope/correlation-id protocol -- that remains the hub-era relay's job per docs/hub-spoke-wire-protocol.md section 4, which explicitly left this tier-2-local state machine unspecified. Proven via unit tests plus a real MCP-wire integration test (connects a JWT client, calls report_status through the actual SDK, verifies the session correlation genuinely works). Full suite green (124 files/1891 tests), tsc clean.

title: Add unit test coverage: jwt, session-registry, send-message, http-transport JWT/member-param paths
description:
Zero test coverage today for jwt.ts, session-registry.ts, send-message.ts, and JWT/member-param paths in http-transport. Plan section 2.3.7, step 7.

Model: Sonnet -- bulk but mechanical test-writing across four files/modules; moderate complexity, no novel design once the underlying code (2.3.2) is stable.
close_reason: Added full unit coverage for the previously zero-coverage jwt.ts, session-registry.ts, send-message.ts modules and the JWT/member-param paths in http-transport.ts (39 new tests across 4 files). Covers sign/verify roundtrip and rejection paths (tamper, expiry, key rotation, malformed), workspace-scoped session registry semantics, send_message's cross-workspace non-leak guarantee, and both the JWT-authenticated and unauthenticated ?member= URL-param registration paths on /mcp initialize. Full suite green (122 files/1877 tests).

title: Gate register-member bootstrap behind explicit interactive flag
description:
Bootstrap does a real HTTP GET to 127.0.0.1:7523 and, if a fleet server is running on the dev machine, writes settings and spawns a real claude process from unit tests. Gate behind explicit flag/env, inject http+spawn for testability. Plan section 2.3.4, step 5.

close_reason: Gated interactive Claude bootstrap behind explicit env opt-in in test mode, added injectable deps + tests

Model: Haiku -- small, mechanical, single-file fix with an already-known fix shape (read server.json / DEFAULT_PORT).
close_reason: Resolve actual port via checkRunningInstance()/server.json instead of hardcoded 7523

title: [build] CI build-binary logs are full of false-positive warnings that mask real ones
description: User feedback (2026-07-21, first fully-green CI run 29869270095): the linux/macos build-binary job logs read as broken despite succeeding, because of KNOWN-BENIGN warnings -- (1) postject repeats warning: Can't find string offset for section name '.note.100' ~10x per linux build (upstream noise against Node 22 ELF sections; injection succeeds); (2) the macOS 'Stripping macOS codesign...' step reads like a failure though it is a required, intentional step (postject cannot inject into a signed binary); (3) setup-action deprecation warnings (punycode, url.parse, Node 20) from GitHub's own actions. False positives train contributors to ignore warnings, so real ones get skimmed past. Fix: filter postject stderr to drop ONLY the exact known-benign patterns (keep everything else verbatim, fail if postject exits nonzero); reword the codesign step to state it is expected ('codesign stripped by design: postject requires an unsigned binary; re-signed ad-hoc after injection'); pin/upgrade the deprecated setup actions where upstream fixes exist. The filter must be an explicit allowlist of benign patterns with a comment linking the upstream issue -- never a blanket 2>/dev/null.
acceptance_criteria: Machine-verifiable: (1) a clean build-binary run's log contains ZERO occurrences of the .note string-offset warning and zero unexplained 'Stripping' lines (the reworded line states it is expected); (2) the postject wrapper script has a unit test proving a NON-allowlisted stderr line passes through verbatim and a nonzero postject exit still fails the build; (3) grep of the workflow yaml shows no bare 2>/dev/null on the postject invocation.

title: [viewer] Phase header shows phase duration top-right: slow-ticking elapsed while live, frozen total after exit
description: User request (2026-07-21), phase-level sibling of apra-fleet-eft.45 (which covers per-activity elapsed ticking). Users currently cannot tell how much time the workflow spent in each phase, even though the engine knows both phase entry and exit precisely. Requirements: (1) each phase header shows a duration at its top-right, mirroring the placement used for activity durations; (2) while the phase is LIVE, show elapsed-since-phase-start as a slowly ticking value (coarse cadence is fine -- e.g. 1h 2m 15s -> 1h 2m 30s; ticks derived client-side from a server-provided phaseStartedAt, no per-tick server polling required beyond the normal refresh); (3) when the phase EXITS, the final duration is remembered (phaseEndedAt - phaseStartedAt, persisted in state) and rendered statically top-right exactly like completed activities show theirs; (4) applies to all phase headers in the tree, including completed phases of earlier cycles after resume/reload. Engine side: stamp phaseStartedAt/phaseEndedAt in the state tree at phase() transitions (the engine already knows these moments); viewer side: render + tick.
acceptance_criteria: DOM-verifiable via fixture state: (a) a fixture with a completed phase (phaseStartedAt/phaseEndedAt set) renders the formatted duration inside the phase header element, positioned via a right-aligned class, matching the activity-duration formatting convention; (b) a fixture with a live phase (phaseStartedAt set, no phaseEndedAt) renders an elapsed value computed from phaseStartedAt and a mocked clock, and advancing the mocked clock by 15s and firing the ticker updates the rendered text WITHOUT a server fetch; (c) GET /state (or the lean state payload) carries phaseStartedAt/phaseEndedAt for every phase entry -- asserting the engine stamps them at phase transitions; (d) after simulated reload of a finished run, earlier phases still show their frozen durations.
notes: Related: apra-fleet-eft.45 (per-activity elapsed ticker) -- same ticking/formatting machinery; whichever is implemented first should factor the elapsed-tick helper so the other reuses it.

title: [viewer] Beads tree: separate backlog from sprint visually, top-level status sorting, goal-based top-level placement
description: User-reported usability gaps in the dashboard beads tree (2026-07-21). (1) Backlog vs sprint are visually indistinguishable and the backlog header is nearly invisible: make the backlog panel collapsible and give it a distinct visual treatment (muted band + prominent section header) so the two regions cannot be confused. (2) Sprint section: sort TOP-LEVEL items by status In-progress -> Open -> Blocked -> Closed; children keep natural DAG order (explicitly NOT recursive, to preserve hierarchy comprehension). (3) Goal-based placement, top-level only: a top-level item outside the sprint goal (e.g. P3 under a P1/P2 goal) belongs in the backlog section, not the sprint section; EXCEPTION: a non-goal item connected to an in-goal top-level item via a blocks/dependency edge stays rendered with that sprint subtree for visual continuity.
acceptance_criteria: DOM-verifiable via viewer fixture state: (a) backlog container carries a distinct CSS class and a toggle control; toggling asserts collapsed/expanded class states; (b) with a fixture containing all four top-level statuses, the sprint section's top-level row order reads exactly In-progress, Open, Blocked, Closed while a nested child list remains in DAG order; (c) a fixture non-goal P3 top-level renders inside the backlog container, and a non-goal P3 that an in-goal top-level blocks renders inside the sprint container; (d) goal membership computed in the state payload (server-side), not by CSS hiding.
notes: integ-test-runner (2026-07-21): checked bd dep list apra-fleet-eft.52 -- no children/[test] task exists yet for this feature. Nothing to run this cycle. Left open.

title: [integ] viewer-perf-regression-e2e: on-demand description fetch/cache test mocks wrong wire field ('description' instead of 'text'), 3 subtests fail
description:
Feature: N/A (real-bd regression suite, part 1 of integ-test-playbook.md)
Expected: viewer-perf-regression-e2e.test.mjs's 'apra-fleet-eft.27.3: on-demand description fetch + cache timing' describe block (4 tests) passes.
Actual: 3 of 4 tests FAIL this run: 'first expand (cache miss) fetches and paints...', 'repeat expand of an unchanged bead is a pure cache hit...', and 'a changed updatedAt invalidates the cache...' all assert on rendered text and get '(no description)' or the wrong value instead of the expected description text.
Root cause (test-authoring bug, NOT a production defect): the test's mock 'globalThis.fetch' returns '{ id, description: ..., updatedAt: ... }' (field named 'description'), but the CLIENT code under test (packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/viewer-extensions.mjs loadBeadDescription(), line ~541) reads 'data.text', and the REAL server-side wire contract (packages/apra-fleet-workflow/src/viewer/index.mjs's GET /extensions/:extId/detail/:itemId route, ~line 1069) responds with '{ id, text, updatedAt }' -- 'text', never 'description'. The client's detailLookup hook contract (beadsExtension.detailLookup, viewer-extensions.mjs ~line 436) is explicitly documented to return '{text, updatedAt}' specifically so core/client code never has to know bd's raw field name ('description'). The test's mock fetch simply used the wrong field name for the wire shape it is simulating; production code and the real wire contract already agree with each other and are NOT the source of the mismatch.
Test: node scripts/run-integ-suites.mjs full 85-file real-bd pass; viewer-perf-regression-e2e.test.mjs in integ-suite-status.json, failures[] for the 3 tests named above.
Repro: cd packages/apra-fleet-se && node --test test/viewer-perf-regression-e2e.test.mjs (also reproduces outside the real-bd suite -- this describe block has no bd dependency).
Fix direction: update the test's mock fetch responses in this describe block from '{ id, description, updatedAt }' to '{ id, text, updatedAt }' to match the real wire contract. This is test code -- integ-test-runner does not modify test code, filing for a doer/developer to fix.

title: [integ] golden-transcript real-bd divergence: D-pull gating skips 'bd dolt pull' in no-remote scratch dir, stale golden
description: Under real bd (APRA_FLEET_BD_MOCK=off), golden-transcript.test.mjs test 'mock sprint happy-path dispatch sequence matches the committed snapshot' FAILS at JSONL line 5 (dispatch seq 4): GOLDEN expects 'bd dolt pull', ACTUAL is 'bd list --all --limit 0 --json'. The committed golden (fixtures/golden-transcript/mock-sprint-happy-path.jsonl, last regenerated in adec2dce/eft.35) still contains the D-pull, but the D-pull gating that landed in adec2dce ('gate D-pull the same way D-push is gated') now SKIPS 'bd dolt pull' when the scratch bd-init dir has no configured sync.remote. So the real-mode dispatch sequence no longer contains the D-pull the golden expects. The test PASSES in mock mode (APRA_FLEET_BD_MOCK=1, 2/2) because mock mode synthesizes the dolt-sync brackets as present no-ops, so the golden now only matches mock-mode dispatch, making the test mode-dependent. Root cause is the gating (eft.35) plus possibly the eft.37.1 workflow refactor (bb4652c3), NOT the eft.17.1 dolt-sync caching (which is downstream of the dispatch-decision/transcript push and runs the FIRST dolt pull for real). Fix direction: either regenerate the golden against real-mode gated dispatch AND make the test harness configure a sync.remote so mock and real agree, or make the golden tolerant of the no-remote D-pull skip. Evidence: real-mode run 2026-07-21 golden-transcript durationMs=355316 pass=1 fail=1; mock-mode pass=2 fail=0. ASCII-only.
notes: integ-test-runner (2026-07-21, this cycle): recurs identically -- golden-transcript.test.mjs and golden-transcript-3bead.test.mjs both FAIL again on the same D-pull-gating divergence in this cycle's full 85-file real-bd pass. Still open, same root cause, no new bug filed.

title: [viewer] Show elapsed-since-start on running activities (right edge), not just final duration
description:
USER ENHANCEMENT (2026-07-21). Today a running AGENT dispatch shows nothing
on its right edge until it finishes, when the full duration appears. During
a long prompt (5-15 min) the user cannot tell running from stuck.

FIX: for activities with isRunning true and no final duration, render
elapsed time since start at the row's right edge (where the final duration
will eventually appear): now - startTime, formatted like the final duration
(0s, 5s, 10s, 1m 20s...). Both fields already exist in the state payload
(sample real activity keys include startTime and isRunning) -- this is a
CLIENT-side render change only, no wire-format change.

EXPLICITLY NOT REQUIRED: an automatic per-second ticking timer. The value
just needs to be correct whenever the page renders -- which the existing
poll/refresh cycle already provides (each poll repaint recomputes it), and
a manual refresh must show the right elapsed value. If a trivial
setInterval repaint of ONLY these labels is added later it must not
regress snappiness (standing eft.27 constraint) -- but it is out of scope
for acceptance.

ACCEPTANCE:
1. A running agent/command activity row shows a nonzero, monotonically
   increasing elapsed value across successive polls/refreshes.
2. On completion the label is replaced by the final duration exactly as
   today.
3. No new timers required for acceptance; no measurable render-time
   regression on a 500+ activity tree.
notes: integ-test-runner (2026-07-21, re-check): re-verified via bd dep list apra-fleet-eft.45 -- still no children/[test] task. Nothing to run this cycle. Left open.

description:

Framework: node:test + node:assert (vitest-compatible), matching the existing file. No browser/jsdom.

Assertions to add/adjust:

Given tasks [{id:'41'},{id:'41.1',parent:'41'},{id:'41.2',parent:'41'},{id:'41.4',parent:'41',dependencies:[{depends_on_id:'41.1',type:'blocks'}]}], the rendered HTML nests 41.1/41.2/41.4 UNDER 41 (parent containment), and 41 is NOT a childless sibling. Replace/repurpose the old 'nests a task under its blocking dependency, not a parent field' expectations, which are now inverted.
2. A 'blocks' dependency (41.4 blocked by 41.1) renders as an inline annotation/chip (e.g. 'blocked by #41.1'), NOT as tree nesting of 41.4 under 41.1.
3. Ready/blocked/status visual cues are still present (assert the status badge markup is unchanged for closed/open/in_progress/blocked cases).
4. Existing HTML-escaping test for nested parent/child rows still passes.
5. A regression guard that the render path performs no fetch/await (e.g. renderBeadsHtml is a pure sync function returning a string; assert typeof result === 'string' and that it does not return a Promise).

Done criteria:

- Tests fail if the tree reverts to blocks-based nesting.

title: [impl] Beads tree: build hierarchy from parent-containment field; render blocks-deps as inline annotations
description:
Rework renderBeadsHtml's tree builder in packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/viewer-extensions.mjs (currently the block ~lines 206-266 that builds childrenOf/roots from 'blocks'-type dependency edges) so that PARENT CONTAINMENT is the primary tree.

Files to change: packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/viewer-extensions.mjs (only). Also update the module doc-comment (~lines 29-48) that currently states the tree is intentionally built from blocks edges, so the comment matches the new behavior.

Behaviour:
- Build the tree from each task's 'parent' field (already present in the snapshot data shape; see existing test fixture { id:'child', parent:'root' }). All 41.x children render nested under 41; the parent bug is no longer a childless sibling.
- Tasks with no in-dataset parent are roots.
- 'blocks'-type dependency edges are NO LONGER tree structure. Render them as a compact inline annotation on the row (e.g. a 'blocked by #X, #Y' chip/badge), preserving all existing dependency info that the old blockedBy badge showed. No dependency information is lost.
- Preserve existing ready/blocked/status visual cues exactly (statusBadge behaviour, blocked colouring).
- MUST NOT add any new data fetch or network call: containment comes from the parent field already in the snapshot. No regression to lean-viewer snappiness (standing eft.27 constraint).

Done criteria:
- renderBeadsHtml nests children under their parent field, not under a 'blocks' blocker.
- 'blocks' edges appear only as inline annotations, never as parent/child nesting.
- No new fetch/await/network introduced in the render path.
- Existing viewer-extensions.test.mjs still passes for status/escaping cases (dependency-tree cases will be updated by the sibling [test] task).
close_reason: Closed

title: [viewer] Beads tree: render parent containment as primary hierarchy, dependencies as annotations
description:
USER FEEDBACK (2026-07-20, screenshot of eft.41 family): the beads-tree
extension builds its hierarchy from blocks-type dependency edges by design
(viewer-extensions.mjs ~line 29: 'show me what blocks what'), so eft.41.1/.2/
.3 rendered as top-level roots, eft.41.4 nested under its lexicographically
smallest blocker 41.1, and the parent bug eft.41 sat childless as a sibling.
Data was correct; the rendering masquerades as a parent tree and reads as
broken.

DESIRED: containment (parent field) is the primary tree -- all 41.x children
under 41 -- with dependency edges rendered as annotations (e.g. a compact
'blocked by 41.1, 41.2' chip/arrow on the row), not as tree structure.
Preserve the current ready/blocked visual cues. Must not regress lean-viewer
snappiness (non-negotiable per standing eft.27 feedback): containment comes
from the parent field already present in the snapshot; no extra fetches.
close_reason: integ-test-runner (2026-07-21): no longer reproduces. packages/apra-fleet-se/test/viewer-extensions.test.mjs's 'renderBeadsHtml: containment tree (parent-based nesting, blocks-deps as annotations)' suite (incl. 'nests children under their parent; the parent is not left as a childless sibling' and the cycle-guard test) passed as part of this cycle's full 85/85 real-bd suite run (part 1 of integ-test-playbook.md) -- not among the run's 4 recorded failures.

title: [impl] M3: generic extension detailLookup hook + relocate findBeadById + route alias
description: Per docs/workflow-core-boundary-refactoring.md M3. Replace the core GET /beads/:id/description handler (findBeadById reaching into state.extensions.beads.sprintTasks/backlogTasks, ~viewer/index.mjs:500-515) with a GENERIC on-demand-detail hook. Extensions may register { id, title, html, js, detailLookup?: (state,id)=>{text,updatedAt}|null }; core serves GET /extensions/:extId/detail/:itemId by delegating to that extension's detailLookup. Move findBeadById verbatim into the beads extension module packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/viewer-extensions.mjs as its detailLookup. Extension js (owns the 'more...' UI) calls the new route; keep old /beads/:id/description as a 302/alias for one release (tag BOUNDARY-COMPAT if it lives in core). Core template-method: define the hook surface with a default no-op; se overrides.
acceptance_criteria: Core has no beads/sprintTasks/backlogTasks identifiers; GET /extensions/beads/detail/:itemId returns bead description via the se extension's detailLookup; old /beads/:id/description still resolves via a one-release alias; the 'more...' control (eft.27) works through the generic route; workflow + se suites green.

title: [impl] Cache/reuse the shared scenario dolt clone across dispatches within real-bd mock-sprint test files
description: Root cause (per apra-fleet-eft.17): now that eft.16 made D-pull/D-push brackets run for real, every mock-sprint-*/golden-transcript/budget-live scenario pays real dolt clone/pull/push latency PER DISPATCH, pushing 28/74 real-bd files over the 5-min single-file budget and the full suite to ~3228s (~53.8min) vs the ~6.5-8min target. Fix direction: within a single test file's shared scenario fixture, batch/cache the dolt clone/pull operations so repeated dispatches reuse an already-hydrated local dolt working copy instead of re-cloning/re-pulling per dispatch. Scope: the shared scenario/fixture helper used by packages/apra-fleet-se/test/mock-sprint-*.test.mjs and golden-transcript*/budget-live tests (the common setup module, not each individual test file). Keep correctness identical (all files must still PASS); only remove redundant dolt round-trips. Do NOT weaken what each scenario actually exercises. ASCII-only; npm test / the real-bd suite must stay green.
acceptance_criteria: The shared real-bd scenario fixture reuses a single hydrated dolt working copy across dispatches within a test file (no per-dispatch re-clone/re-pull for the same fixture). After the change, node scripts/run-integ-suites.mjs --start; --status --wait shows: (a) 0 failures (correctness unchanged), and (b) a materially reduced full-suite wall clock and per-file durationMs for the previously-worst mock-sprint/golden-transcript/budget-live files (worst offenders such as mock-sprint-doer-max-turns, golden-transcript, budget-live must drop well below their prior 1000s+ times; target is under the documented 5-min single-file budget). Report before/after durationMs from integ-suite-status.json as evidence. ASCII-only.
notes: STATUS AUDIT (2026-07-21): in_progress since 2026-07-20 with zero commits referencing it -- stale claim from a descoped streak, released. P3, out of gate.

Real-bd single-file before/after: mock-sprint-doer-max-turns 1543s->328s (~4.7x, 3/3 pass), budget-live 1233s->321s (~3.8x, 5/5 pass), golden-transcript 1409s->355s (~4x). Evidence doc committed in 81a4bc69. golden-transcript's remaining real-mode test divergence is an unrelated D-pull-gating/stale-golden issue filed as apra-fleet-eft.17.3 (passes in mock mode; caching is downstream of the dispatch/transcript point).

title: [Phase 3] Server-side member reservation in the fleet server (close the manual-CLI bypass)
description: Plan Part 2.2 known-hole callout + Part 4 Phase 3 + resolved Decision (reservation authority: service-local ledger accepted for v1, server-side enforcement is Phase 3 work). The service-local ledger cannot stop a human running bin/cli.mjs directly, or another tool calling execute_prompt, against a reserved member. Close it by moving reservation into the apra-fleet server itself: a reservation field on the member record (e.g. Agent.reservedBy: sprintId | null), reserve/release operations (new tools or tag reuse - implementer choice per plan), surfaced in list_members, enforced at dispatch time (execute_prompt against a member reserved by another sprint is rejected like the existing inFlightAgents busy rejection). Supervisor ledger becomes the client of this authority rather than the sole record. Narrowed in practice by the CLI-convergence feature (bypass now requires deliberately running cli.mjs outside its intended role) but still the real fix.
acceptance_criteria: Member records carry a reservation field visible in list_members; reserve/release operations exist and the supervisor uses them at sprint launch/terminal events; an execute_prompt (or manual cli.mjs run) against a member reserved by a different sprint is rejected server-side with an error naming the owning sprint (integration test); force-release path exists for wedged reservations; behavior with no reservations is unchanged.
notes: integ-test-runner (2026-07-20, cycle 2): [test] task eft.10.4 still open (not closed by doer). Existing coverage found and run green (npx vitest run tests/execute-prompt.test.ts tests/list-members.test.ts tests/member-reservation.test.ts -- 77/77 passed): cross-sprint dispatch rejected naming owner, manual-CLI bypass closed, owner dispatch allowed, list_members shows reservedBy, unreserved baseline unchanged, reserve/release/force_release unit behavior. Gap: no test exercises eft.10.4 assertion (4) end-to-end -- force_release clearing a wedged reservation followed by a subsequent dispatch from a NEW sprint succeeding. Did not write test code myself (out of scope). Left open, inconclusive pending that assertion.

title: Template a pre-initialized empty bd directory for test setup() calls
description: Every heavy test scenario's setup() (advanced-mock-runner-test.mjs, budget-live.test.mjs, golden-transcript*.test.mjs) runs 'bd init' plus several 'bd create'/'bd update --parent' round-trips from scratch per scenario -- real subprocess-spawn and Dolt-DB bootstrap cost repeated 25+ times. Investigated reusing the pre-seeded fleet-e2e-toy repo's beads DB for this (2026-07-15): NOT viable -- its 7-8 issues are a flat, unrelated toy CLI backlog with no parent-child hierarchy, dependency edges, or --metadata model-tier fields, none of which match what these unit-mock scenarios specifically need to exercise (reject/reopen paths, 3-bead sort ordering, etc.); copying its content would save nothing. The real, smaller optimization: template a pre-initialized, empty-schema .beads/ directory (just past 'bd init', no seeded issues) and copy it fresh per scenario instead of re-running bd init's own bootstrap from scratch every time -- independent of fleet-e2e-toy, which doesn't help here.

title: [test] round-cap exit logs a distinct 'deferring to next cycle' message, not 'Looping back'
description:
Add automated coverage for the auto-sprint Develop/Review loop-exit logging (apra-fleet-0pu) proving the two exit paths are distinguishable from the log alone.

What to test:
- Round-cap path: with devRounds reaching 3 while stillOpen/currentReady remains non-empty, the runner emits an explicit 'round cap reached ... deferring to next cycle' log line (including the still-open bead count) and does NOT emit the misleading 'Looping back to develop' as the terminating line.
- Organic-completion path: when stillOpen.length === 0, the runner emits a distinct 'cycle organically complete' style message and exits without the round-cap line.

How to assert pass/fail: drive the loop logic in packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/runner.js with the existing runner test harness (mock bd list --ready results to control stillOpen), capturing emitted log lines and asserting on their text. If runner.js has no unit-test seam, factor the minimal check or assert via the harness already used by other runner tests. Framework: vitest (npm test).

Acceptance criteria:
- Test fails against pre-fix behavior (no distinct round-cap line) and passes after apra-fleet-0pu impl lands.
- Both exit paths asserted: round-cap-with-open-work and organic-completion.
- Confirms carry-forward of reopened beads is unchanged (no bead lost at the cap).
- Green under 'npm test'.

title: [impl] Log an explicit 'Develop/Review round cap reached' message at loop exit
description:
In the auto-sprint runner (packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/runner.js, the per-cycle 'while (devRounds < 3)' Develop/Review loop, ~lines 1689 and 1959-1964), add an explicit log line that fires when the loop exits because the round cap was hit with work still open -- distinct from the organic 'stillOpen.length === 0' completion.

Current bug (apra-fleet-0pu): when devRounds reaches 3 with beads still open/reopened, the loop logs the misleading 'System found N beads still open/ready. Looping back to develop.' and then silently exits into Deploy on the next while-condition check -- the 'Looping back' message implies the opposite of what actually happens.

Files to change: packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/runner.js (single file).

Suggested fix: after the while loop exits, detect the case devRounds === 3 AND the last iteration's stillOpen/currentReady was non-empty, and emit an explicit line such as: 'Develop/Review round cap (3) reached this cycle with N bead(s) still open/reopened -- deferring to next cycle.' Keep the organic 'stillOpen.length === 0' break path logging a distinct 'cycle organically complete' message. Do not change control flow or the carry-forward behavior (reopened beads already correctly flow to the next Sprint Cycle) -- this is a logging/observability fix only.

Acceptance criteria:
- Round-cap exit (devRounds===3 with work still open) logs a distinct, accurate 'round cap reached ... deferring to next cycle' line naming the count of still-open beads.
- The organic completion path (stillOpen empty) logs a clearly different message and no longer prints the misleading 'Looping back to develop' when the loop is actually terminating.
- No change to which beads carry into the next cycle; only log output changes.
- Existing runner behavior/tests unaffected.
close_reason: Closed

title: [bug] Dashboard Backlog panel shows empty despite real project-wide open/deferred/blocked beads existing
description:
Live-verified 2026-07-15 on the running apra-fleet-7pm dashboard Beads Tasks tab (port 18300): Backlog section renders 'No backlog items.' At the same moment, a direct 'bd list --status=open,deferred,blocked --json' against the same working directory returned 25 real beads (including several I filed minutes earlier in this same session: apra-fleet-p4f, h7x, 0pu, k7s, 4p5, plus many pre-existing ones like apra-fleet-9ub, adl, 1cb, nbp, 4yr, 3ns.6, aw8, 92d).

The sprint log has zero occurrences of 'failed to refresh backlog panel' or 'failed to refresh sprint-tree panel' (the try/catch log lines added in the Sprint/Backlog updateDashboard() rewrite), so this is not a caught/logged fetch error -- the backlog fetch appears to either return empty, or its result is not reaching publishState/the browser correctly, or the panel is stale and simply never re-rendered after the beads in question were created (updateDashboard() runs once per Develop/Review round, and many rounds have elapsed since bead creation, so simple staleness alone seems insufficient to explain a fully-empty result).

Needs direct investigation: (1) confirm the orchestrator member's bd command actually queries the same .beads/ database as the manual check (should be identical for a local same-checkout member, but verify); (2) add temporary logging or re-run the exact 'bd list --status=open,deferred,blocked --json' command via the same command()/orchestratorMember path used by updateDashboard() to see what it actually returns; (3) check whether BACKLOG_STATUSES filtering or the sprintIds exclusion in updateDashboard() (packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/runner.js) has a bug that zeroes out the result. This is a live, reproducible discrepancy between real beads-DB state and what the dashboard displays -- filed as an observed symptom pending code-level root-cause.

title: [bug] Dashboard cost always shows $0.000 Spent despite real token usage and premium-tier dispatches
description:
Live-verified 2026-07-15 on the running apra-fleet-7pm dashboard (port 18300): header shows '173,199 Tokens' and multiple individual log entries show real per-dispatch usage (e.g. '[premium] 3,527 tkns 124.9s'), but the header's '$0.000 Spent' figure never moves from zero across the entire multi-hour, multi-cycle run.

This looks like a regression or incomplete fix of the previously-fixed apra-fleet-13o ('cost always $0.000' -- execute-prompt.ts computed real token usage but never returned it in a structured field; fixed by adding a structuredContent channel, per docs/handoff-auto-sprint-7pm.md). Token counts are clearly flowing through correctly now (visible per-entry and in the aggregate header token count), so the structuredContent plumbing itself seems to work -- but the USD cost computation/aggregation on top of it does not, or is not wired to the header's displayed value.

Possible causes worth checking (not yet investigated in code): (1) the per-model pricing table (pricing.mjs) may not have an entry for whatever concrete model string this member's dispatches actually report, causing cost computation to silently no-op/return 0 for every dispatch rather than warn; (2) the header's cost aggregation may sum a different field than the one structuredContent actually populates; (3) this may be specific to 'local' OAuth/subscription-based Claude sessions (as opposed to metered API-key billing), which may have no real per-token USD price at all -- in which case the fix is to detect that case and show something honest (e.g. 'n/a' or 'included in subscription') instead of a misleading '$0.000' that reads as 'this cost nothing.'

Needs code-level investigation (grep pricing.mjs / wherever the header total is computed and rendered) before concluding root cause; filed as an observed, live-reproduced symptom.
close_reason: Duplicate of apra-fleet-202, which already has a more precise root cause (wrong usage field names, prompt_tokens/completion_tokens vs input_tokens/output_tokens).

title: [bug] Develop/Review round cap (devRounds<3) exits silently, log says 'Looping back to develop' but doesn't
description:
TRIAGED 2026-07-16: bug still real, only line numbers have drifted (unrelated commits added ~150 lines above this region since filing).

runner.js: the per-cycle Develop/Review loop is bounded by `while (devRounds < 3)` at line 1834 (was ~1689). The reopen-handling loop (`for (const id of verdict.reopenIds) { ... bd update ... --status=open ... }`) is now at lines 2083-2094 (was ~1925-1936). The loop-bottom logic is now at lines 2118-2124: `if (stillOpen.length === 0) { break; } else { log('System found N beads still open/ready. Looping back to develop.'); }` -- the log line itself is now at line 2123 (was ~1964).

Confirmed live-reproduced scenario and gap both still hold: if `stillOpen.length===0` the loop breaks silently (expected, natural completion). Otherwise it logs "Looping back to develop" and falls through to the `while(devRounds<3)` re-check at the top -- if devRounds is already 3, the loop exits with NO additional log line distinguishing "hit round cap with N still open/reopened" from "organically done". The misleading "Looping back to develop" message still precedes an exit that does not, in fact, loop back.

Not a data-loss bug: a reopened bead still correctly carries into the next outer Sprint Cycle's Plan/Develop.

Suggested fix unchanged: at the while-loop exit (~line 2125-2126), check whether devRounds===3 AND stillOpen was non-empty on the last iteration; if so, log an explicit 'Develop/Review round cap (3) reached this cycle with N bead(s) still open/reopened -- deferring to next cycle' message.
close_reason: Closed

title: [UI] Backlog panel should render dependency tree, not flat list
description: Sprint/Backlog dashboard (packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/viewer-extensions.mjs renderFlatRow/renderBeadsHtml) currently renders the Backlog section as a flat sorted list of rows. If backlog items have dependency relationships among themselves (dependencies[].type === 'blocks'), those should be rendered as a tree (same nesting approach as the Sprint section's renderNode) rather than flattened, so users can see backlog structure/blocking relationships at a glance. Items with no dependencies among the backlog set can remain flat/top-level. User feedback from live dashboard review session (2026-07-13/14).

title: [UI] Beads dashboard tree nodes should be collapsible/expandable
description: Sprint/Backlog dashboard tree (packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/viewer-extensions.mjs renderBeadsHtml) currently renders every node fully expanded with no way to collapse. Tree nodes (and the two top-level Sprint/Backlog section headers themselves) should be collapsible/expandable like standard tree-view UI, so users can fold away completed or uninteresting subtrees. User feedback from live dashboard review session (2026-07-13/14).

title: auto-sprint: repo missing integ-test-playbook.md and deploy.md lacks required ## Deploy / ## Smoke test sections
description:
Confirmed live during apra-fleet-7pm sprint (2026-07-14, restart #2 after the apra-fleet-aw8 timeout fix): Deploy C1 phase failed cleanly (not a crash -- the deployer agent correctly refused to improvise per its runbook rules and reported the gap instead of faking a deploy):

'Deploy FAILED this cycle (C1): Stopping per runbook rules: integ-test-playbook.md is entirely absent from the repo root (only deploy.md exists)... Additionally, deploy.md itself does not contain the expected "## Deploy" and "## Smoke test" sections required to execute a deploy operation -- it has a generic "## Steps" section (Identify build / Download binary / Install / Verify) instead, so there is no defined Smoke test.'

Integration Test phase was also skipped as a consequence (no playbook found).

Confirmed on disk: integ-test-playbook.md does not exist at the repo root. deploy.md's actual section headers are: Prerequisites, Steps (with numbered ### subsections: Identify the build / Download the binary / Install / Verify), Rollback, Platform binaries -- none named '## Deploy' or '## Smoke test'.

This is not a bug in the sprint engine -- the deployer agent behaved correctly (fail loudly, don't improvise). It's a real gap in this repo's runbook docs: either (a) author integ-test-playbook.md and restructure/rename deploy.md's sections to match what the deployer agent contract (vendor/apra-pm/agents/deployer.md) expects, or (b) if the deployer/integ-test-runner contract's expected section names are themselves wrong/outdated for this repo's actual deploy process, update the agent contract instead. Not investigated which side is correct -- filed for someone to decide and fix. P3 since it doesn't block Develop/Review progress (confirmed: sprint proceeded to Final Review C1 despite this), only Deploy/Integ-Test verification.
close_reason: Fixed manually (not via sprint) per user request: deploy.md restructured with ## Permissions/## Deploy/## Smoke test sections matching vendor/apra-pm/agents/deployer.md's contract; integ-test-playbook.md created at repo root with ## Setup/## Reset/## Teardown sections plus a documented sandbox test scenario and 'adding new features' maintainer guide.

title: Auto-sprint: add an explicit pause/resume control distinct from hard Stop
description:
Idea surfaced while running the apra-fleet-7pm auto-sprint live: the
dashboard currently only exposes a hard 'Stop' (POST /stop, forcibly cancels
the workflow via `FleetWorkflow.requestStop()` -> aborts every in-flight
`agent()`/`command()` via `AbortController`, see
packages/apra-fleet-workflow/src/viewer/index.mjs:521 and
src/workflow/index.mjs:460). We verified the system is ALREADY resumable in
practice at the granularity of a full process restart -- beads (task state)
and git (commits) are the durable source of truth, so relaunching the
identical CLI invocation after a stop/crash correctly skips already-closed
tasks and picks up remaining open work.

What's missing is a deliberate, first-class pause/resume mechanism that is
cheaper and more graceful than "kill the process and relaunch," and that
resumes the SAME run rather than re-deriving everything from beads state and
re-running Plan from scratch.

## Key finding: the substrate for this already exists

`packages/apra-fleet-workflow`'s engine already has a journal/replay system
(`journal.mjs`, `computeActivityKey`, `journalEnabled`, `completedByKey`,
`diverged`) that backs instant-replay resume for `agent()`/`command()` calls
-- this is the same mechanism the Workflow tool's `resumeFromRunId` exposes:
re-running a script replays every already-completed activity from the
journal cache instantly, and only the first new/changed call executes live.
Pause/resume does not need a new persistence layer -- it needs a new
SUSPEND primitive built on the journal, distinct from both normal
completion and `requestStop()`'s hard cancellation.

## Proposed design: `interrupt()` as a journaled activity type

Modeled on LangGraph's `interrupt()` (raises inside a running graph node,
checkpoints state, halts until a human supplies a resume value, then
continues that exact node with the value in hand):

1. **New script-level primitive**: `await interrupt(checkpointName, {
   message, schema? })`, callable from `runner.js` at safe boundaries only
   (between cycles, after Deploy before Integ-Test, before Publish PR --
   never mid-agent-dispatch). Safe boundaries matter: unlike a hard Stop,
   Pause must never leave an `agent()` call half-abandoned, so it can only
   suspend at points the script itself designates, not at arbitrary
   moments an external Pause button is pressed.

2. **On first execution** (not yet in the journal): `interrupt()` writes a
   new journal entry of type `interrupt` (sequence-numbered like `agent`/
   `command` entries), containing `checkpointName`, `message`, and current
   script-level state useful for the dashboard to render (phase, cycle,
   round, streak -- the same state bead 9ub already names). It then emits
   an `interrupt` event (mirrors the existing `state`/`log`/`end` events in
   viewer/index.mjs) and the run's `executeFile()` promise resolves with
   status `'paused'` -- a new terminal status distinct from `'completed'`,
   `'cancelled'`, `'failed'`. Critically this is NOT `requestStop()`: no
   `AbortController` fires, nothing in flight is rejected, because by
   construction nothing is in flight at a designated checkpoint.

3. **Dashboard**: a `Pause` control becomes meaningful as "pause at the
   next checkpoint" (sets a flag the next `interrupt()` call checks) rather
   than "pause now" -- this is the honest UX given #1's constraint, and
   should be presented as such (e.g. "Pausing after current phase..." while
   waiting, not an instant pause). Once paused, the dashboard renders the
   `interrupt` event's `message`/checkpoint like a card, with a `Resume`
   button (and, if `schema` was supplied, an input form -- mirrors
   LangGraph's `Command(resume=value)` payload; e.g. a checkpoint before
   Publish PR could ask "proceed with PR title: <draft>?" and accept an
   edited title as the resume value).

4. **Resume**: `POST /resume { runId, value }` re-invokes
   `WorkflowEngine.executeFile()` with `journalEnabled: true` and the
   persisted journal for `runId`. Every activity before the `interrupt()`
   call (all completed `agent()`/`command()` calls, and the `interrupt`
   entry itself) replays from cache -- cheap, no re-dispatch, no re-running
   Plan. When the script reaches the SAME `interrupt()` call again, instead
   of suspending a second time, it returns the supplied `value` (or
   `undefined` if none was needed) and the script continues live from
   there. This is the exact mechanism that already makes `resumeFromRunId`
   work for `agent()` -- `interrupt()` is "the same replay machinery,
   generalized to a script-level suspend point instead of just an agent
   dispatch."

5. **`bin/cli.mjs` surface**: a `--resume-run-id <id>` flag, so the CLI
   launch this feeds from the dashboard doesn't need to reconstruct any
   auto-sprint-specific state -- it just hands the runId to
   `executeFile()` and the journal does the rest.

## Open questions / scoping notes (why this stays a backlog idea, not
scheduled work)

- Where exactly in `runner.js` do `interrupt()` checkpoints make sense?
  Candidates: end of each outer cycle, after Deploy succeeds and before
  Integ-Test runs (a natural "confirm before touching anything
  test-adjacent" point), before Publish PR (confirm PR title/description).
  Needs a pass through the phase list to pick boundaries that are both
  safe (no abandoned in-flight work) and useful (a human would actually
  want to intervene there).
- Interaction with the existing phase-resume/concurrency-lock work already
  landed in apra-pm (PR #19) -- needs a check that a second `executeFile()`
  invocation for the same `runId` doesn't race the lock that work put in
  place.
- Journal storage lifetime/location for a PAUSED (not finished) run --
  existing journal files are presumably write-once-per-run; pausing needs
  the journal kept live/appendable across the gap between suspend and
  resume, including across a full process restart if the dashboard's Node
  process itself needs to come down while paused.
- Whether `interrupt()` should be a generic `packages/apra-fleet-workflow`
  engine primitive (usable by any workflow script) or something auto-sprint
  specific in `runner.js` -- leaning toward the former since the journal
  machinery it rides on lives in the engine, not in auto-sprint.

Do not start without further design discussion -- this description is a
starting proposal, not an approved spec.

title: Auto-sprint: some failed activities never print to the text log, only visible via GET /state
description: Observed via GET /state on the live apra-fleet-7pm run: the 'Streak [apra-fleet-7pm.1, apra-fleet-7pm.2]' activity failed twice before succeeding on a 3rd attempt -- state.tree shows two prior activity entries with success:false and duration ~300866ms / ~301142ms (both just over a 300000ms/5-min boundary, consistent with a timeout). Neither failure produced ANY corresponding line in the workflow's own stdout/text log (the CLI's printed output that auto-sprint-7pm-run.log captures) -- the only place either failure is visible is the structured activity list from /state. Contrast with the apra-fleet-7pm.12 streak's failures (apra-fleet-jkw), which DID print '[Agent API Error]' / '[Workflow Log] ... threw' lines to the text log. So error-path logging is inconsistent: some agent-dispatch failures (e.g. schema/JSON-repair failures) are mirrored to the console/log stream, while others (e.g. this apparent timeout case) are only recorded in the structured state and silently retried, making a live user watching the log/console alone unable to tell a retry happened at all. Reported by user-requested API analysis; not yet investigated -- record only.
close_reason: fixed, commit 51df121 on feat/fleet-reorg (merged from feat/fleet-workflow-subsystem); see commit body for details

title: Auto-sprint viewer: activity view has no scroll, only workaround is collapsing items
description: Observed live during the apra-fleet-7pm auto-sprint run (dashboard at localhost:8090): the activity table/view has no scrollbar or scroll behavior for its client area. When activity items overflow the visible area, the only way to see more is to manually collapse other items -- there is no way to simply scroll the list. Expected behavior: the activity view's client area should be independently scrollable (e.g. overflow-y: auto on the list container) so all items remain visible/reachable regardless of expand/collapse state. Marked by user as worth fixing (not just recording). Reported by user observation; not yet investigated.
close_reason: fixed, commit 51df121 on feat/fleet-reorg (merged from feat/fleet-workflow-subsystem); see commit body for details

title: Auto-sprint viewer: agent activity entries show model tier as n/a
description: Observed live during the apra-fleet-7pm auto-sprint run (dashboard at localhost:8090): activity entries for dispatched agents show the model tier/name as 'n/a' instead of the actual tier (cheap/standard/premium/ultra) or resolved model name/id (haiku/sonnet/opus/fable). The dispatch clearly has this info available (task metadata carries a model tier, e.g. apra-fleet-7pm.1 = premium, apra-fleet-7pm.2 = standard), so it should be threaded through to the viewer's activity display instead of falling back to n/a. Reported by user observation; not yet investigated -- record only.
notes: CORRECTION after inspecting GET /state and the viewer's inline HTML/JS directly: the 'model' field (e.g. "model":"premium") IS present and populated on every agent-type activity in state.tree -- it is not actually missing server-side. But the front-end template (src/viewer, the inline <script> served at /) never renders act.model anywhere. The only per-activity meta text shown is tokensHtml, computed as: act.usage ? '<n> tkns' : (type==='agent' && !isRunning ? 'n/a' : ''). Since act.usage is always null (see apra-fleet-13o), every finished agent activity shows literal 'n/a' in that slot -- which is what the user was reading as a missing model tier, because there is no separate model-tier badge/column at all to distinguish it from a token count. Two real, distinct gaps: (1) add an actual model-tier display element that reads act.model, (2) separately fix the usage/token plumbing (apra-fleet-13o) so the tokens slot itself isn't perpetually 'n/a'. Analysis only, not yet fixed.
close_reason: fixed, commit 51df121 on feat/fleet-reorg (merged from feat/fleet-workflow-subsystem); see commit body for details

title: [bug] pm-skill planner.md/plan-reviewer.md use literal Claude model names in task metadata, inconsistent with apra-fleet-se's cheap/standard/premium tier vocabulary
description:
Discovered live during apra-fleet-7pm's real auto-sprint run (2026-07-13). The standalone `planner` and `plan-reviewer` subagents (installed from vendor/apra-pm's agents/planner.md, agents/plan-reviewer.md -- generic pm-skill agents, not apra-fleet-se-specific) write/expect task metadata like `{"model": "opus"}` / `{"model": "sonnet"}` -- literal Claude model family names.

But packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/runner.js's real dispatch (FIXED_ROLE_TIER, apra-fleet-dv5) expects the three literal tier strings 'cheap'/'standard'/'premium' instead -- de-hardcoding literal model names was the entire point of apra-fleet-dv5.

When I used the generic `planner` agent to build apra-fleet-7pm's DAG (ahead of running auto-sprint against it) and had `plan-reviewer` review it, both used the opus/sonnet vocabulary, matching planner.md's own documented convention. auto-sprint's OWN internal Plan phase (running as part of the real sprint, not the standalone agent) caught the mismatch and silently rewrote all 14 tasks' metadata to the tier vocabulary before proceeding -- so this round self-healed, but it cost an extra rewrite pass and could easily NOT self-heal for a differently-worded task set (there's no guarantee auto-sprint's internal planner always catches every task, e.g. if it deems some already fine and doesn't compare against the full ready-list from a fresh angle).

Root cause: vendor/apra-pm's planner.md/plan-reviewer.md were written independently of, and predate, apra-fleet-dv5's tier-vocabulary contract, and were never updated to match it.
acceptance_criteria: Decide and document the correct relationship: either (a) update vendor/apra-pm's planner.md/plan-reviewer.md to use the cheap/standard/premium tier vocabulary when the target is an apra-fleet-se auto-sprint run (possibly conditioned on some signal distinguishing 'pm skill standalone sprint' from 'apra-fleet-se auto-sprint target'), or (b) make auto-sprint's runner.js Plan phase's self-correction an explicit, tested, always-on step (not incidental) with a warning surfaced when a rewrite happens, so users know their upstream planning tool used the wrong vocabulary. Either way, the two docs should stop silently disagreeing.

If none do, update the affected mocks to only set isError on a genuine runCmd() spawn failure (e.g. err.code === undefined / ENOENT), matching real execute-command.ts semantics, and confirm the existing test suites still pass.
