title: N12 follow-up: harvester mock contract-check is regex-weak; analysisArtifactFile slug collision risk
description:
Adversarial re-verification of apra-fleet-unw2.10 (N12) found two real, non-blocking gaps. Filed as follow-up per reviewer's discretion (both narrow, neither affects N12's current correctness -- runner.js's actual wiring is genuinely live/correct, verified via source tracing, golden transcript, and the full 174/174 suite).

1. advanced-mock-runner-test.mjs's "hardened" harvester contract check (buildMockFleetApi, ~line 577-589) does not actually validate that analysisText/costAnalysis carry real content -- it only regexes for the STATIC instructional label text ("...pre-computed by the orchestrator...") that the prompt template always emits regardless of the underlying value. Proven via scratch-edit: forcing `costAnalysis = ''` in runner.js and re-running the full advanced-mock-runner-test.mjs suite still reports the harvester status as OK, not FAILED, in every scenario. Separately, the analysisArtifactFile regex (`/analysisArtifactFile:\s*\S+/`) is unanchored across newlines -- `\s*` spans the blank-line paragraph break and matches into the next paragraph's first word ("analysisText"), so a blanked-out analysisArtifactFile also silently passes. (A completely OMITTED -- not blank -- analysisText/costAnalysis key does still crash the runner via fence()'s `.match()` on undefined, which is a strong failure mode, just not the designed 'graceful FAILED status' path, and doesn't cover the blank-string case at all.) Net effect: a future regression that makes buildAnalysisText/buildCostAnalysis silently return blank/near-blank content would NOT be caught by this 'hardened' mock, defeating its stated purpose. Fix direction: anchor analysisArtifactFile's regex to a single line (e.g. `/analysisArtifactFile:\s*(\S+)/m` matched against a single line, or split on double-newline first) and check the fenced VALUE strings for analysisText/costAnalysis are non-trivially long / non-empty, not just that the wrapper label exists.

2. runner.js's branchSlug computation (`validated.branch.replace(/[\/]+/g, '-')`, ~main()) only replaces slashes/backslashes with hyphens; it does not otherwise disambiguate. Two differently-named sprint branches whose only difference is a slash vs. a pre-existing hyphen at the same position (e.g. `feat/fleet-reorg` and `feat-fleet-reorg`) collapse to the identical `docs/sprint-analysis-<slug>.md` path and would clobber each other's analysis artifact if both sprints ever ran against the same repo/worktree in overlapping windows. Narrow (requires a specifically colliding branch-naming pattern) but real. Fix direction: consider hashing the branch name (or a short content hash) into the slug, or rejecting/warning on slug collisions at runtime.

Non-blocking: apra-fleet-unw2.10 (N12) itself is correctly implemented and its acceptance criteria are met; this bead only hardens the regression-guard/edge-case robustness further.
notes:
ADVERSARIAL REVIEW: APPROVED

Worktree: C:\akhil\git\wt-unw2-22
Branch: tmp/unw2-22-mock-hardening
HEAD SHA: c91dd278fd2dd0b58181c477febfe9872afedce6 (confirmed matches doer report, working tree clean)

Scope confirmed exactly 3 files vs parent 5d8fe26 (no scope creep):
- packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/runner.js
- packages/apra-fleet-se/test/advanced-mock-runner-test.mjs
- packages/apra-fleet-se/test/fixtures/golden-transcript/mock-sprint-happy-path.jsonl

Independent re-verification performed (not trusting doer's self-report):

1. Regex-anchoring bug (analysisArtifactFile): reproduced the ORIGINAL bug myself.
   Built a real harvester prompt via the actual exported buildHarvesterPrompt()
   with analysisArtifactFile: ''. Old regex /analysisArtifactFile:\s*\S+/ ->
   test() returned TRUE (bug: \s* spanned the blank-line paragraph break and
   matched into the next paragraph's "analysisText" word). New anchored regex
   /^analysisArtifactFile:[ \t]*(\S+)[ \t]*$/m -> exec() returned null against
   the same prompt, correctly flagging it missing. Confirmed via direct node
   execution, not by reading the test file alone.

2. Content-length check (analysisText/costAnalysis): reproduced the ORIGINAL
   bug myself via a scratch edit to runner.js -- made buildCostAnalysis()
   unconditionally return '', then ran the full advanced-mock-runner-test.mjs
   suite. The mock's harvester branch (buildMockFleetApi) consistently reported
   status FAILED, notes "Missing required harvester input(s): costAnalysis."
   across every mock sprint scenario in the suite -- this is the real
   production wiring catching a real regression, not a hand-built prompt.
   Reverted the scratch edit; git diff on runner.js confirmed clean afterward.

3. Whitespace-only check is a real length check, not a non-empty check:
   independently confirmed a single-space analysisText trims to length 0 and
   fails the MIN_NONTRIVIAL_LEN=15 threshold.

4. computeBranchSlug: independently ran it against the exact colliding
   example from the bead. feat/fleet-reorg -> feat-fleet-reorg-3eab93b8,
   feat-fleet-reorg -> feat-fleet-reorg-ff8e91e4. Distinct, and calling
   computeBranchSlug('feat/fleet-reorg') twice returns the identical slug
   (deterministic). Confirmed main() calls computeBranchSlug(validated.branch)
   in place of the old naive .replace(/[\/]+/g, '-').

5. buildHarvesterPrompt is genuinely exported and the new regression tests
   import and call the real function (not a hand-rolled reimplementation) --
   confirmed via the diff's import statement and usage sites.

6. Golden fixture diff is exactly the one line claimed: analysisArtifactFile
   value gains a -49d8f2b3 hash suffix, nothing else changed.

7. Full test suite: npm test in packages/apra-fleet-se -> 174/174 pass,
   0 fail (node --test), plus the custom advanced-mock-runner-test.mjs
   suite separately reports PASS (deterministic across 2 runs, all
   runner.js phases exercised). Ran directly, no tail piping.

Adversarial finding (non-blocking, judgment call, noting for the record):
MIN_NONTRIVIAL_LEN=15 is a pure length check on trim()'d content, not a
semantic/meaningfulness check. Constructed a scratch scenario feeding
analysisText = 16 repeated '.' characters (no real content) -- trimmed
length 16 >= 15, so it PASSES the hardened check despite carrying zero
analytical value. The specific scenarios the bead and task explicitly
asked about (empty string, whitespace-only, single space/char) are all
correctly caught since they trim to length 0. A regression that emits
sufficiently-long placeholder/padding text instead of real content would
slip past this check, but that is a narrower and different failure mode
than what this bead targeted. Not blocking; flagging as a residual,
narrow limitation worth knowing about.

Branch slug readability: assessed the hash-suffix design as sound --
feat-fleet-reorg-3eab93b8 keeps the full human-readable branch-derived
prefix intact and legible; the 8-hex-char suffix is a short, clearly
demarcated disambiguator, not filename-unwieldy.

No security issues found. No regressions in adjacent code. Consistent
with existing runner.js export conventions (parseBdJson, checkMemberTopology,
etc.).

Ready for merge by orchestrator (I did not merge).

title: Expose --budget flag on apra-fleet-se CLI (bin/cli.mjs)
description: Follow-up to apra-fleet-unw2.8 (N10): runner.js/validateArgs now accepts an optional numeric 'budget' arg that sets context.budget.total before any dispatch, and the underlying enforcement (agent() budget check in apra-fleet-workflow/src/workflow/index.mjs) is verified correct and pre-dispatch. But bin/cli.mjs has no --budget flag and never populates args.budget, so a real end user invoking the CLI has no way to set a budget ceiling -- the feature is only reachable today by calling WorkflowEngine.executeFile() directly, as apra-fleet-se/test/budget-live.test.mjs does. apra-fleet-unw2.8's own Scope (files) line explicitly excluded cli.mjs, so this was a legitimate scope decision for that issue, not an oversight -- but it leaves live budget enforcement practically inert for real sprints. Add a --budget <usd> CLI flag (following the existing buildOptionsSpec()/buildRunnerArgs() pattern in bin/cli.mjs) that threads through to args.budget.
close_reason:
APPROVED after adversarial re-verification (reviewed independently, not doer's self-report).

title: [bug] parallel() replay.diverged flag is shared by reference across branch forks (divergence-propagation timing, not key-determinism)
description: Follow-up from apra-fleet-unw2.14's review: replay.diverged is a single flag shared by reference across all parallel() branch store forks (pre-existing, not introduced by unw2.14's key-determinism fix). If a genuine divergence occurs in one branch while siblings are still mid-flight, those siblings could be forced live for calls that would have cache-hit, depending on scheduling. Orthogonal to N6 (key-computation determinism) -- this is about divergence-propagation timing/isolation across branches. Does not affect unw2.14's acceptance criteria. Scope: packages/apra-fleet-workflow/src/workflow/index.mjs's parallel() store-fork/divergence-flag handling. Consider per-branch divergence isolation if desired.

title: [V/N18] Vendored agent defs: replace nonexistent 'bd remember' instruction (doer/reviewer/integ-test-runner)
description:

comments.author: Azure Pipeline
comments.text:
CHANGES NEEDED -- adversarial review of commit 751f9c305b82712c90b0b1f89a89ef8eb9bbf46d (branch tmp/unw2-20-vendor-bd-remember-fix, checkout C:\akhil\git\wt-unw2-20\vendor\apra-pm).

MECHANICAL CLAIMS VERIFIED (all correct):
- HEAD SHA matches: 751f9c305b82712c90b0b1f89a89ef8eb9bbf46d.
- History includes prior session fixes unw.13/nnx/unw.20/unw.21/unw.24/c5t/omj/aqr (git log -20 confirmed).
- Exactly 3 files changed, 1 line each: agents/doer.md:74, agents/reviewer.md:118, agents/integ-test-runner.md:128. No other content touched.
- Grep sweep of agents/ and skills/ for "bd remember" returns zero hits (independently confirmed).
- ASCII-only: confirmed clean (checked programmatically, no bytes >0x7F in the diff).
- Nothing pushed; outer repo (C:\akhil\git\wt-unw2-20, branch tmp/unw2-20-scratch) shows vendor/apra-pm as an unstaged/uncommitted working-tree diff only (git diff --cached is empty, git ls-tree HEAD still shows ea653f1) -- this is expected two-level-worktree noise, not a committed submodule-pointer change.
- AGENTS.md (lines 68, 125) and CLAUDE.md (line 23) at the submodule root still contain "bd remember" -- confirmed via independent grep. Correctly out of the stated scope (issue text explicitly scopes to agents/*.md and skills/*.md); this is a legitimate scope read, not a dodge. NOT filing a follow-up issue for this per the task instructions' own guidance that it's genuinely out of scope -- flagging here only for the record.

BLOCKING PROBLEM -- the fix's core premise is factually wrong:
I ran `bd remember --help` and a live test invocation myself: `bd remember "test-review-check tokens: input=1 output=1" --key test-review-check-DELETE-ME` succeeded (exit 0, "Remembered [...]"), and `bd recall` returned it correctly (test memory then removed with `bd forget`). bd version 1.1.0 (8e4e59d39), the only `bd` binary on PATH (C:\nvm4w\nodejs\bd).

`bd remember "<insight>"` is a real, fully working, ID-FREE command in the current bd CLI -- it stores cross-session persistent memory injected at `bd prime`. It is NOT "nonexistent" and does not "fail member-side" as asserted by both the parent finding (N18) and this commit's message ("the current bd CLI does not implement [bd remember]"). That claim is independently falsified.

Consequences:
1. The task solves a problem that does not reproduce in this environment. Before mechanically swapping the command, the doer should have run `bd remember --help` (or attempted an actual invocation) to confirm the premise, not just verified that the replacement (`bd note`) exists.
2. The swap is not a like-for-like syntax fix -- it's a semantic change: `bd remember` is global, cross-session, ID-free memory; `bd note <id>` appends to one specific issue's `notes` field. These are different persistence mechanisms with different retrieval paths (`bd recall`/`bd prime` vs `bd show <id>`).
3. The replacement introduces a real correctness gap in 2 of the 3 files:
   - agents/doer.md:74 -- `<id>` is well-defined in context (the doer's Step 2 loop tracks `<id>` per claimed task; "Before committing your last task" naturally refers to that task's id). This one is contextually sound.
   - agents/reviewer.md:118 -- the token-tracking step runs "After completing your review," i.e. after Step 2's loop over potentially many closed tasks ("For each recently closed task, run bd show <id>"). There is no single `<id>` naturally in scope at that point -- on an APPROVED verdict reviewing multiple tasks, which id does `bd note <id> ...` target? Unaddressed.
   - agents/integ-test-runner.md:128 -- same problem: "After completing all features" follows a loop over multiple open features (Step 2, "For each open feature"). No single `<id>` is naturally in scope.

Recommend: either (a) revert this change since the premise doesn't hold in this bd version, keeping `bd remember` (ID-free, matches original usage exactly), or (b) if `bd note` truly is preferred for durability-under-review reasons, pick a well-defined `<id>` per file (e.g. the sprint/track id passed to the role, if one exists in the dispatch prompt) rather than leaving an ambiguous placeholder in reviewer.md and integ-test-runner.md, and correct the commit message's factual claim about bd remember's nonexistence.

Reviewer: Sonnet 5, independent verification, no self-report trusted.

title: [D/N18] Viewer: treat cancelled as a terminal state in the browser poll error path
description:
Findings: feedback-reassessment.md N18 item (c) / F9 residue (viewer/index.mjs:359 checks only success/failed, so a CANCELLED run renders OFFLINE after the 5s server close).

Scope (files): packages/apra-fleet-workflow/src/viewer/index.mjs + tests.

Work: include 'cancelled' in the terminal-state check in the browser poll error path so a CANCELLED run keeps rendering CANCELLED instead of OFFLINE. While touching the file, add a one-line doc note that the viewer's group/phase tracking is single-run by design (accepted single-tenant usage).

Acceptance criteria: unit test (no DOM/network, same style as existing viewer tests) asserting the poll-error handler treats cancelled as terminal; suite green.
close_reason: Round 2 approved. Original fix (2a3831f) confirmed still present unchanged: index.mjs:359 terminal-state check correctly includes cancelled alongside success/failed, running remains non-terminal. Test-quality defect from round 1 fixed in 8612e7d: the poll-error-handler test now reads the real src/viewer/index.mjs via fs.readFile and regex-matches the actual terminal-state condition (globalState.status === 'success' || ... 'failed' || ... 'cancelled'), following the same source-grep pattern as the adjacent 'no live process.exit()' test. Independently reproduced the revert-and-fail proof myself: removed the cancelled clause from index.mjs in the worktree, reran the test, got a genuine failure (ERR_ASSERTION, 'Expected to find terminal state check that includes success, failed, AND cancelled'), restored the file via git checkout, confirmed git status clean and test passing again. Full apra-fleet-workflow suite: 87/87 pass (test rewritten in place, not net-new, so count unchanged from round 1). Only 2 files touched across both commits (index.mjs + test file) - matches scope, no stray files. Worktree: C:/akhil/git/wt-unw2-19, branch tmp/unw2-19-viewer-cancelled, HEAD 8612e7ddd298d3e26dd0e14d62a2f93596fbf773 (contains 2 commits to land together: 2a3831f original fix + 8612e7d test-quality fix). Ready for orchestrator to merge into feat/fleet-reorg.

comments.author: Azure Pipeline
comments.text:
CHANGES NEEDED (adversarial review, commit 2a3831f0434a8ed041ce20006d95764acba0cbf9).

Verified independently:
1. Real HEAD SHA matches reported SHA (2a3831f0434a8ed041ce20006d95764acba0cbf9).
2. src/viewer/index.mjs:359-360 - the terminal-state check is correctly and minimally widened to include 'cancelled' alongside 'success'/'failed'. Confirmed 'running' remains non-terminal (correctly still falls to OFFLINE on poll error, which is the intended behavior for a truly-still-running state losing its connection). No other logic in the poll handler was touched.
3. The one-line doc comment above `let currentGroup = ...` (line ~395) is a comment-only addition; no behavior/refactor change alongside it. Confirmed via diff.
4. Only 2 files touched (index.mjs + apra-fleet-workflow-viewer-lifecycle.test.mjs) - matches scope, no stray files.
5. Ran the full apra-fleet-workflow suite myself: 87/87 pass, 0 fail, 0 skipped (matches doer's report).

DEFECT FOUND - the new test does not exercise the actual fixed code path:

test/apra-fleet-workflow-viewer-lifecycle.test.mjs lines 255-274 ("poll error handler treats cancelled as a terminal state...") defines its OWN standalone isTerminal() function inline in the test and asserts against that local copy. It never imports, reads, or evaluates the real HTML_TEMPLATE string / poll-error-handler logic from src/viewer/index.mjs.

Proof: I reverted the index.mjs fix locally (removed the "|| globalState.status === 'cancelled'" clause, restoring the pre-fix behavior) and reran just this new test in isolation - it still PASSED (1/1), because it's only asserting against its own hardcoded duplicate, not the real source. I then restored the file (working tree verified clean via git status --porcelain).

This means the test provides no regression protection: it would keep passing even if the real fix in index.mjs were reverted or the terminal-state list were changed to something wrong.

The correct style already exists in the same file: the adjacent test "src/viewer/index.mjs contains no live process.exit() call" (lines 276-289) reads the actual source file via fs.readFile and asserts against real file content, because the poll handler lives inside a client-side HTML_TEMPLATE string that can't be imported/executed directly in Node. The new test for apra-fleet-unw2.19 should follow this same source-grep (or source-extraction + eval) pattern so it actually fails if the 'cancelled' check regresses.

Please rewrite the test to genuinely exercise/verify the real index.mjs source (e.g. grep the terminal-state condition string out of HTML_TEMPLATE and assert it contains 'cancelled', or extract+eval the actual condition), then re-close.

title: [D/N18] Runner minors: deferred in NOT_DONE_STATUSES; fence reviewer prompt's bd show JSON
description:
Findings: feedback-reassessment.md N18 items (a) and (d).

Scope (files): packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/runner.js + tests.

Work:
(a) Add 'deferred' to the NOT_DONE_STATUSES list (runner.js:94) so a goal-priority bead deferred mid-sprint (the harvester defers issues per its contract) counts as NOT done for the exit check. Add an exit-check test with a deferred goal-priority bead.
(b) Wrap the reviewer prompt's embedded raw bd show --json (planner-authored titles/descriptions) in wrapUntrustedBlock, consistent with every other inter-agent text path (A7 fencing rule).

Acceptance criteria: exit-check test with a deferred bead at goal priority does not exit success; reviewer prompt snapshot shows the fenced block; suite green (golden updated if prompt text changes).

Verified independently (not trusting doer self-report):

1. runner.js:157 -- NOT_DONE_STATUSES changed from 'open,in_progress,blocked' to 'open,in_progress,blocked,deferred'. Confirmed this constant is actually consumed by the exit-gating logic at lines 1887/1890 (per-cycle goal-priority open-bead check that decides whether to exit the cycle loop) and lines 2008/2011 (final-review evidence gathering) -- not dead code.

2. runner.js:693 -- buildReviewerPrompt now wraps the raw `bd show --json` output via wrapUntrustedBlock('bd show --json', acceptanceCriteriaJson), matching the same pattern used elsewhere (e.g. buildDoerPrompt's wrapUntrustedBlock('reviewer.notes', feedback) at line 667). Read wrapUntrustedBlock's implementation in contracts.mjs (dynamic backtick-fence sizing, UNTRUSTED_BLOCK_PREAMBLE, 'untrusted-agent-output' fence label) and confirmed acceptanceCriteriaJson is always a string (built via `command()` at runner.js:1733) satisfying wrapUntrustedBlock's type check.

3. Adversarial revert-and-confirm-fail proof (self-reproduced, not taken on faith): temporarily reverted runner.js:157 back to 'open,in_progress,blocked' (deferred removed) via scratch edit, ran `node test/advanced-mock-runner-test.mjs` in full. Result: exactly one failure --
   "Expected the sprint to NOT exit as success while a goal-priority bead remains deferred (never closed), got: {"status":"success","verdict":"PASS",...}"
   -- proving the corrected test genuinely exercises the NOT_DONE_STATUSES fix. Reverted the scratch edit; `git diff` on runner.js confirmed clean afterward, HEAD unchanged.

No scope creep, no stray files (git status --porcelain --ignored shows only the expected node_modules/dist ignores).

title: [D/N16] 3-bead golden variant protecting the unw.19 ordering fixes
description:
Findings: feedback-reassessment.md N16 (the sort-by-(title,id) fixes at runner.js:806-807, 850-851, 979-984 have no regression protection -- the committed golden scenario is single-bead where every sort is a no-op; reverting the sorts today passes the whole suite).

Scope (files): packages/apra-fleet-se/test/** (new golden variant + fixtures). No runner.js behavior changes.

Work: a 3-bead golden variant that snapshots ONLY the order-sensitive artifacts -- the streak-assignment prompt text and the reviewer prompt's bead-id list -- NOT the full dispatch order, sidestepping the genuine parallel-completion race the single-bead golden rightly avoids. Same normalization + UPDATE_GOLDEN gate as the existing golden test.

Acceptance criteria: reverting any of the three sort lines fails this test (demonstrated in the issue); two consecutive runs produce identical snapshots; suite green. Sequenced after the runner prompt-changing chain (unw2.10) so snapshots are written once against final prompt text.
close_reason:
APPROVED after independent adversarial re-verification (not doer self-report).

Worktree: C:/akhil/git/wt-unw2-17, branch tmp/unw2-17-golden-variant, HEAD=9b236a22a45b8a5bf7f94161892eb4960ae760db (confirmed via git rev-parse, working tree clean).

1. Diff scope: `git diff 5d8fe26..9b236a2 --stat` shows ONLY the two expected new files (test/golden-transcript-3bead.test.mjs, test/fixtures/golden-transcript/mock-sprint-3bead.jsonl), 534 insertions, 0 deletions elsewhere. Zero runner.js diff, satisfying the explicit "No runner.js behavior changes" constraint.

2. Read the full test file: it snapshots ONLY the streak-assignment prompt text and the reviewer prompt's bead-id list, explicitly documented (and structurally enforced via the `artifacts` filter in run3BeadScenario) to exclude full dispatch/event ordering across the 3 parallel beads. Header comment correctly explains why (avoiding the genuine parallel-completion race the single-bead golden avoids).

3. Ran the new test file standalone 3 separate times (not just trusting doer's claim): all 3 runs passed 2/2 subtests each (golden-match + determinism-proof), producing byte-identical JSONL snapshots each time (Windows EBUSY temp-dir cleanup warnings appeared but did not fail any test, consistent with the known Windows teardown-retry pattern in the codebase).

4. Independently reproduced the regression-detection proof myself:
   - Scratch-edited the `currentReady` comparator at runner.js:1422 (reversed sort), ran the golden-match subtest: FAILED with a clear diff showing the streak-assignment prompt's "Ready bead ids:" line in reversed order (Zzz, Mmm, Aaa instead of golden's Aaa, Mmm, Zzz) at JSONL line 1. Reverted via `git checkout --`, confirmed clean.
   - Scratch-edited the `assignedBeadIds` comparator at runner.js:1589-1593 (reversed sort), ran the golden-match subtest: FAILED independently at JSONL line 2 (the reviewerBeadIdList field), with line 1 (streak-assignment) unaffected -- proving the test distinguishes WHICH artifact regressed, not just "something changed." Reverted, confirmed `git diff` clean before finishing.

5. Confirmed the existing single-bead golden-transcript.test.mjs still passes unmodified (2/2 subtests, ~256s).

6. Ran the full apra-fleet-se suite (`npm test`, which chains advanced-mock-runner-test.mjs && node --test test/*.test.mjs): 176/176 pass, 0 fail, 0 cancelled/skipped, exit code 0.

Adversarial assessment on snapshot brittleness: a legitimate, non-regression runner.js change (e.g. cosmetic prompt-text rewording, or a new field added to the reviewer/streak-assignment prompt) would spuriously break this test. This is judged acceptable -- it's the same maintenance cost as the existing single-bead golden fixture, gated the same way (UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 env var, not automatic). Minor nit (non-blocking): the `update-golden` npm script alias in package.json only regenerates golden-transcript.test.mjs, not this new 3-bead file; the 3-bead file's own UPDATE_GOLDEN path still works when invoked directly, so this is a documentation/convenience gap only, not a scope violation.

Worktree/branch/SHA reported above for orchestrator to merge. Not merged by this review.

title: [D/N14] CLI robustness: strict flags, missing-member abort, expose requirementsFile/roleMap, member-side bd precondition, viewer port
description:
Findings: feedback-reassessment.md N14 (silently-ignored unknown flags via strict:false; warn-and-continue on missing members shrinks the pool silently; skill-advertised requirementsFile/roleMap unreachable from the CLI; bd show precondition runs on the LOCAL machine while the sprint's bd runs on the member; viewer port hardcoded 8080 with no listen error handler).

Scope (files): packages/apra-fleet-se/bin/cli.mjs (+ small runner.js touch only if flag plumbing requires it) + tests.

Work: (a) parseArgs strict: true -- unknown flags fail loudly with usage; (b) missing configured members abort unless an explicit --allow-missing-members flag is passed; (c) add --requirements-file and --role-map (JSON or @file) flags wired through to validateArgs(); (d) run the bd show issue precondition on the orchestrator MEMBER (via the fleet transport) rather than the local machine -- or, if transport is not yet up at precondition time, verify on the member immediately after initialize and before any phase; (e) --viewer-port flag (default 8080) + an error handler on server.listen so a port collision reports cleanly.

Acceptance criteria: tests for each of (a)-(e) (typo'd flag rejected; missing member aborts; both new flags reach the runner's validated args; precondition provably targets the member; port collision message). Suite green.
close_reason:
Independently verified all 5 sub-fixes in packages/apra-fleet-se/bin/cli.mjs (commit 57b85c08fe73ade0cef2e9e0437b99b3925b79ee, branch tmp/unw2-16-cli-robustness, worktree C:\akhil\git\wt-unw2-16):

(a) Strict flags: parseCliArgs uses parseArgs({..., strict:true}). Confirmed via code read and by test "rejects a typo'd flag with a clear usage message instead of silently defaulting" (--max-cycle) and "rejects an unknown flag entirely" -- both throw "Invalid command-line arguments" + full USAGE_TEXT.

(b) Missing-member abort: resolveMemberValidation aborts by default when any configured member is unregistered, proceeds with warn-and-continue only when allowMissingMembers=true. Verified the all-missing edge case is genuinely handled in code (validMembers.length===0 check runs BEFORE the allowMissingMembers check, so it aborts even with the flag set) and is covered by test "aborts regardless of the flag when ALL members are missing".

(c) --requirements-file/--role-map: read auto-sprint/runner.js myself -- KNOWN_ARG_KEYS already included 'requirementsFile' and 'roleMap' (lines 123-125), and the diff confirms runner.js has ZERO changes (git diff base..head -- runner.js is empty). resolveRoleMap supports inline JSON and @file indirection, rejects malformed JSON, non-object shapes, and non-array-of-strings entries with clear errors -- all covered by 6 tests in describe(c), including an actual @file tmpdir round-trip through validateArgs().

(d) Member-side bd show precondition: checkIssuesExistOnMember's runBdShow is wired in main() to `fleetApi.executeCommand({ command: 'bd show '+id, member_name: member })` -- confirmed NOT a local execFile call (the old execFile/promisify import was removed in this diff). Traced ordering in main(): transport.start() -> initialize handshake -> notifications/initialized -> member validation (step 2) -> checkIssuesExistOnMember (step 3) -> checkMemberTopology (step 4) -> sprint start. Precondition runs after transport+member validation and before any sprint phase, as required.

(e) Viewer port: --viewer-port flag exists (default 8080, validated as integer 1-65535). attachViewerErrorHandler registers a real 'error' listener producing a clean EADDRINUSE message ("viewer port N is already in use, try --viewer-port <other port>"). Confirmed the collision test is genuine (not mocked): it binds a real http.createServer() to port 0, gets the OS-assigned port, then attempts server.listen() on a second real server against that same port and asserts the actionable error message resolves.

Test file cli-robustness.test.mjs: 19 subtests across 5 describe blocks as claimed, all substantive (real JSON parsing, real file I/O via tmpdir, real socket collision, real strict-parseArgs throw paths) -- not trivial "does not throw" assertions.

File hygiene: git diff base..head --stat shows only packages/apra-fleet-se/bin/cli.mjs and packages/apra-fleet-se/test/cli-robustness.test.mjs touched. No scope creep.

Full apra-fleet-se suite run in foreground: 166 tests, 165 pass, 0 fail, 1 skip (pre-existing, unrelated harvester tripwire), duration 114s, exit code 0. git status clean post-run.

Verdict: APPROVED. Ready for orchestrator merge from worktree C:\akhil\git\wt-unw2-16, branch tmp/unw2-16-cli-robustness, SHA 57b85c08fe73ade0cef2e9e0437b99b3925b79ee into feat/fleet-reorg.

title: [C-D/N15] Handle orchestrator as a documented pseudo-role; normalize roleMap keys
description:
Findings: feedback-reassessment.md N15 (getMemberForRole('Orchestrator') at runner.js:573 is a capitalized non-ROLES stray of exactly the A2 casing class; a roleMap author writing lowercase 'orchestrator' silently gets physicalMembers[0]).

Scope (files): packages/apra-fleet-se/auto-sprint/runner.js + tests.

Work: handle 'orchestrator' deliberately as a documented application-level pseudo-role (NOT added to the vendored ROLES enum); normalize all roleMap keys via normalizeRole() at validateArgs() time; the runner.js:573 call site uses the canonical lowercase form.

Acceptance criteria: test proves roleMap { orchestrator: [...] } is honored (orchestrator commands go to the mapped member); mixed-case roleMap keys are normalized or rejected loudly (pick one, document it); suite green.

comments.author: Azure Pipeline
comments.text:
Adversarial review PASSED. Verified independently (not from doer's self-report):

SHA/tree: worktree C:\akhil\git\wt-unw2-11, branch tmp/unw2-11-orchestrator-role, HEAD 5e6eedde081ef384d48c19d20611d88a7fe912fe (matches doer report), working tree clean.

runner.js:
1. ROLE_ORCHESTRATOR = 'orchestrator' constant added with doc comment explaining pseudo-role status excluded from contracts.ROLES. Grepped every use site: only ever passed to getMemberForRole()/command() (member_name routing for bd/git commands), NEVER to agent()/executePrompt() (which require agentType+schema and would trigger ROLES/model-metadata validation). Confirmed command() in apra-fleet-workflow/src/workflow/index.mjs takes member_name as a plain routing string with no role/schema validation at all -- structurally impossible for orchestratorMember to reach a ROLES-enum check.
2. Stray getMemberForRole('Orchestrator') (capitalized) at old runner.js:573 confirmed fixed to getMemberForRole(ROLE_ORCHESTRATOR) (canonical lowercase).
3. normalizeRole() (trim+lowercase, imported from contracts.mjs) applied to every roleMap key inside validateArgs(), with collision detection: two keys normalizing to the same string throw a clear error rather than silently clobbering. Verified via own script: {Doer:..., doer:...} throws; roleMap stays undefined when omitted (no side effect).

cli.mjs (scope judgment: LEGITIMATE, not scope creep): resolveRoleMap() had its own independent capitalized-Orchestrator bug (roleMap.Orchestrator used in the pre-transport bd-show precondition, executed via CLI main() before runner.js's validateArgs() ever runs -- confirmed by reading the precondition block, which runs immediately after fleet transport init and BEFORE engine.executeFile() invokes runner.js at all). This is a separate bug instance of the identical N15 class at a call site the runner.js fix cannot reach. Fixing it here was necessary, not scope creep. Confirmed:
4. resolveRoleMap() imports normalizeRole from ../auto-sprint/contracts.mjs (not reimplemented) and applies the SAME collision-rejection logic/error format as validateArgs().
5. roleMap.Orchestrator -> roleMap.orchestrator fix in the pre-transport precondition is correct; verified resolveRoleMap() runs (line ~369) before the precondition block (line ~397+) in main(), preserving unw2.16's established ordering (transport init -> member validation -> bd-show precondition, now correctly targeting the normalized orchestrator member).

Dual-normalization-path analysis: constructed an adversarial roleMap { orchestrator, ' Doer ', 'INTEG-TEST-RUNNER' } and ran it through both resolveRoleMap() and validateArgs() independently and in sequence. Both normalize identically; validateArgs()'s re-normalization of already-normalized CLI output is a safe idempotent no-op (not redundant dead code -- it's defense-in-depth for callers that bypass the CLI and call engine.executeFile() with a raw roleMap directly, per the code comment). Collision rejection also verified identical in both paths.

Tests: runner-arg-contract.test.mjs unit tests cover mixed-case/whitespace normalization, orchestrator acceptance, Orchestrator->orchestrator normalization, collision rejection, and the roleMap-stays-undefined regression check. Mock-level tests assert actual member_name on dispatched bd commands (not just comments) and assert zero executePrompt calls with agent==='orchestrator', genuinely proving orchestrator never reaches agent dispatch. cli-robustness.test.mjs additions cover resolveRoleMap normalization/collision and a substantive end-to-end resolveRoleMap->buildRunnerArgs->validateArgs integration test.

Full apra-fleet-se suite run in foreground: 183/183 pass, 0 fail, 0 cancelled (268.8s). Working tree remained clean throughout (my own transient test-output.log artifact was removed before verification concluded).

No hygiene issues: exactly 4 files touched (runner.js, cli.mjs, 2 test files), matches claimed scope + justified out-of-scope cli.mjs fix.

APPROVED for merge. Worktree: C:\akhil\git\wt-unw2-11, branch: tmp/unw2-11-orchestrator-role, SHA: 5e6eedde081ef384d48c19d20611d88a7fe912fe.

title: vendor/apra-pm doer-reviewer-loop.md and sprint.md still describe plan-reviewer/reviewer writing feedback.md (same contradiction pattern, different files)
description:

apra-fleet-unw.13 (reviewer role) and apra-fleet-unw.20 (plan-reviewer role) both fixed skills/pm/SKILL.md to say the reviewer/plan-reviewer return structured output only and never write feedback.md, matching agents/reviewer.md and agents/plan-reviewer.md's Rules. Related: apra-fleet-unw.13 (reviewer-role fix), apra-fleet-unw.20 (plan-reviewer-role fix, same pattern), apra-fleet-unw (parent epic).

title: [W6] Document AgentOptions.schema single-source + dispatch-prompt-precedence contract in agent() JSDoc

title: [bug] vendor/apra-pm SKILL.md plan-reviewer bullet contradicts agents/plan-reviewer.md "NEVER write feedback.md" rule (same pattern as V1, different role)
description:

This is the exact same contradiction pattern that apra-fleet-unw.13 (V1) found and fixed for the reviewer role (SKILL.md said reviewer writes feedback.md, agents/reviewer.md said NEVER write feedback.md) -- but for the plan-reviewer role, which was out of V1s declared scope (V1 was specifically about the reviewer role).

Discovered during round-2 review of apra-fleet-unw.13 (self-reported by the doer as a new discovery, verified independently by the reviewer). Also relevant generic SKILL.md mentions of feedback.md (line 90 Sprint-core roles intro narrative-files list, line 230 R2 sandboxing example, line 364 /pm status description) remain technically valid as long as plan-reviewer still writes feedback.md -- once this issue fixes plan-reviewer to stop writing it, those generic mentions should also be revisited.

Fix: update SKILL.md line ~100 plan-reviewer bullet (and check doer-reviewer-loop.md, sprint.md for the same plan-reviewer/feedback.md assumption) to say plan-reviewer returns structured output (verdict/notes/taskAssignments per planReviewerVerdict schema), matching agents/plan-reviewer.md verbatim. Scope: vendor/apra-pm submodule, same branch/vendoring process as unw.13.

Related: apra-fleet-unw.13 (this contradiction pattern, reviewer-role half fixed there), apra-fleet-nnx (separate stale --notes model-tier leftover-docs finding from the same review).

Three other generic feedback.md mentions (intro narrative-files list, R2 sandboxing, /pm status) also updated for internal consistency, confirmed accurate. agents/plan-reviewer.md confirmed byte-identical (no diff) between 5c5fed9 and fix commit 0908d96. title: [W5] Golden-transcript snapshot test for the mock sprint (testing gap 4)
description:
Findings: feedback.md Testing gap 4 -- no golden-transcript test; prompt drift is invisible.

Scope (files): packages/apra-fleet-se/test/** (+ a snapshot fixture directory).

Work:
1. With the deterministic mock fleet (from the W0 mock issue) and the finalized runner (post-W4),
   record the full ordered sequence of dispatched prompts/commands per cycle (member, agentType,
   schema id, prompt text, command text) into a golden JSONL snapshot.
2. Snapshot-assert the sequence on every test run; a diff fails the test with a readable
   side-by-side of the first divergence.
3. Provide an explicit update path (env var or npm script, e.g. UPDATE_GOLDEN=1) so intentional
   prompt changes are a conscious, reviewed act.
4. Normalize volatile fields (timestamps, temp paths, run ids) before comparison.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Two consecutive runs produce identical transcripts (determinism proof).
2. Changing any runner prompt string fails the test until the golden file is regenerated.
3. Golden file is committed and human-readable (one dispatch per line).
close_reason:
APPROVED after independent adversarial re-verification (worktree C:\akhil\git\wt-unw19, branch tmp/unw19-golden-transcript, HEAD 0d0f545c759d36ed5d914fbd360d2efd74ba9017 confirmed via git rev-parse, no mismatch with doer's report -- length discrepancy in task framing was just a trailing-newline artifact from wc -c).

Golden-transcript deliverable (packages/apra-fleet-se/test/golden-transcript.test.mjs):
- Drives one real deterministic mock sprint cycle through the actual runner.js (via FleetWorkflow/WorkflowEngine.executeFile), not a hand-rolled fake.
- Snapshot-asserts the full ordered dispatch transcript (member, agentType, schemaId, prompt/command text) against a committed, human-readable JSONL fixture (test/fixtures/golden-transcript/mock-sprint-happy-path.jsonl, 42 lines, one dispatch per line, real prompt text visible).
- diffFirstDivergence() does a genuine field-level diff of the first divergent line (verified by direct read), not a generic mismatch message.
- Volatile-field normalization (bead ids -> <BEAD:slug>, timestamps -> <TIMESTAMP>, tempdir -> <TMPDIR>) is real and title-keyed/stable; confirmed it does not scrub prompt text itself (spot-checked normalizeText() and the committed fixture -- full prompt wording is intact).
- update-golden npm script exists and requires an explicit UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 env var / dedicated script; normal `npm test` (node test/advanced-mock-runner-test.mjs && node --test test/*.test.mjs) never invokes it.
- AC1 (determinism): confirmed via the doer's own "two consecutive runs" test AND by running it myself twice (independently, post-revert) -- 2/2 pass both times.
- AC2 (prompt-drift detection): did my own proof -- edited buildDoerPrompt()'s literal prompt text in runner.js, ran the golden test, got a clean field-level diff pinpointing exactly the changed prompt at seq 12/line 13, then reverted; `git diff`/`git status --porcelain` confirmed clean revert, no lingering edit.
- AC3: golden file is committed, human-readable JSONL, one dispatch per line -- confirmed by direct read.

Heightened-scrutiny item -- runner.js production-code change (already-merged in unw.16/unw.17):
- Read the full diff (git show HEAD -- .../runner.js). It ONLY adds `.sort((a,b) => a.title.localeCompare(b.title) || a.id.localeCompare(b.id))` to readyBeads/currentReady (ready-list ordering) and an equivalent title-keyed sort to assignedBeadIds (review-phase evidence-gathering ordering). Traced the surrounding code: the `parallel(streaks, ...)` doer dispatch call itself, its concurrency, and the streak-outcome recording logic are byte-for-byte unchanged -- only the deterministic reporting/comparison order changed, never actual dispatch concurrency or decision logic.
- Confirmed the fix is benign, not a race-condition mask: `bd list --ready --json`'s prior ordering fed only prompt text and doerPool round-robin index (cosmetic/assignment, not correctness), and assignedBeadIds' prior ordering fed only which order beads appear in a `bd show`/reviewer prompt (same underlying bead set either way, just reordered) -- no bead-state decision depended on this order before or after.
- Ran the full apra-fleet-se suite (95 tests, 27 suites) myself, independently, TWICE: both runs report 95/95 pass, 0 failures, matching the doer's claim. No ordering-dependent test broke.

File hygiene: exactly 4 files touched, all justified -- runner.js (the reviewed fix above), package.json (adds update-golden script), plus the new test file and its golden fixture. No stray/temp files.

This is the last remaining child of the apra-fleet-unw epic; all 25 other children are now closed, so closing this one completes the epic (25/26 -> 26/26).

Not merged by this reviewer per protocol -- orchestrator to merge worktree C:\akhil\git\wt-unw19 (branch tmp/unw19-golden-transcript, HEAD 0d0f545c759d36ed5d914fbd360d2efd74ba9017) into feat/fleet-reorg.

title: [W5] Failure-path regression tests for F1/F3/F4/F5/F6/F7/A1/A2/A5 (testing gap 3)
description:
Findings: feedback.md Testing gap 3 -- zero coverage of the load-bearing failure paths.

Scope (files): packages/apra-fleet-workflow/test/**, packages/apra-fleet-se/test/** only.

Work: add targeted regression tests, each named for the finding it guards (skip any already added
by the earlier remediation issues -- audit first, do not duplicate):
- F4: member-not-found -> typed error (no null / .includes TypeError).
- F5: schema-scrape on multi-JSON output; repair-loop exhaustion.
- F6: MCP non-response -> timeout; crash/resume via journal replays without double-dispatch.
- F3: budget exhaustion aborts dispatch.
- F1: export-function scripts load and run.
- F7: extra positional args to sequential throw.
- A1: unapproved-plan fallthrough aborts.
- A2: doer throw inside parallel does not abort siblings.
- A5: blocked-beads false completion; stall abort.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Each bullet above maps to at least one test whose name references the finding id; reviewer
   checks the mapping table in the PR description.
2. All tests deterministic (no Math.random, no timing races; fake timers where needed).
3. Full suite green via npm test on Windows.
close_reason: Independently re-verified by reviewer (not the doer): all 9 findings (F1,F3,F4,F5,F6,F7,A1,A2,A5) confirmed genuinely covered by real, passing, deterministic tests -- 7 pre-existing (audited each cited test) + 2 new (F1 examples load/run, F6 MCP timeout classification). Both packages' full test suites green: apra-fleet-workflow 86/86, apra-fleet-se 93/93 x2 runs (no flakiness). Only test files touched, no src/ changes. See comment for full finding-by-finding verification notes.

comments.author: Azure Pipeline
comments.text:
Independent verification (reviewer, not the doer) of apra-fleet-unw.18, commit ad49147ba6dd9d21739d25c878ac45ae054d8e27 on tmp/unw18-failure-regression-tests (worktree C:\akhil\git\wt-unw18). SHA confirmed via `git rev-parse HEAD`.

Scope check: `git diff feat/fleet-reorg..HEAD --stat` shows only 2 files touched, both test files (apra-fleet-workflow-errors.test.mjs +28, apra-fleet-workflow-examples.test.mjs new/+79). No src/ changes -- matches the "no bugs found" claim. git status --porcelain clean.

Finding-by-finding re-verification (each test file opened and read in full, cited test run individually and observed passing):

- F1 (new): apra-fleet-workflow-examples.test.mjs -- loads/runs examples/01-hello-world.js and examples/03-transform-sequential.js (the exact file/line feedback.md cited) via WorkflowEngine.executeFile() against a mock fleetApi. Ran standalone: 4/4 pass. Genuinely exercises the old AsyncFunction-loader SyntaxError regression via assert.doesNotReject.
- F6 (new, timeout half): apra-fleet-workflow-errors.test.mjs "F6: an MCP client-side timeout..." -- read in full. Confirmed deterministic: throws a plain Error synchronously from executePromptImpl (no setTimeout/fake timers/Math.random), asserts FleetTransportError with code TRANSPORT_ERROR and explicitly asserts code !== 'CANCELLED'. Distinct scenario from the cooperative-cancellation ABORTED path (verified in src/workflow/index.mjs lines 743-763: ABORTED -> CancelledError, everything else -> FleetTransportError) and distinct from the already-covered crash/resume journal test. Ran standalone: pass.
- F6 (crash/resume half, claimed pre-existing): apra-fleet-workflow-journal.test.mjs:161 "a crash after 2 of 3 activities, then resume: ... ZERO new dispatches" -- read and ran, passes. Genuinely exercises no-double-dispatch on replay.
- F4 (claimed pre-existing): apra-fleet-workflow-errors.test.mjs:30 and :148 "member-not-found response text throws MemberNotFoundError" for both agent() and command() -- read and ran, passes. Typed error, not null/.includes TypeError.
- F3 (claimed pre-existing): apra-fleet-workflow-budget-usage.test.mjs:113 "agent() never dispatches to the fleet API once the budget is exhausted" -- read and ran, passes.
- F5 (claimed pre-existing, compound finding, both sub-behaviors checked): apra-fleet-workflow-schema-repair.test.mjs -- sub-behavior 1 (multi-JSON scrape) at line 38 "a reply with two JSON objects plus prose yields the schema-valid one"; sub-behavior 2 (repair-loop exhaustion) at line 126 "persistent garbage across all repair attempts throws AgentOutputError". Both read and ran, pass. Both genuinely distinct assertions, not a near-duplicate pair.
- F7 (claimed pre-existing): apra-fleet-workflow.test.mjs:78 "(F7) sequential(items, fn1, fn2) -- the old multi-stage form -- throws a TypeError". Read and ran, passes.
- A1 (claimed pre-existing): apra-fleet-se/test/advanced-mock-runner-test.mjs runRejectedPlanScenario / checks at lines 716-727: SprintPlanRejectedError + zero doer dispatches when plan never approved. Read in context, part of the full-suite run (see below).
- A2 (claimed pre-existing, compound): advanced-mock-runner-test.mjs lines 775-799 "doer streak throws, sibling completes" -- isolation scenario: failing task's doer dispatched exactly twice (original+retry), never closes; sibling closes normally; executeFile() still resolves. Both sub-behaviors present.
- A5 (claimed pre-existing, compound: false-completion + stall-abort): advanced-mock-runner-test.mjs lines 943-972 (orphaned in_progress bead must not be read as sprint success) and lines 990-1031 (StalledSprintError after 2 consecutive stale cycles, staleCycles===2). Both sub-behaviors present and distinct.

Test suite (ran twice each, per instructions, given Windows/Dolt flakiness risk for apra-fleet-se):
- apra-fleet-workflow: npm test -> 86 tests, 0 fail (matches doer's claim).
- apra-fleet-se: npm test -> run 1: 93 tests/0 fail; run 2: 93 tests/0 fail. No flakiness observed.

No security issues, no stray/temp files, no tool config slipped in. All 9 findings genuinely mapped to a real, passing, deterministic test that exercises the actual failure path (not superficial name matches). The "7 of 9 already covered" claim holds up under independent audit -- verified against source (src/workflow/index.mjs error-classification logic) as well as the tests themselves.

Verdict: APPROVED.

Update them so a future reader sees the current decision, not a stale in-progress negotiation. Cross-link to fleet-dashboard's docs/architecture.md where relevant (it already references docs/api-contract-reconciliation.md back).

title: Wire RelayStrategy.transferFiles/receiveFiles to file-transfer-relay.ts's send path
description: Independent review ledger (2026-07-05) confirmed file-transfer RECEIVING is real-pipeline-tested (tests/hub-service/file-transfer-e2e.test.ts) via createFileTransferReceiver wired into src/cli/spoke.ts, but the SENDING side is unreachable through AgentStrategy: RelayStrategy.transferFiles/receiveFiles/deleteFiles (src/services/relay-strategy.ts:77-86) all throw 'not yet supported over relay'. sendFileOverRelay (src/services/file-transfer-relay.ts) already exists and is tested directly (file-transfer-e2e.test.ts calls it via getRelayContext() rather than through RelayStrategy). This task wires transferFiles to call sendFileOverRelay using the same relay-context.ts singleton RelayStrategy.execCommand already reads from.
acceptance_criteria: RelayStrategy.transferFiles(localPaths, destinationPath) reads each local file and calls sendFileOverRelay per file, addressed to agent.relayMemberId, and returns a TransferResult ({success, failed}) reflecting real outcomes. A real end-to-end test (mirroring tests/hub-service/file-transfer-e2e.test.ts's two-spoke pattern) proves RelayStrategy.transferFiles actually delivers a file through the real hub to a receiving spoke. receiveFiles/deleteFiles may remain honestly unsupported if no wire-protocol kind exists for pull-direction transfer yet -- do not fabricate a fake success path for them.
close_reason: Implemented (commit 341d31a): RelayStrategy.transferFiles now reads each local file and sends it via file-transfer-relay.ts's sendFileOverRelay, addressed to agent.relayMemberId, returning a real TransferResult ({success, failed}) per file. Proven end-to-end through getStrategy()/AgentStrategy (not sendFileOverRelay directly) in tests/hub-service/file-transfer-e2e.test.ts's new describe block: two real spoke instances, real HTTP hub, real pg-mem, real local files verified on disk after transfer, plus a per-file-failure case. receiveFiles/deleteFiles remain honest not-yet-supported (no pull-direction wire kind exists). 5 new tests, 2133 total, tsc clean.

title: Intra-workspace ack/announce trust: verify member_id belongs to calling machine
description: Threat-model finding 3d (docs/threat-model-workspace-iron-wall.md). POST /ws/:id/ack (http-server.ts:272-300) takes member_id from the request body and acks scoped only by workspace_id (relay-queue.ts:133-146); it does NOT verify member_id is served by the calling machine (claims.member_id). A compromised spoke inside a workspace could ack (retire) envelopes addressed to a DIFFERENT member in the SAME workspace, suppressing their delivery. Intra-tenant only (does not cross the iron wall), hence lower severity. Root cause shared with presence.announce trusting spoke-supplied member_ids verbatim (envelope-routes.ts:87-103, presence.ts:22-58); that is now harmless for cross-tenant reads after the us9.11 fetchDeliverable workspace-scoping fix, but remains an intra-tenant integrity gap.
acceptance_criteria: ack cross-checks that member_id resolves to a member currently present on claims.member_id's machine (presence.listForMachine) before acking, and/or presence.announce validates each announced member_id resolves within the machine's workspace via getMember(claims.workspace_id, ...). Regression test proves a spoke cannot ack another member's envelope within the same workspace.
close_reason: Fixed (commit 7c7b2ea): envelope-routes.ts's handlePresence now verifies each announced member_id (and heartbeat's single member_id) resolves via getMember(claims.workspace_id, ...) before trusting it into the presence snapshot -- unresolvable entries are silently dropped, not a hard rejection, so one bad id doesn't block the rest of a valid announce. POST /ws/:id/ack now cross-checks the target member_id against listForMachine(claims.member_id) (the calling machine's own announced presence) and returns 403 if the machine never announced that member, closing the intra-workspace ack-suppression gap. 3 new tests plus updated existing presence tests to createMember() first (previously announced synthetic ids with no backing row, which the fix now correctly drops). 2086 tests total, tsc --noEmit clean.

title: Docs reconciliation headers on cloud-fleet-architecture.md and member-onboarding-journey.md
description:
Add short revision headers pointing both docs at docs/hub-spoke-master-plan.md as the superseding design where relevant: cloud-fleet-architecture.md's 2-tier direct-CLI-to-hub model and 'SSH as permanent control plane' stance are both reversed by the new mission; member-onboarding-journey.md's Journey B LAN/mDNS discovery is superseded by hub-brokered enrollment (cheap to supersede -- it was never built). Journeys A and C's WAN-readiness framing both still hold.

Model: Haiku -- mechanical doc cross-referencing, no design judgment needed.
close_reason: Added revision-note headers: cloud-fleet-architecture.md now points at hub-spoke-master-plan.md as superseding its 2-tier direct-CLI-to-hub model and 'SSH as permanent control plane' stance (confirms sections 6/11 still hold -- basis for 2xs.7/.8, us9.8, us9.14). member-onboarding-journey.md notes Journey B's LAN mDNS/broadcast proposal is superseded by hub-brokered enrollment (fnz.4 remains open/unimplemented); Journeys A and C's WAN framing still hold. Mechanical cross-referencing only, no design judgment. Full suite unaffected (129 files/1913 tests).

title: Hub-brokered file transfer (replaces SFTP)
description:
send_files/receive_files re-implemented over the hub relay instead of direct SFTP; storage/transport choice ties to the persistence ADR (H3) and depends on spoke mode (H6) existing. Lower priority than the command/prompt paths -- file transfer is used less often than execute_command/execute_prompt.

Model: Sonnet -- scoped implementation once the relay transport and storage decision exist.
close_reason: Fixed the gap the independent adversarial review found (commit 0ffadb9). tests/hub-service/file-transfer-e2e.test.ts now exists for real: two real spoke instances (runSpoke) against a real HTTP hub + real pg-mem, machine A sending a real multi-chunk file to a member hosted on B, B's spoke reassembling+verifying+writing it to a real test-scoped directory via the newly-wired createFileTransferReceiver in src/cli/spoke.ts's dispatch, and A receiving the correlated file_transfer.result over its own stream. A second test proves chunk tampering is caught (corrupt result, no write). Writing this test's sandboxedWriteFile default also caught a REAL latent bug: a Windows absolute path wasn't reliably rejected by the original resolve-based sandbox check, silently misinterpreted as a relative segment instead -- fixed with an explicit isAbsolute() check, covered by tests/spoke-sandbox.test.ts (5 tests including path-traversal and absolute-path rejection). RelayStrategy.transferFiles/receiveFiles remain an honest, separately-tracked not-yet-supported gap (the sending side via AgentStrategy) -- this fix covers the receiving side, now reachable and proven through the real spoke entrypoint. 13 new tests, 2125 total, tsc --noEmit clean.

comments.author: Azure Pipeline
comments.text: Reopened by independent adversarial review. File transfer is implemented + unit-tested but NOT proven end-to-end through the real hub. tests/file-transfer-relay.test.ts header (lines 4-6) cites tests/hub-service/file-transfer-e2e.test.ts as the real-HTTP/pg-mem round-trip proof, but that file does NOT exist (verified via ls + repo-wide grep). All file-transfer tests mock submitEnvelope (async () => ({ok:true,status:202})) and feed the receiver chunks directly; sender->hub->receiver is never exercised. The one real-hub e2e (relay-correlation-e2e.test.ts) covers only execute_command.result, not file_transfer.chunk/result. This is the exact mocked-transport blind spot that hid 3 wire bugs this session. Also: RelayStrategy.transferFiles/receiveFiles/deleteFiles all throw 'not yet supported', so it is unreachable via strategy dispatch. Needs a real file_transfer.chunk -> hub admission/fetchDeliverable -> receiver round-trip test, or an honest re-scope of the close reason.

Cost of not doing: cosmetic/misleading. Fold into a later package.json touch or Phase 7.

title: Auto-sprint viewer: Stop uses old-school browser confirm()/alert() dialogs, needs modern styling
description: Observed live: clicking 'Stop' in the workflow dashboard triggers two native browser dialogs in sequence -- a confirm() ('Are you sure you want to forcibly stop the workflow?') and then an alert() ('Stop signal sent.') once /stop resolves. These are unstyled OS-native dialogs that clash with the dashboard's otherwise modern dark-glass UI. The confirmation step itself should stay (don't remove the safety check before a forceful stop) -- just replace the native confirm()/alert() with an in-page modal/toast matching the existing --bg/--bg-glass/--accent/--danger CSS variables and visual language already used elsewhere in the dashboard (see the .btn-stop styling). Cosmetic, low priority. Reported by user observation; not yet investigated -- record only.
notes: Deferred by user decision (2026-07-13): purely cosmetic, not urgent. Leave open, pick up later.

title: Auto-sprint viewer: long activity titles wrap to multiple rows instead of truncating with ellipsis
description: Observed live during the apra-fleet-7pm auto-sprint run (dashboard at localhost:8090): activity/phase titles in the viewer's activity list can be long and wrap onto multiple rows, hurting scanability. Should be truncated with an ellipsis (CSS text-overflow or JS truncation) at the row level, with the full untruncated text still available on drill-down/expand/hover (e.g. title attribute or a detail panel) so no information is lost. Cosmetic, low priority. Reported by user observation; not yet investigated -- record only.
notes: Concrete example: the Plan Reviewer verdict entry (long JSON/notes string returned on CHANGES_NEEDED, e.g. the C1 R1 apra-fleet-7pm review) is a case where the activity title/body runs to multiple rows in the viewer. Same fix applies: truncate with ellipsis in the row, keep full text on drill-down.
close_reason: fixed: extended log-message truncation to long single-line JSON verdicts (all agent roles), commit 08105eb on feat/fleet-reorg

title: [follow-up] Drift detection: echo resolved model/tier on execute_prompt, verify against get_member_model_pricing (P4, not a prerequisite for dv5.5/.6)
description:
REVISED 2026-07-12: SUPERSEDED as the primary cost-accuracy mechanism. Per project-owner correction 3 on epic apra-fleet-dv5, real per-member cost tracking is now solved WITHOUT needing per-dispatch echo-back: tier->model resolution is deterministic and config-driven (src/tools/execute-prompt.ts resolveModelForTier(), each provider's modelTiers()/modelForTier()), so a new `get_member_model_pricing` MCP tool (apra-fleet-dv5.5) can answer "what does member X's premium tier cost" ahead of time, once per member per run, with no echo-back required. apra-fleet-dv5.6 wires that into apra-fleet-workflow's budget tracking as the primary pricing source, falling back to pricing.mjs's tier-band estimate only when unavailable.

This issue is NOT closed as invalid -- it retains narrower residual value: DRIFT DETECTION. A member's actual runtime resolution could theoretically diverge from its registered model_tiers config (e.g. a provider-side override, a manually-edited settings file the fleet server doesn't know about, a mid-run config change) in ways get_member_model_pricing's static/cached lookup would not catch. Echo-back would let the client verify "the model I was actually billed against matches the model I priced against" rather than trusting the static resolution stays true for the whole run. This is a correctness/verification enhancement on TOP of dv5.5/dv5.6's primary mechanism, not a prerequisite for it.

Scope (files, if/when taken up): src/tools/execute-prompt.ts (already computes resolvedModel/resolvedTier ~293-329 -- include both in the tool's result payload), packages/apra-fleet-client (surface them on the execute_prompt response), packages/apra-fleet-workflow/src/workflow/index.mjs (compare echoed resolvedModel against the pre-dispatch get_member_model_pricing-based price; log/flag a mismatch rather than silently trusting either source).

Priority lowered to P4: no longer blocking real per-member costing (that's apra-fleet-dv5.5/.6); this is now a drift-detection nice-to-have. Depends conceptually on apra-fleet-dv5.5/.6 landing first (there is no static price to compare an echo against otherwise).

Risks/unknowns: journal/replay compatibility for new activity fields; remote/relayed members (hub-spoke RelayStrategy) may not have the resolved model available at the same point; mixed fleets where some members echo and some do not must not double-count or fabricate.

Acceptance criteria (for the eventual implementer): a dispatch against a member with model_tiers configured shows the concrete resolved model in the workflow journal; if it disagrees with the price pre-computed via get_member_model_pricing, a warning is logged naming the discrepancy; absent an echo, behavior is unaffected (dv5.5/.6's static resolution is used as-is, unchanged).

comments.author: Azure Pipeline
comments.text: Superseded as the PRIMARY cost mechanism by apra-fleet-dv5.5/.6 (real per-member pricing via deterministic tier resolution, no echo-back needed). Kept open, re-scoped, priority lowered to P4: retains residual value as a drift-detection check on top of the new static-resolution mechanism, not as its prerequisite.

title: [DEFERRED] Relay/NAT-traversal command execution -- revisit once fleet-dashboard's bidirectional WebSocket relay ships
description:
Explicitly deferred, not abandoned: fleet-dashboard's docs/architecture.md documents its own bidirectional WebSocket relay (member <-> cloud <-> browser/dashboard-initiated command push) as 'DESIGNED, NOT YET BUILT.' apra-fleet-reorg's own envelope/SSE/Postgres relay system (built during the hub-spoke migration sprint, src/services/relay-executor.ts, relay-request.ts, hub-client.ts, spoke.ts) proved the wire-protocol semantics (at-least-once delivery, TTL, redelivery, workspace isolation) but per this epic's scoping decision is not the production transport -- SSH remains the primary/permanent execution transport, and NAT-traversal relay is a secondary transport for machines SSH cannot reach, not a priority for the current bootstrap/sync-focused phase.

Revisit once: (a) fleet-dashboard ships its bidirectional WebSocket half, and (b) there is a concrete customer need for command execution against a machine SSH cannot reach. At that point, decide whether apra-fleet.exe's relay client speaks fleet-dashboard's WebSocket protocol (likely) or something else, using apra-fleet-reorg's already-built envelope semantics as the reference design.
acceptance_criteria: No action expected until explicitly reprioritized. This issue exists so the deferred decision and its rationale are not lost.

title: Enforce hub-jwt role value-domain against contract's RoleSchema enum
description: Independent review (2026-07-05) found hub-jwt.ts's HubJwtClaims.role field names now match packages/fleet-api-contract/src/schemas/jwt.ts's JWTClaimsSchema field-for-field (iss/ws/sub/exp/role, fixed 2026-07-05), but the VALUE domain is not enforced: the contract's RoleSchema is z.enum(['member','admin','superadmin']), while hub-jwt.ts's sign()/verify() accept/return a bare string. Machine tokens are minted with role:'spoke' (tests/hub-service/http-server.test.ts and others), which is outside the contract's enum -- meaning either the contract's RoleSchema needs a machine/spoke role value added, or hub-jwt.ts needs its own machine-vs-member role distinction reconciled with the contract. This is a real, if minor, spec/implementation mismatch worth a deliberate decision rather than silent drift.
acceptance_criteria: Either: (a) RoleSchema in packages/fleet-api-contract is extended to include whatever machine-token role values are actually minted (e.g. 'spoke'), with hub-jwt.ts validating against it; or (b) hub-jwt.ts documents and enforces that machine-level tokens use a distinct claims shape/field not subject to RoleSchema, with a test proving the boundary. Do not just silently loosen the contract's enum without deciding which is correct.
close_reason: Resolved per acceptance option (b): documented in hub-jwt.ts that HubJwtClaims.role has three distinct value vocabularies depending on token family (dashboard workspace-selection: contract's RoleSchema member/admin/superadmin; member/agent tokens: 'doer'; machine/spoke tokens: 'spoke') -- only the first is contract-bound, and unifying them would be a category error, not a fix. Added RoleSchema.safeParse defense-in-depth at the one call site that mints contract-bound tokens (POST /workspaces/:id/select, commit 341d31a). A new test proves the practical guarantee already holds independently at the DB layer: the users table's own CHECK constraint rejects an out-of-enum role via direct SQL. 1 new test, 2133 total, tsc clean.

title: Harden sandboxedWriteFile against pre-planted symlink escape
description: Independent review (2026-07-05) noted src/cli/spoke.ts's sandboxedWriteFile does a purely lexical path-containment check (no realpath/symlink resolution). A pre-existing symlink planted inside FLEET_DIR/received-files (out-of-band, not plantable via this write API itself) would be followed, allowing a write to escape the sandbox. Reviewer assessed this as a hardening opportunity, not a live exploit (this API cannot itself create the symlink), but worth closing given destPath is fully sender-controlled untrusted input.
acceptance_criteria: sandboxedWriteFile resolves the real path (fs.realpathSync or equivalent) of the containing directory after mkdir and re-validates containment before the write, OR uses an equivalent symlink-safe write primitive. A test proves a pre-planted symlink inside received-files pointing outside the sandbox is rejected, not followed.
close_reason: Implemented (commit 341d31a): sandboxedWriteFile re-validates against the real (fs.realpathSync-resolved) path of the containing directory after mkdir, and separately refuses if the destination file itself is already a symlink. Two new tests in tests/spoke-sandbox.test.ts actually create real symlinks (a directory symlink and a file symlink) pointing outside the sandbox and prove both are rejected with a clear error, not silently followed -- verified they actually ran (not EPERM-skipped) on this Windows environment. 2 new tests, 2133 total, tsc clean.
